alignments.rationale: The publication provides specific performance numbers for the developed neural network, making the preprint's general statement more specific (clarified) while preserving the same substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Significant discriminative improvements over basic demographic predictors were observed for 1,774 (94.3%) conditions when integrated into Cox Proportional Hazard Models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed neural network showed discriminative improvements over basic demographic predictors for 1774 (94.3%) endpoints.
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the same quantitative result (1,774/94.3% discriminative improvements); differences in model integration detail do not change the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The risk models showed generalizability across healthcare systems, replicating discriminative improvements for 1,347 (89.8%) of 1,500 investigated endpoints in the All of US cohort.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The unmodified risk models replicated improvements for 1347 (89.8%) of 1500 investigated endpoints in the All of US cohort.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the replication result with the same numbers and cohort; wording differences are minimal and do not alter meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates the potential of medical history to rapidly respond to health threats by estimating risk for thousands of diseases at minimal cost.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates the potential of medical history to support guidance for emerging pandemics at minimal cost.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the general preprint claim about rapid responses to health threats into a more specific claim about supporting guidance for emerging pandemics, narrowing the stated application.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Strong flow and RBC shape impairment are found in COVID-19 samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Strong flow and RBC shape impairment are found in COVID-19 samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result verbatim; no substantive change is present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Changes in RBC flow properties are reversible upon suspension of COVID-19 RBCs in healthy plasma.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Changes in RBC flow and shape are reversible upon suspension of COVID-19 RBCs in healthy plasma.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same reversible effect, adding minor wording but not changing meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Healthy RBCs immediately resemble COVID-19 RBCs when suspended in COVID-19 plasma.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Healthy RBCs resemble COVID-19 RBCs when suspended in COVID-19 plasma.
alignments.rationale: The claim is effectively identical between preprint and publication (minor tense/wording variation only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Proteomics and metabolomics analyses reveal the effect of plasma exchanges on both plasma and RBCs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Proteomics and metabolomics analyses reveal the effect of plasma exchanges on both plasma and RBCs.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RBCs play a new role in maintaining plasma equilibria at the expense of their flow properties.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RBCs play a new role in maintaining plasma equilibria at the expense of their flow properties.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach, based only on the Spike protein sequence, allows us to efficiently identify the variants of concern (VoCs) and of interest (VoIs), as well as other emerging variants occurring during the diffusion of the virus.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our novel algorithm allowed us to define persistent variants as chains that remain stable over time.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on defining persistent variants and stability over time; this clarifies the methodological framing of the preprint's Spike-sequence identification claim, but the mapping is somewhat indirect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our analysis provides an early warning for the emergence of new persistent variants once its associated dominant Spike sequence reaches 1% of the time-binned sequence data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis and the relevant tools introduced in our work serve as an early warning for the emergence of new persistent variants once the associated cluster reaches 1% of the time-binned sequence data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the early-warning threshold and role of the tools exactly, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Comparison with the epidemiological data demonstrates that each new wave is dominated by a new emerging variant, thus confirming the hypothesis that there is a strong correlation between the emergence of variants and the multi-wave temporal pattern depicting the viral spread.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrated that each new wave is dominated by a new emerging variant, thus confirming the hypothesis of the existence of a strong correlation between the birth of variants and the pandemic multi-wave temporal pattern.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same claim linking emergent variants to successive epidemiological waves with equivalent strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our ML algorithm works as an efficient early warning system for the emergence of new persistent variants that may pose a threat of triggering a new wave of COVID-19.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis and the relevant tools introduced in our work serve as an early warning for the emergence of new persistent variants once the associated cluster reaches 1% of the time-binned sequence data.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the algorithm/tool functions as an early warning system; the publication specifies the numeric threshold but preserves the preprint's conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The same methodology can be applied to other viral diseases, influenza included, if sufficient sequencing data is available.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include or reiterate the claim about generalizing the method to other viruses; that preprint claim is not present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learning missingness patterns in electronic health record data decreases early warning system performance if the missingness pattern changes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tree-based methods that learn missingness patterns in electronic health record data lead to decreased performance in new settings with different missingness patterns.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about reduced performance when missingness patterns differ; wording is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Randomized imputation and Bayesian regression imputation methods prevent tree-based methods from learning missingness patterns.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Randomized and Bayesian regression imputation methods mitigate the issue of learning the missingness pattern for tree-based methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint claim that these imputation strategies mitigate learning missingness patterns, preserving meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PICTURE, an early warning system for patient deterioration, has performance comparable to currently used systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PICTURE is comparable to state-of-the-art machine learning systems based on a rudimentary comparison with published scores.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames comparability more cautiously (noting a rudimentary comparison) and also supplies separate numeric performance claims elsewhere, which tempers the original general claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PICTURE can explain predictions via feature ranking.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or emphasize an explainability claim about feature ranking, so the preprint's claim is not present in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The variational autoencoder's code and weights have been released to facilitate robust comparisons and development of early warning systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The code and weights of the variational autoencoder have been released to facilitate robust comparisons and development of early warning systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms the release of the VAE code and weights, matching the preprint claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction model achieved AUCs of 0.723 for severe infections and 0.814 for fatal infections among infected individuals.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prediction model achieved an AUC-ROC of 0.723 for severe infections and 0.814 for fatal infections among infected individuals.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same numeric performance metrics, preserving the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The top five contributing factors for severity were age, number of drugs taken, cystatin C, waist-hip ratio, and Townsend Deprivation index.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The top contributing factors for severity of COVID-19 infections were age, number of drugs taken, cystatin C, waist-to-hip ratio, and Townsend deprivation index.
alignments.rationale: The publication lists the same top contributing risk factors with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction models may be useful at a population level to help identify those susceptible to developing severe or fatal infections.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prediction models can identify individuals at risk of developing severe or fatal COVID-19 infections, which may facilitate targeted prevention strategies.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts the emphasis from population-level utility to identification of at-risk individuals and actionable prevention strategies, narrowing the stated application while preserving intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further replications in independent cohorts are required to verify our findings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further replications in independent cohorts are required to verify the findings of this study.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the replication limitation but lowers the stated certainty from high to medium, indicating a slight tempering of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieved 98.72% accuracy for two-class classification of COVID-19 and healthy individuals.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method reached 98.72% accuracy for two-class classification (COVID-19, No-findings).
alignments.rationale: Numbers and class definition match directly (healthy ~ No-findings); wording difference is minor.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method also reached 92% accuracy for three-class classification of COVID-19, healthy, and pneumonia.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieved 92% accuracy for multiclass classification (COVID-19, No-findings, and Pneumonia).
alignments.rationale: Same result and class set; only minor wording change (No-findings vs healthy).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Chest X-ray images provide superior results compared to RT-PCR for COVID-19 identification.
alignments.rationale: No corresponding claim about superiority to RT-PCR appears in the publication; the assertion about RT-PCR is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a pre-trained network, DenseNet169, and feature selection method (ANOVA) improved predictive accuracy while reducing computations and time complexity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results show that the proposed framework outperforms other methods and can be helpful for radiologists in the diagnosis of COVID-19 cases.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a more general claim that the framework outperforms others and is clinically useful but omits the specific method details and explicit computational/time complexity claims, reducing specificity.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method's precision, recall, and specificity rates on two-class classification were 99.21%, 93.33%, and 100%, respectively.
alignments.rationale: These detailed performance metrics appear in the publication but are not present in the listed preprint claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieved 94.07% precision, 88.46% recall, and 100% specificity for multiclass classification.
alignments.rationale: Additional per-class performance metrics are reported in the publication but were not in the preprint claim list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study quantifies spleen iron in 41,764 participants of the UK Biobank and provides the first reference range for spleen iron in an unselected population.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study quantifies spleen iron in 41,764 participants of the UK Biobank and provides a reference range for spleen iron in an unselected population.
alignments.rationale: Text and meaning are effectively identical; only minor wording variation ("first" omitted).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Associations between spleen iron and regulatory variation at two hereditary spherocytosis genes, ANK1 and SPTA1, were identified through genome-wide association study.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Associations between spleen iron and regulatory variation at two hereditary spherocytosis genes, ANK1 and SPTA1, were identified through genome-wide association study.
alignments.rationale: Claim is identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spherocytosis-causing coding mutations in ANK1 and SPTA1 are associated with lower reticulocyte volume and increased reticulocyte percentage.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Spherocytosis-causing coding mutations in ANK1 and SPTA1 are associated with lower reticulocyte volume and increased reticulocyte percentage.
alignments.rationale: Direct match in content and specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Common alleles associated with ANK1 and SPTA1 are linked to increased expression in blood and larger reticulocyte volume, as well as reduced reticulocyte percentage.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Common alleles of ANK1 and SPTA1 are associated with increased expression in blood and with larger reticulocyte volume and reduced reticulocyte percentage.
alignments.rationale: Same associations reported with semantically equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of deep learning and efficient image processing enables non-invasive measurement of spleen iron.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of deep learning and efficient image processing enables non-invasive measurement of spleen iron.
alignments.rationale: Contribution statement is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models were trained to classify TUR-P whole slide images into prostate adenocarcinoma and benign lesions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning models were trained to classify TUR-P whole slide images into prostate adenocarcinoma and benign lesions.
alignments.rationale: The contribution (model training objective) is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models achieved an ROC-AUC of up to 0.984 in TUR-P test sets for adenocarcinoma.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models achieved an ROC-AUC of up to 0.984 in TUR-P test sets for adenocarcinoma.
alignments.rationale: Result numbers and claim are identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate the high promising potential of deploying these models in a practical TUR-P histopathological diagnostic workflow system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results demonstrate the promising potential of deploying these models in a practical TUR-P histopathological diagnostic workflow system.
alignments.rationale: Publication softens phrasing slightly (drops "high") but the overall conclusion about deployment potential is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimal parameters for IED detection correlated with the clinical duration of an IED and achieved the best AUC, AUPRC, and F1 scores of 0.98, 0.80, and 0.77 on the private datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimal parameters for IED detection correlated with the clinical duration of an IED.
alignments.rationale: Publication emphasizes the correlation as a standalone result and reports metrics separately; core correlation claim is preserved though metric reporting is reorganized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AUC, AUPRC, and F1 scores on the Temple University Events (TUEV) dataset were 0.99, 0.99, and 0.97, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AUPRC and F1 on the TUEV dataset were 0.99 and 0.97, respectively.
alignments.rationale: Performance on the TUEV dataset is reported consistently; publication omits explicit AUC but the AUPRC/F1 match the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Algorithms trained on the private datasets maintained performance when tested on the TUEV data, but those trained on TUEV could not generalise well to the private data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Algorithms trained on the private sets maintained their performance when tested on the TUEV data, but those trained on TUEV could not generalize well to the private data.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion about asymmetric generalization across datasets is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The differences in class distributions across datasets indicate a need for public datasets with better diversity of IED waveforms, background activities, and artifacts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a need for public datasets with a better diversity of IED waveforms, background activities, and artifacts to facilitate standardization and benchmarking of algorithms.
alignments.rationale: Same recommendation about dataset diversity and need for standardization; wording is slightly expanded in the publication for clarity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher-altitude regions of Ethiopia exhibit significantly lower risk exposure rates and a reduced disease burden.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Higher-altitude regions of Ethiopia are associated with significantly lower risk exposure rates and a reduced disease burden.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated with equivalent meaning in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biological aging, as assessed through facial photographs, occurs at a faster rate with increasing elevation due to greater UV exposure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Biological aging, as assessed by facial photographs, shows a faster rate of aging with increasing elevation due to greater UV exposure.
alignments.rationale: Same observation and causal attribution (UV exposure) preserved verbatim in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a significant decrease in DNA damage-induced senescence in both monocytes and lymphocytes with increasing elevation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DNA damage-induced senescence decreases in both monocytes and lymphocytes with increasing elevation.
alignments.rationale: Identical biological result reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings support the idea that oxidative stress may drive aging, as disease and DNA damage-induced senescence decreases with altitude.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings support the idea that oxidative stress may drive aging.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about oxidative stress driving aging is maintained though the publication statement is slightly more concise.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Life expectancy is increased by lower mortality rates in higher-altitude regions of Ethiopia.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit life-expectancy result (lower mortality/increased life expectancy) not stated in the preprint claim list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deaths due to COVID-19 were associated with immunosuppression due to disease, type-2 diabetes, chronic respiratory disease, age, and male sex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Demographic and health records are associated with outcomes of severe COVID-19, specifically death and ICU admission. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same association but in broader terms (demographic and health records) rather than the preprint's specific list of factors, so the claim is clarified and somewhat narrowed in scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Factors are differentially associated with death and ICU admission among patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Obesity and chronic respiratory diseases have very different associations with death and ICU admission. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives specific examples (obesity, chronic respiratory disease) illustrating the preprint's general statement that factors differ by outcome, narrowing and clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gradient-boosted decision trees (GBDTs) had similar performance to logistic regression in predicting ICU admission and death. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not report or compare GBDT vs logistic regression performance, so this specific methodological comparison was omitted from the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Self-reported ethnicities were strongly but variably associated with both outcomes of death and ICU admission. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Shapley explanation of GBDTs identified varying effects of some factors among patients, highlighting the importance of individual patient assessment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the variability as patient-level heterogeneous effects revealed by Shapley explanations; this clarifies the preprint's observation but is less specific about ethnicity, narrowing the scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clinical support systems should not only predict patients at risk but also yield interpretable outputs for validation by domain experts. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study's results are relevant for evaluating complex automated clinical decision systems, which should optimize prediction scores while remaining interpretable to clinicians. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same recommendation about interpretability of clinical decision systems but reports the conclusion with somewhat lower certainty, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Discussion about FGM/C has not evolved significantly over time. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Discussion about FGM/C has not evolved significantly over time. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion with the same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The majority of the conversation about FGM/C on English-speaking Twitter is advocating for an end to the practice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The majority of the conversation about FGM/C on English-speaking Twitter is advocating for an end to the practice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical result claim and certainty in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Supporters of Donald Trump make up a substantial voice in the conversation about FGM/C. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Supporters of Donald Trump make up a substantial voice in the conversation about FGM/C. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Directly matched; the claim and certainty remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Understanding the nuances in how people across cultures refer to and discuss FGM/C could be important for the design of public health communication and intervention. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Understanding the nuances in how people across cultures refer to and discuss FGM/C could be important for the design of public health communication and intervention. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion and scope but reports it with lower certainty, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed early alert system can detect the onset of seizure events at high accuracy, often before being noticed by caregivers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed machine learning framework accurately captures seizure onset moments and was replicated in an independent patient cohort. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms accurate detection of seizure onset and adds replication in an independent cohort, clarifying and strengthening the original detection claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The high-dimensional personal physiome and activity profiles displayed a clustering pattern anchored by patient age groups. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The unique dataset collected from 99 children diagnosed with epilepsy allowed for the quantification of physiological dynamics and identification of irregularities upon epilepsy onset. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach could detect subtle seizures not recognized by humans and could detect seizures prior to clinical onset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach can detect subtle seizures not recognized by humans and can identify seizures prior to clinical onset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about detecting subtle and preclinical seizures with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The feasibility of a real-time mobile infrastructure in a clinical setting has been demonstrated, which is valuable in caring for epileptic patients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation of a cloud computing infrastructure integrating wearable sensors and machine learning improves early detection of seizure onsets in children. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication describes a specific cloud/wearable/ML implementation demonstrating improved early detection, which clarifies and operationalizes the preprint's feasibility claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extension of the developed system has the potential to be leveraged as a health management device or longitudinal phenotyping tool in clinical cohort studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The real-time mobile infrastructure demonstrated in this study has the potential to be valuable in caring for epileptic patients and could serve as a health management device. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication directly echoes the preprint's forward-looking claim about potential use as a health management device with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The original NILS model was externally validated in NKBC with an AUC of 0.699, indicating good calibration and the potential to spare 16% of patients with node-negative disease from SLNB. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The original NILS model was externally validated in NKBC with an AUC of 0.699, indicating good calibration and the potential to spare 16% of patients with node-negative disease from SLNB. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical validation result and interpretation in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The LVI model was externally validated with an AUC of 0.747, but it did not improve the discriminatory performance of the N models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The LVI model was externally validated with an AUC of 0.747, but it did not improve the discriminatory performance of the nodal status models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same validation and conclusion about lack of improvement in discriminatory performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new N model developed in NKBC without information on LVI achieved an AUC of 0.709 and demonstrated the potential to omit 24% of patients from unnecessary SLNB. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new nodal status model developed in NKBC without information on LVI achieved an AUC of 0.709 and demonstrated the potential to omit 24% of patients from unnecessary SLNBs. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The imputation of LVI status did not improve the discriminatory performance of the NILS model. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about LVI imputation is unchanged and retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hybrid forecasting model named Theta-ARNN (TARNN) can generate real-time out-of-sample forecasts of COVID-19 outbreaks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hybrid forecasting model developed in this paper, named Theta-ARNN, can generate real-time out-of-sample forecasts of COVID-19 outbreaks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The newly introduced hybrid forecasting model has an acceptable prediction error rate. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The newly introduced hybrid forecasting model has an acceptable prediction error rate, aiding healthcare and government in effective planning and resource allocation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method outperforms traditional univariate and hybrid forecasting models for the test data sets on average. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Theta-ARNN model outperforms traditional univariate and hybrid forecasting models for the test datasets on average. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Directly matched result with the same performance claim and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: COVID-19 patients administered unfractionated heparin have higher rates of mortality compared to those administered enoxaparin.
alignments.pub_claim_text: COVID-19 patients administered unfractionated Heparin have higher rates of 28-day mortality compared to those administered Enoxaparin.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients administered unfractionated heparin experience higher rates of thrombotic events than those administered enoxaparin.
alignments.rationale: There is no corresponding claim about thrombotic event rates in the publication, so this preprint result appears omitted in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The differences in clinical outcomes between enoxaparin and unfractionated heparin remain statistically significant after controlling for potential confounding factors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The risk ratio for 28-day mortality in patients receiving unfractionated Heparin is 4.3 with a 95% Confidence Interval of [1.8, 10.2].
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a quantified risk estimate but does not explicitly assert that the differences remain significant after controlling for confounders, thus the original claim about adjusted significance is toned down/revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: COVID-19 patients administered unfractionated heparin have higher rates of acute kidney injury compared to those administered enoxaparin.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the need for further investigation into the differential effects of Enoxaparin and unfractionated Heparin on COVID-associated coagulation cascades.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hypertension was the most significant risk factor associated with 10 different complications of COVID-19 infection.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pleural effusion was the most frequent complication of early COVID-19 infection, occurring in 23% of complications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Novel associations between cancer and early-onset heart failure (risk ratio: 3, p=0.02) and immunosuppression and early-onset heart failure (risk ratio: 4.3, p=0.04) have been identified.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report these specific novel association statistics, so this preprint result appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Onset of new complications after 30 days is rare, with pleural effusion being the most common complication during this period.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The onset of new complications after 30 days is rare, with pleural effusion being the most common complication during this period.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this result with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The associations between pre-COVID conditions and COVID-associated complications may form the basis for the development of risk assessment scores to guide clinical care pathways.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The associations between pre-COVID conditions and COVID-associated complications may form the basis for the development of risk assessment scores to guide clinical care pathways.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this conclusion with similar phrasing and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The complexities in modeling COVID-19 transmission dynamics can be circumvented by using scaling principles that lead to universality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The complexities in modeling COVID-19 transmission dynamics can be circumvented by using scaling principles that lead to universality.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Blue Sky model explains one class of transmission dynamics through a solution at the edge of a blue sky bifurcation.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same model contribution with equivalent framing and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The data collapse achieved in this study enhances predictability in disease spread across various geographical scales.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps this empirical/result claim intact and unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodology developed can potentially be applied to the transmission of other infectious diseases.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the potential cross-disease applicability but omits the additional speculative statement about revealing new universality classes, narrowing and tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictability in transmission dynamics and the simplicity of the methodology can assist in formulating policies for exit strategies and mitigation methods during a pandemic.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictability in transmission dynamics can assist in formulating policies for exit strategies and mitigation methods during a pandemic.

alignments.pub_claim_text: ECG intervals and amplitudes were similar between the DeepFake and real ECGs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The synthetic dataset will be available as open access for researchers at OSF.io.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The synthetic dataset will be available as open access for researchers at OSF.io.

alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on the successful generation of realistic ECGs from two population studies and omits the explicit claim that this addresses privacy issues, narrowing the stated contribution.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Allspice R package was developed to predict ALL subtypes and driver genes from unadjusted mRNA-seq read counts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication specifies the implementation/form (R package) of the Allspice tool mentioned in the preprint, a clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Allspice includes quantitative classification and safety metrics to help determine the most plausible genetic drivers for inconclusive cases. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state this specific claim about quantitative classification and safety metrics; the particular contribution appears omitted in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-task deep autoencoders outperform state-of-the-art machine learning approaches for predicting AD progression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed multi-task deep autoencoders outperform state-of-the-art machine learning approaches for predicting Alzheimer's disease progression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-confidence result about outperforming state-of-the-art methods for AD progression with consistent wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed multi-task deep autoencoders can predict AD progression accurately using only historical data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multi-task deep autoencoders can predict Alzheimer's disease progression accurately using only historical DNA methylation data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the modality (DNA methylation) underlying the 'historical data', narrowing and clarifying the original claim while keeping the same confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the multi-task deep autoencoders is further improved by including all temporal data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the multi-task deep autoencoders is further improved by including all temporal DNA methylation data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines 'temporal data' to temporal DNA methylation data (narrowing/clarifying) and expresses slightly lower certainty (high -> medium).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LASSO-regularized logistic regression performed on the whole development dataset yielded a 23- and 11-variable model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Five different multivariable prediction models of serological response after the third and fourth vaccine dose against SARS-CoV-2 were developed and validated. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a different set/number of models (five models) and does not repeat the specific 23/11-variable claim, indicating the original model description was revised in the published report.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: External validation showed AUC-ROC of 0.855, 0.749, 0.828, and 0.787 for the sparser 11-variable model, yielding an overall performance of 0.819. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: External validation of the 10-variable LASSO-regularized LR model showed an AUC-ROC of 0.783, indicating good overall accuracy in predicting vaccination response in kidney transplant recipients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives different validation results and a different model (10-variable vs 11-variable and different AUCs), so the numeric/model details were revised though the general conclusion of good accuracy remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An 11-variable LASSO-regularized logistic regression model predicts vaccination response in kidney transplant recipients with good overall accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: External validation of the 10-variable LASSO-regularized LR model showed an AUC-ROC of 0.783, indicating good overall accuracy in predicting vaccination response in kidney transplant recipients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published model retains the conclusion of good predictive accuracy but reports a different (10-variable) model and validation metric, so the claim was revised in detail but not in overall intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed model can guide decisions when choosing between different immunization strategies to improve protection against COVID-19 in kidney transplant recipients. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed prediction model can guide decisions on modulating immunosuppressive therapy or performing passive immunization in previously seronegative, COVID-19-naïve kidney transplant recipients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same practical conclusion but specifies actionable decisions and a target subgroup (previously seronegative, COVID-19-naïve), narrowing and clarifying the original recommendation.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Repeated vaccination against SARS-CoV-2 increases serological response in kidney transplant recipients with high interindividual variability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result about the effect of repeated vaccination is present in the publication but was not stated among the preprint claims, so it is an added published claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The metapopulation susceptible–exposed–infectious–recovered (SEIR) model parameterised with human movement data from 340 cities in China replicates the early case trajectory in the COVID-19 pandemic. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The metapopulation susceptible-exposed-infectious-recovered model replicates the early-case trajectory in the COVID-19 pandemic. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-confidence result about the SEIR model replicating the early pandemic trajectory with consistent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Travel time is the most important network property that predicts the spread of SARS-CoV-2 between cities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Travel time is the most important network property predicting the spread of SARS-CoV-2 between cities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same high-confidence claim about travel time being the dominant network predictor of intercity spread.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High connectivity between cities reduces the impact of travel time on transmission speed after local influences are accounted for. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: High connectivity between cities reduces the impact of travel time on transmission speed. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same medium-confidence conclusion about connectivity moderating travel-time effects with equivalent scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Only significantly reduced movement substantially impacts infection spread times throughout the network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Only significantly reduced movement substantially impacts infection spread times throughout the network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This high-confidence result about the scale of movement reduction needed to affect spread is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified 29 features that were highly predictive of functional movement disorder (FMD) condition, classifying patients from controls with 80% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified 29 features of interest that were highly predictive of the FMD condition, classifying patients and controls with 80% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim repeats the same result (29 features, 80% classification) with equivalent confidence and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The features selected by the model highlight the importance of the interconnected relationship between areas associated with emotion, reward, and sensorimotor integration. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The features selected by the model highlight the importance of the interconnected relationship between areas associated with emotion, reward, and sensorimotor integration. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this medium-confidence conclusion identically, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Exploratory machine learning was able to identify a distinctive, abnormal pattern in FMD patients. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Exploratory machine learning was able to identify this distinctive abnormal pattern, suggesting that alterations in functional linkages between these regions may be a consistent feature of the condition in many FMD patients. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the finding more cautiously (adds a suggestive interpretation and lower certainty), so the claim is clarified and expressed with reduced confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Alterations in functional linkages between regions associated with motor function, attention, and executive function may be a consistent feature of the condition in many FMD patients. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Exploratory machine learning was able to identify this distinctive abnormal pattern, suggesting that alterations in functional linkages between these regions may be a consistent feature of the condition in many FMD patients. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates this low-confidence suggestion about consistent alterations, merging it with the exploratory ML finding but retaining the same content and uncertainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our research presents novel results that further elucidate the pathophysiology of functional movement disorder (FMD) with a machine learning model that classifies FMD and healthy controls correctly 80% of the time. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an explicit contribution statement framing novelty and impact that was not present among the preprint claims, so this is an added published claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different tumour sites present distinct response patterns to neoadjuvant chemotherapy in high grade serous ovarian cancer patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or reiterate a claim about distinct response patterns by tumour site; that specific preprint claim is absent in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Volumetric response assessment is a better predictor of overall survival than RECIST 1.1 status in high grade serous ovarian cancer patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make the specific comparative claim about volumetric response versus RECIST 1.1 for overall survival, so this preprint claim does not appear in the published paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An ensemble machine learning approach can predict tumour volume response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy from data obtained prior to treatment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integrated radiomics model reduces the prediction error by 8% compared to the clinical model in predicting the response of HGSOC patients to neoadjuvant chemotherapy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a quantified improvement in prediction error for an integrated radiomics model predicting response, which refines and quantifies the preprint's predictive-model claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights the importance of comprehensive patient characterisation in predicting treatment response. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Including radiomics data in integrative models of treatment response is valuable for predicting outcomes in HGSOC patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication more explicitly identifies radiomics as a valuable component of integrative patient characterization, clarifying the preprint's general point about comprehensive characterisation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed radiogenomic framework sets the foundation for developing new clinical trials of neoadjuvant chemotherapy in high grade serous ovarian cancer. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework presented can aid in developing new biomarker-based clinical trials of neoadjuvant chemotherapy in HGSOC. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the contribution claim but specifies 'biomarker-based' trials, narrowing and clarifying the intended application of the framework.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The ensemble machine learning model achieves an AUC of 0.78 for RECIST 1.1 classification in the external cohort. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific published metric (AUC 0.78 for RECIST classification) is not present among the preprint claims, making this an added, reported result in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 10 percent of the population at highest risk accounts for 15-30 percent of infant mortality in sub-Saharan Africa, depending on the country.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 10 percent of the population at highest risk accounts for 15-30 percent of infant mortality in sub-Saharan Africa, depending on the country.
alignments.rationale: The wording and numeric range are identical between preprint and publication; the claim content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Successful targeting in machine learning risk models relies on several variables other than wealth.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Successful targeting in the risk models relies on several variables other than wealth.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same dependency on variables other than wealth with slightly different wording and reduced certainty, so the claim is clarified and expressed with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models that employ only wealth data perform little or no better than chance in predicting high-risk births.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models that employ only wealth data perform little or no better than chance in predicting high-risk births.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried forward verbatim and retains the same meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Employing flexible data and models to predict high-risk births could substantially improve the targeting of life-saving interventions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Employing flexible machine learning risk models can substantially improve the targeting of interventions to save lives.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the phrasing to machine learning risk models and states the conclusion with higher certainty, so the claim is clarified and presented with amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Covid-19 mortality rate of males was higher than that of females by 16.1%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The COVID-19 mortality rate of males was 29.9% higher than that of females.
alignments.rationale: The claim about higher male mortality is preserved but the estimated effect size was updated substantially in the publication, so the claim is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Males with Covid-19 had a 25.4% higher rate of receiving a thrombotic diagnosis compared to females.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Males had a 35.8% higher rate of receiving a thrombotic diagnosis compared with females.
alignments.rationale: The directional claim is the same but the magnitude changed between preprint and publication, so the result is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mortality rate of all patients with a thrombotic diagnosis was 42.4%, which is over twice that of Covid-19 patients without a thrombotic diagnosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mortality rate of all patients with a thrombotic diagnosis was over twice that of patients with COVID-19 without a thrombotic diagnosis.
alignments.rationale: Both versions report that mortality with a thrombotic diagnosis is over twice that of those without; the publication omits the specific 42.4% number but retains the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Over half of the excess mortality in male patients could be explained by thrombophilia.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 16.4% of the excess mortality in male patients could be explained by increased thrombotic risk.
alignments.rationale: The explanatory fraction was substantially reduced in the publication from 'over half' to 16.4%, representing a clear revision and a tempered interpretation of the contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The higher Covid-19 mortality rate in males may be significantly accounted for by the increased propensity for thrombophilia among males.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The higher COVID-19 mortality rate in males may be significantly accounted for by the elevated risk of thrombosis among males.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion linking elevated thrombotic risk to higher male mortality with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The unsupervised, self-administered ASRT task is sensitive to cognitive impairments in MCI/mild AD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The unsupervised, self-administered ASRT task is sensitive to cognitive impairments in MCI and mild AD.
alignments.rationale: The sensitivity claim is preserved verbatim (minor punctuation/formatting differences only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ASRT task shows good usability, high parallel forms reliability, and convergent validity with established cognitive tests.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ASRT task showed good usability, high parallel-forms reliability, and high convergent validity with established cognitive tests.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the usability, reliability, and convergent validity claims with similar language and slightly stronger wording for convergent validity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Remote, low cost, low burden, and automatically scored speech assessments could be used to support diagnostic screening, healthcare, and treatment monitoring.
alignments.rationale: This broader implementation-oriented claim about potential uses of remote speech assessments does not appear in the published claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adherence to daily assessment was moderate and did not decline over time, with higher adherence in cognitively unimpaired participants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adherence to daily assessment was moderate and did not decline over time, with higher adherence in cognitively unimpaired participants.
alignments.rationale: The adherence result is repeated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Task performance improved modestly across the week and was better for immediate recall.
alignments.rationale: This performance-over-time result appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Affective volatility was raised in patients with Bipolar Disorder (BD) whereas affective noise was raised in patients with Borderline Personality Disorder (BPD).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Affective volatility was raised in patients with bipolar disorder (BD), whereas affective noise was raised in patients with borderline personality disorder (BPD).
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried into the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lithium increased the volatility of positive affect in both studies rather than suppressing affective variability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lithium increased the volatility of positive affect in both studies rather than suppressing affective variability.
alignments.rationale: The finding about lithium's effect is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides a quantitative measure of the affective variability associated with BD and BPD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides a quantitative measure of the affective variability associated with bipolar disorder and borderline personality disorder.
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is preserved with only minor wording standardization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest a novel mechanism of action for lithium, whereby periods of persistently low or high affect are avoided by increasing the volatility of affective responses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings suggest a mechanism of action for lithium, whereby periods of persistently low or high affect are avoided by increasing the volatility of affective responses.
alignments.rationale: The publication removes the adjective 'novel' and slightly tones the language, clarifying the mechanistic suggestion without changing its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning methods can produce meaningful 'nowcasts' of disease incidence ahead of healthcare-based estimates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning methods can produce meaningful 'nowcasts' of disease incidence ahead of healthcare-based estimates.
alignments.rationale: The claim is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed computational methods can retrospectively estimate disease activity in recent outbreaks weeks ahead of traditional surveillance methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed computational methods can retrospectively estimate disease activity in emerging outbreaks weeks ahead of traditional surveillance methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same claim with only a minor wording change ('recent' -> 'emerging').

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods frequently provide useful real-time incidence estimates that help fill temporal data gaps from surveillance reporting delays.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods frequently provide useful real-time incidence estimates that help fill temporal data gaps from surveillance reporting delays.
alignments.rationale: The claim about filling temporal data gaps is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods are limited by issues of sample bias and skew in search query volumes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods are limited by issues of sample bias and skew in search query volumes.
alignments.rationale: The limitation is carried forward unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with a severe course of COVID-19 had significantly increased IL-6, CRP and leukocyte counts and significantly decreased lymphocyte counts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients with a severe course of COVID-19 had significantly increased interleukin (IL)-6, C-reactive protein (CRP), and leukocyte counts and significantly decreased lymphocyte counts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording is effectively identical (minor expansion of IL-6 acronym) and the measured result and certainty are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The radiological severity grading was significantly increased in ICU patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The radiological severity grading was significantly increased in ICU patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim text and implication are the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The need for ICU treatment is independently associated with affected lung volume, radiological severity score, CRP and IL-6. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The need for ICU treatment is independently associated with affected lung volume, radiological severity score, CRP, and IL-6. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same variables and independence conclusion are reported with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multivariate random forest modelling showed a mean sensitivity, specificity and accuracy of 0.72 ± 0.1, 0.86 ± 0.16 and 0.80 ± 0.1 respectively. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multivariate random forest modelling showed a mean sensitivity, specificity, and accuracy of 0.72 ± 0.1, 0.86 ± 0.16, and 0.80 ± 0.1 respectively. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric performance metrics and phrasing are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Ensemble Learning Technique, Random Forest, provides a good evaluation score on the test data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Ensemble Learning Technique, Random Forest, provides a good evaluation score on the tested data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Minor wording change ('test data' vs 'tested data') but meaning and certainty are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Kalman Filter shows good results for short-term estimation of COVID-19 spread. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Kalman Filter shows good results for short term estimation of the spread of SARS-Cov-2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same methodological claim; only minor terminology change (COVID-19 vs SARS‑CoV‑2) with identical implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis identifies various important factors contributing to the spread of COVID-19. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis and plots successfully identify different contributing factors to the spread of COVID19. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication wording emphasizes successful identification and includes plots but preserves the substantive conclusion about contributing factors.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Kalman Filter does not perform well for long-term forecasting of COVID-19 spread. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Kalman Filter does not perform well for long term forecasting of SARS-Cov-2 spread. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Same limitation reported; only minor terminology change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inferences from Random Forest feature importance and Pearson Correlation reveal similarities and dissimilarities in contributing factors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis and plots successfully identify different contributing factors to the spread of COVID19. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication retains the general finding about contributing factors but drops the specific methodological emphasis on Random Forest feature importance and Pearson correlation, narrowing the methodological detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most common observed craniofacial features in individuals with 3q29 deletion syndrome are prominent forehead, prominent nose tip, and thin upper lip vermillion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The most common craniofacial features observed in individuals with 3q29del include prominent forehead, prominent nose tip, and thin upper lip vermillion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The listed features and certainty are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The FDNA technology demonstrated an ability to distinguish 3q29 deletion syndrome cases from controls with an AUC-ROC value of 0.873. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning algorithm DeepGestalt demonstrated an ability to distinguish 3q29del cases from controls with an AUC-ROC value of 0.873. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same quantitative performance but the publication specifies the algorithm name (DeepGestalt) rather than the FDNA technology label, clarifying the technical attribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study found a recognizable facial pattern in individuals with 3q29 deletion syndrome, as observed by trained clinical geneticists and next generation phenotyping technology. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study found a recognizable facial pattern in 3q29del, as observed by trained clinical geneticists and next-generation phenotyping technology. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Substantively identical claim about a recognizable facial pattern observed by experts and technology.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results expand the potential application of automated technology such as FDNA in identifying rare genetic syndromes, even when facial dysmorphology is subtle. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results expand the potential application of automated technology such as FDNA in identifying rare genetic syndromes. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication preserves the conclusion about expanding applications of automated technology but omits the explicit note about subtle dysmorphology, making the claim slightly narrower.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study details the craniofacial phenotype associated with 3q29 deletion syndrome (3q29del). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication includes a general framing statement about detailing the phenotype that is not presented as a separate, explicit claim in the preprint (it is a broader summary), so it is treated as an added publication-level claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mining knowledge from longitudinal studies has the potential to significantly contribute to the improvement of preventive healthcare. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ATHLOS Project aims to improve the interpretation of the impact of aging on health through the analysis of complex longitudinal studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements relate to leveraging longitudinal studies for health insights; the publication frames this as the ATHLOS project's aim and adds methodological context, and its stated certainty is lower (medium) than the preprint's claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ATHLOS Project aims to achieve a better interpretation of the impact of aging on health. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ATHLOS Project aims to improve the interpretation of the impact of aging on health through the analysis of complex longitudinal studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same project aim but the publication adds specificity about using complex longitudinal studies and reports a lower certainty level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The HealthStatus (HS) score is an index that estimates the human status of health. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No direct counterpart to the HS score claim appears in the publication; the concept is not reported, so the claim appears omitted in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results indicate the critical importance of data imputation in enhancing preventive medicine’s crucial role. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Data imputation significantly enhances the predictive power of classification and regression models in preventive healthcare. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the point with more specific performance-oriented language (predictive power of models) and higher certainty, effectively amplifying the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictor correlates approximately 0.76 with lipoprotein A level, which is highly heritable and an independent risk factor for heart disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Polygenic Score (PGS) predictor correlates ∼0.76 with lipoprotein A level, which is highly heritable and an independent risk factor for heart disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same numeric correlation and interpretation are reported; publication specifies 'PGS' but meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This may be the most accurate genomic prediction of a quantitative trait that has yet been produced for European ancestry groups. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This may be the most accurate genomic prediction of a quantitative trait that has yet been produced for European ancestry groups. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and level of certainty in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individuals at high risk for conditions such as coronary artery disease and diabetes can be identified using blood and urine biomarkers alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individuals at high risk for conditions such as coronary artery disease and diabetes can be identified using biomarker risk scores (BMRS) alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication uses the term 'biomarker risk scores' rather than explicitly 'blood and urine biomarkers', clarifying terminology while preserving the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ASCVD predictor performs as well as or better than the American College of Cardiology ASCVD Risk Estimator. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (ASCVD) predictor performs as well as or better than the American College of Cardiology ASCVD Risk Estimator. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same comparative performance claim with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Polygenic risk scores for common diseases are compared to risk predictors resulting from the concatenation of learned functions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: No explicit corresponding claim about concatenation of learned functions vs polygenic risk scores appears in the publication, so the preprint claim seems omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Applying closed-loop, direct electrical stimulation to the lateral temporal cortex can reliably improve memory in a cohort of patients with a history of moderate-to-severe traumatic brain injury. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Applying closed-loop, direct electrical stimulation to the lateral temporal cortex can reliably improve memory in a cohort of patients with a history of moderate-to-severe traumatic brain injury. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with equivalent wording and certainty; no meaningful change in scope or confidence is evident.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The strategy of using personalized machine-learning classifiers to predict momentary fluctuations in mnemonic function and trigger stimulation yielded a 19% boost in recall performance on stimulated lists compared to non-stimulated lists. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The strategy of using personalized machine-learning classifiers to predict momentary fluctuations in mnemonic function and trigger high-frequency stimulation resulted in a 19% boost in recall performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same quantitative effect and methodology with similar wording and unchanged certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results provide a proof-of-concept for using closed-loop stimulation of the brain in the treatment of TBI-related memory impairment. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study provides a proof-of-concept for using closed-loop stimulation of the brain in the treatment of traumatic brain injury-related memory impairment. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is restated verbatim with consistent framing and certainty, representing no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model significantly predicted post-therapy body congruence in individuals with gender dysphoria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning model significantly predicted post-therapy body congruence using clinical and MRI data collected before therapy. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The highest predictive features for post-therapy body congruence came from the fronto-parietal and cingulo-opercular networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The highest predictive features for post-therapy body congruence came from the cingulo-opercular and fronto-parietal networks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The feature-localization is identical but the reported certainty is reduced from high to medium, so the claim content is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hormone therapy efficacy can be predicted from information collected before therapy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning model significantly predicted post-therapy body congruence using clinical and MRI data collected before therapy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's broad conclusion about predictability is made more concrete in the publication by specifying the model and data used, clarifying but not contradicting the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patterns of functional brain connectivity may provide insights into body-brain effects of hormones, affecting one’s sense of body congruence. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patterns of functional brain connectivity may provide insights into the body-brain effects of hormones, affecting one's sense of body congruence. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this interpretive conclusion with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Results could help identify the need for personalized therapies in individuals predicted to have low body-self congruence after standard therapy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Results could help identify the need for personalized therapies in individuals predicted to have low body-self congruence after standard therapy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The translational implication is retained verbatim with unchanged certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: COVINet outperforms all competing models for Mean Absolute Errors (MAEs) and Mean Relative Errors (MREs) when predicting total deaths. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: COVINet outperforms all competing models for Mean Absolute Errors (MAEs) and Mean Relative Errors (MREs) when predicting total deaths. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative performance claim with equivalent metrics and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Mean Relative Errors (MREs) are small for all predictions made in the last 7 or 30 days before March 23, 2023. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Mean Relative Errors (MREs) are small for all predictions made in the last 7 or 30 days before March 23, 2023. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: COVINet offers high interpretability, enhancing the understanding of pandemic dynamics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: COVINet offers high interpretability, enhancing the understanding of pandemic dynamics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: COVINet is positioned as a powerful tool for informing effective strategies in pandemic prevention and governmental decision-making. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: COVINet is positioned as a powerful tool for informing effective strategies in pandemic prevention and governmental decision-making. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The applied-policy implication is preserved in the publication with comparable certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Between 2015-2019, weekly AKI admissions peaked in winter, with additional summer peaks related to periods of extreme heat. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Weekly AKI admissions peaked in winter, with additional summer peaks related to periods of extreme heat. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The temporal patterning of admissions is reported identically in the publication with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate winter seasonality of AKI admissions in England, particularly among those with AKI diagnosed on admission, suggestive of community triggers. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Winter seasonality of AKI admissions was more evident in those diagnosed with AKI on admission. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the same finding (seasonality more evident on-admission), preserving the inference about community-related triggers and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differences in seasonality between phenotypes suggests some groups may be more likely to develop AKI as a result of these factors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Differences in seasonality between phenotypes suggest some groups may be more likely to develop AKI as a result of community triggers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the interpretive conclusion linking phenotype-specific seasonality to differential risk from community triggers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study is the largest and most granular investigation of AKI seasonality in England, detecting acute changes in incidence and differences in peaks year to year. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We describe six phenotypes of people admitted to hospital with AKI, among which seasonality of AKI admissions was observed. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from claiming the study's size/granularity to reporting the description of six phenotypes; this represents a revision in stated contribution/ emphasis rather than a direct one-to-one restatement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future research on AKI should take into account seasonality; uncertainty remains on the main drivers and aetiology of the seasonal patterns observed. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The observed seasonality may be driven by underlying comorbidity profiles or reflect differences in uptake of seasonal interventions such as vaccines. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication moves from stating uncertainty to proposing specific possible drivers with lower certainty, which clarifies hypotheses but lowers confidence relative to the preprint's framing of uncertainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Males exhibited significantly larger mid-AAoD than females. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Males exhibited significantly larger mid-ascending aortic diameter (mid-AAoD) than females. (certainty=high, type=result)
