alignments.pub_claim_text: The model's predictive accuracy was 0.77 for subtype 1, 0.78 for subtype 2, and 0.70 for subtype 3. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The predictive accuracy metrics are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Each imaging cluster exhibited unique molecular mechanisms linked to different pathways. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Each imaging cluster exhibited unique molecular mechanisms linked to specific pathways. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the claim (higher certainty and framed as result), clarifying and amplifying the original conclusion about molecular mechanisms per cluster.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our radiogenomics study indicates that the intrinsic molecular characteristics of tumors correlate with distinct imaging subgroups in pLGG. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our radiogenomics study indicates that the intrinsic molecular characteristics of tumors correlate with distinct imaging subgroups in pLGG. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding statement linking molecular characteristics and imaging subgroups is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Heg.IA web system demonstrates high efficiency in diagnosing Covid-19 with an accuracy of 92.891%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Heg.IA system achieved an average accuracy of 92.891%±0.851 for Covid-19 diagnosis.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same accuracy value but adds the average±SD notation, which clarifies uncertainty around the estimate without changing the substantive result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Heg.IA can predict the type of hospitalization required for Covid-19 patients with accuracies above 99%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Heg.IA system demonstrated excellent performance for hospitalization indication with accuracies above 99%.
alignments.rationale: Wording is effectively identical: both claims state >99% accuracy for hospitalization indication with similar certainty and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system utilizes a Random Forest architecture with 90 decision trees to optimize Covid-19 diagnosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Heg.IA utilizes a Random Forest architecture with 90 trees to optimize Covid-19 diagnosis.
alignments.rationale: Both describe the same model architecture (Random Forest with 90 trees); phrasing is equivalent, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Heg.IA may help overcome testing unavailability in the context of Covid-19.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include or restate a claim about addressing testing unavailability, so this conclusion from the preprint is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of Heg.IA is expected to provide wide access to effective Covid-19 diagnosis and help save lives.
alignments.rationale: The publication omits this broader, lower-certainty societal/consequence claim about access and saving lives that appeared in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The system provides diagnostic reports based on 41 hematological parameters from blood tests and arterial gasometry.
alignments.rationale: This methodological/detail claim about the 41 parameters is present in the publication but was not stated as a separate claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NeuroFlow Severity Score (NFSS) demonstrates a strong correlation with the Personal Health Questionnaire-9 (PHQ-9) and Generalized Anxiety Disorder-7 (GAD-7) scores.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The severity score developed from the mHealth platform shows a strong correlation with PHQ-9 and GAD-7 scores.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about strong correlation with PHQ-9 and GAD-7 without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NFSS shows a strong positive correlation with expert panel clinical assessments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The severity score can be reliably used by clinicians as a proxy measure for screening and monitoring behavioral health symptoms.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized correlation with expert-panel assessments; the publication frames the implication as clinician-usable proxy, which reframes and generalizes the original claim (clarifying the applied use).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NFSS may identify at-risk individuals who are not identified by the PHQ-9.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The severity score may identify at-risk individuals who are not identified by the PHQ-9.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this same tentative conclusion about identifying at-risk individuals missed by PHQ-9.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are significant differences between the NFSS and the PHQ-9 according to Bland-Altman analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bland-Altman analysis indicated that the severity score was significantly different from the PHQ-9.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated in the publication, and certainty is presented as high there (amplified from the preprint's medium), so confidence is increased.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clinicians can reliably use the NFSS as a proxy measure for monitoring symptoms of depression and anxiety longitudinally.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The severity score can be reliably used by clinicians as a proxy measure for screening and monitoring behavioral health symptoms.
alignments.rationale: This conclusion is effectively preserved verbatim in the publication that endorses clinician use for screening and monitoring.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Further research is warranted to evaluate the sensitivity and specificity of the severity score.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit recommendation for further research on sensitivity and specificity that was not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified 16 priority areas for digital health in palliative care, summarized into eight themes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified 16 priority areas for digital health in palliative care, which include applications of technologies for patient and caregiver care, self-management, education, communication, and research methodology.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the 16 priorities and expands on their content, adding detail without changing the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified priorities represent a wide range of important emerging areas in the field of digital health, personalized medicine, and data science.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identified priorities encompass a wide range of emerging areas in digital health, personalized medicine, and data science.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the breadth of the priorities is retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Human-centred design and robust governance systems should be considered in future research on digital health in palliative care.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Future research should consider human-centered design and robust governance systems to address the risks associated with using digital technologies in palliative care.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this recommendation, adding explicit linkage to addressing risks but keeping the substance the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is important to address the risks of using digital health technologies in palliative care to ensure they are used meaningfully and safely.
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is important to ensure that digital health tools in palliative care are used meaningfully and safely to avoid unintentional harm.
alignments.rationale: The limitation/precaution is present in both; the publication frames it with slightly lower certainty (medium) compared with the preprint (high), so confidence is slightly tempered.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The final consensus on research priorities was established through a Delphi questionnaire and public engagement workshop involving experts and public representatives.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit methodological description of how the consensus was reached which was not stated as a discrete claim in the preprint claims list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Genus-level agreement between conventional methods and metagenomic whole genome sequencing (MG-WGS) was 96.2%, increasing to 100% in monomicrobial infections.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Species-level agreement between conventional methods and antimicrobial susceptibility testing predicted from sequencing was 94.2%, increasing to 100% in monomicrobial infections.
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the taxonomic level (genus→species) and the reported percentage, so the claim's content and metric have been revised and made more specific (narrower).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In total, 262 high quality AREScloud AST predictions exhibited categorical agreement of 89.3%, with major error and very major error rates of 10.5% and 12.1%, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In 262 high-quality AREScloud antimicrobial susceptibility predictions across 24 samples, categorical agreement was 89.3%, with major error and very major error rates of 10.5% and 12.1%, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance metrics and sample count are the same between preprint and publication, so this result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Time to reporting from MG-WGS could be achieved within 8-16 hours from blood culture positivity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Results could be achieved in timeframes that are faster than conventional phenotypic methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains a claim of faster turnaround but removes the specific 8–16 hour range, so the precise timeframe is revised/tempered to a more general statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Direct metagenomic sequencing from positive BC broths is feasible and can provide accurate predictive AST for some species and antibiotics, but is sub-optimal for a subset of common pathogens.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Direct sequencing from positive blood culture broths is feasible and can provide accurate predictive antimicrobial susceptibility testing for some species.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains feasibility and accurate prediction for some species but omits the explicit caveat about being sub-optimal for some common pathogens; the claim is therefore presented in a slightly cleaner/less cautious form (clarified).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Improvements in accuracy are required before nanopore-based approaches can be considered for clinical use.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ONT-based approaches may be faster but significant improvements in accuracy are required before it can be considered for clinical use.
alignments.rationale: Both documents convey the same limitation regarding accuracy requirements for ONT/nanopore approaches before clinical adoption; the publication adds the note about speed but does not change the substantive limitation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CA-GAN outperforms state-of-the-art methods in a qualitative and a quantitative evaluation while avoiding mode collapse.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CA-GAN outperforms state-of-the-art methods in a qualitative and a quantitative evaluation while avoiding mode collapse.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this performance claim verbatim; there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synthetic data generated by our CA-GAN improves model fairness in Black patients as well as female patients when evaluated separately for each subpopulation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Synthetic data generated by our CA-GAN improves model fairness in Black patients as well as female patients when evaluated separately for each subpopulation.
alignments.rationale: This subgroup fairness improvement claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CA-GAN generates authentic data of the minority class while faithfully maintaining the original distribution of data, resulting in improved performance in a downstream predictive task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CA-GAN generates authentic data of the minority class while faithfully maintaining the original distribution of data.
alignments.rationale: The publication separates the generation/authenticity claim from downstream performance and reduces the certainty of the authenticity claim to medium; thus the generation claim is clarified and its confidence slightly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CA-GAN generates authentic data of the minority class while faithfully maintaining the original distribution of data, resulting in improved performance in a downstream predictive task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of CA-GAN results in improved performance in a downstream predictive task.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states the downstream performance improvement that was included as part of the preprint claim, so this result is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictive analytics tool CoMET demonstrates model performance during the COVID-19 pandemic. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models were well-calibrated, and there were dynamic increases in the model scores in the hours preceding the clinical deterioration events. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's general claim that CoMET demonstrates model performance is preserved in the publication, which provides additional detail about calibration and temporal dynamics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model performance of CoMET was assessed with areas under the receiver operating characteristic curve ranging from 0.732 to 0.745. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model performance of CoMET was assessed based on areas under the receiver operating characteristic curve, which ranged from 0.725 to 0.737. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims report AUC-based performance but the publication gives slightly revised numeric bounds, so the claim is clarified rather than substantively changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A hypothetical alerting strategy based on a rise in CoMET scores would have had a positive predictive value more than 10-fold the event rate. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A hypothetical alerting strategy based on a rise in score and duration of the rise would have had good performance, with a positive predictive value more than 10-fold the event rate. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The core conclusion about a high PPV is retained; the publication adds the detail that duration of rise was considered, clarifying the alerting strategy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predictive statistical models developed five years before study initiation had good model performance despite the passage of time and the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictive statistical models developed five years before study initiation had good model performance despite the passage of time and the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stability of model performance may be due to continuous cardiorespiratory monitoring, which should not drift as practices, policies, and patient populations change. (certainty=low, type=speculation)
alignments.rationale: The speculative explanation about continuous cardiorespiratory monitoring does not appear in the publication and thus is removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Denoising autoencoder-based imputation of missing key features in heterogeneous Alzheimer's disease data demonstrates robust classification performance with accuracy of 79-85% and precision of 71-85%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Imputed datasets using random forest classification achieved robust predictive performance for Alzheimer's disease, with accuracy ranging from 79% to 85% and precision from 71% to 85%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance numbers match but the publication emphasizes random forest classification on imputed data whereas the preprint emphasized autoencoder imputation; the core result is retained but method attribution is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High recall values for identifying individuals with Alzheimer's disease were particularly observed in datasets with 40% missingness in key features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific high-recall finding or the 40% missingness detail, so this preprint claim is not present in the published paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The feature-selected dataset using feature selection methods, including autoencoder, achieved a higher classification score than the original complete dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Feature selection methods, including autoencoder, resulted in higher classification scores compared to the original complete dataset. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the finding that feature selection improved scores but reports it with lower certainty, so the result is unchanged in content but the confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights the effectiveness and robustness of autoencoder in imputing crucial information for reliable Alzheimer's disease prediction in AI-based clinical decision support systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the potential of autoencoder-based imputation for enhancing reliability in Alzheimer's disease prediction within AI-based clinical decision support systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down the claim from asserting effectiveness/robustness to emphasizing potential, so the claim is clarified and the confidence is tempered.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Denoising autoencoder-based imputation effectively addresses missing key features in heterogeneous Alzheimer's disease data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a general framing statement about autoencoder imputation effectiveness that was not separately stated as such in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research provides preliminary evidence of a transdiagnostic brain subnetwork deficit characterized by information diffusion impairment of the right area 8BM. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research provides preliminary evidence of a transdiagnostic brain subnetwork deficit characterized by information diffusion impairment of the right area 8BM. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this core result verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models of neuromodulation involving targeting the right area 8BM normalize internalizing psychopathologies diffusion dynamics towards those of healthy controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models of neuromodulation targeting the right area 8BM normalize internalizing psychopathology diffusion dynamics towards those of healthy controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the neuromodulation modeling result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed analytic framework identifies brain subnetworks with perturbed information diffusion capacity based on resting state functional connectivity. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed analytic framework can identify brain subnetworks with disrupted information diffusion and potential targets for therapeutic neuromodulatory intervention. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the framework's identification capability but extends it to highlight potential therapeutic targets and raises certainty, broadening the contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights a unifying neuropathological substrate for transdiagnostic symptom expression in internalizing psychopathologies is currently unknown. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The explicit statement framing the unifying substrate as unknown does not appear in the publication, so this limitation was not carried forward.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The impairment of information diffusion in the right area 8BM may underlie previously reported dysfunction of multiple brain circuits in internalizing psychopathologies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an interpretive conclusion linking the 8BM diffusion impairment to broader circuit dysfunction that was not explicitly stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The national seroincidence rate of V. cholerae O1 in Bangladesh is estimated to be 19.1%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The national annual seroincidence of Vibrio cholerae O1 infection in Bangladesh is estimated to be 17.3%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both present national seroincidence estimates but the publication reports a revised numeric estimate, so the claim is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Community-level annual infection risk within the sampled population ranges from 2.4% to 69.0%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is significant heterogeneity in community-level annual infection risk across Bangladesh, ranging from 4.3% to 62.9%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the heterogeneity finding but reports different bounds, indicating a clarified estimate.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Approximately 31.0 million V. cholerae infections occurred in Bangladesh in the year before the survey. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Approximately 28.1 million infections of Vibrio cholerae O1 occurred in Bangladesh in the year prior to the survey. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The overall infection count is preserved as a result but the publication reports a revised numeric estimate.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most V. cholerae infections in Bangladesh occur in urban areas, including Dhaka. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state that most infections occur in urban areas, so this preprint conclusion was not carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Serosurveillance can identify areas with high V. cholerae O1 transmission and help explore key risk factors for infection. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Serosurveillance can identify areas with high Vibrio cholerae O1 transmission and help investigate key risk factors for infection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the contribution about serosurveillance's utility with equivalent meaning.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Serosurveillance could be an important method for countries to plan and monitor progress towards cholera elimination goals by 2030. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a policy-oriented conclusion about use of serosurveillance for elimination planning that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Human-to-human transmissions, as well as asymptomatic transmissions of COVID-19, have been confirmed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Human-to-human transmission and asymptomatic transmission of COVID-19 have been confirmed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this epidemiologic fact with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The impact of temperature and demographic factors on virus transmission may be characterized using a stochastic transmission model. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The impact of temperature and demographic factors on virus transmission may be characterized using a stochastic transmission model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The content is preserved between preprint and publication; the publication frames it as a conclusion rather than a result but the substantive claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Revised estimation and our methodology will aid in improving the human health consequences of COVID-19 elsewhere. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our overall research indicates that revised estimation and methodology will aid in improving the human health consequences of COVID-19 elsewhere. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about the utility of revised estimation and methodology.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Understanding the characteristics of spatiotemporal clustering of the COVID-19 epidemic and R0 is critical in effectively preventing and controlling the ongoing global pandemic. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Understanding the characteristics of spatial clustering of the COVID-19 epidemic and R<sub>0</sub> is critical in effectively preventing and controlling the ongoing global pandemic. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same critical conclusion but slightly adjusts wording (spatiotemporal -> spatial), a minor clarification of phrasing rather than substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An immunoaffinity purification followed by high resolution mass spectrometry-based targeted assay can detect viral antigen in nasopharyngeal swab samples of SARS-CoV-2 infected individuals.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimized targeted assay achieved 98% sensitivity and 100% specificity in detecting SARS-CoV-2 from nasopharyngeal swab samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the detection claim but adds quantified performance (sensitivity/specificity), clarifying the original detection assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nucleocapsid protein was selected as a target antigen based on discovery experiments using purified virus, recombinant viral protein, and nasopharyngeal swab samples from COVID-19 positive patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nucleocapsid protein was selected as a target antigen for the detection of SARS-CoV-2.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same selection of nucleocapsid as target antigen with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed automated antibody capture-based workflow coupled to targeted high-field asymmetric ion mobility spectrometry - parallel reaction monitoring assays achieved 97.8% sensitivity and 100% specificity compared to RT-PCR-based molecular testing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimized targeted assay achieved 98% sensitivity and 100% specificity in detecting SARS-CoV-2 from nasopharyngeal swab samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports nearly identical performance metrics (97.8% rounded to 98% and 100% specificity), so the result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Direct detection of infectious agents from clinical samples by mass spectrometry-based assays has potential to be deployed as diagnostic assays in clinical laboratories.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Direct detection of infectious agents from clinical samples by tandem mass spectrometry-based assays has potential for deployment as diagnostic assays in clinical laboratories.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about the potential for MS-based assays to be deployed as diagnostics, with only minor wording change (tandem MS).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hierarchical logistic regression (HLR) models achieved an area under the ROC curve of 0.81, which was statistically significantly higher than that of standard logistic regression (LR) models at 0.75.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hierarchical logistic regression (HLR) models achieved a statistically significantly higher area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.81 compared to standard logistic regression (LR) models, which had a score of 0.75.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance comparison and numeric AUC values with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HLR models achieved statistically significantly lower expected calibration error (0.07) than LR models (0.16).
alignments.pub_claim_text: HLR models achieved a statistically significantly lower expected calibration error of 0.07 compared to LR models, which had an error of 0.16.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the calibration error comparison with identical values and significance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HLR models perform significantly better than LR models with respect to both discrimination and calibration.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance improvement of HLR models over LR models is likely due to explicitly modeling physician-related variability in selecting relevant data.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the improvement claim but clarifies a likely causal mechanism (modeling physician variability) and uses hedging ('likely'), which tempers the certainty about the reason.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The variability in selecting relevant data by physician reviewers indicates that information-seeking behavior varies across EMR users.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Physician reviewers demonstrated variability in selecting relevant data items for ICU patient cases.
alignments.rationale: The publication documents the same variability but specifies the context (ICU patient cases), narrowing the scope from general EMR users to ICU reviewers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biomarkers had a negligible and non-significant effect for predicting the future AD severity scores and the outcome of the systemic therapy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Serum biomarkers had a negligible and non-significant effect for predicting the future AD severity scores.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the negligible effect claim for biomarkers on future AD severity but omits the preprint's additional mention of therapy outcome, narrowing the claim to severity scores.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed statistical machine learning predictive model outperformed standard time-series forecasting models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed statistical machine learning model outperformed standard time-series forecasting models.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adding biomarkers did not improve predictive performance in the model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adding biomarkers did not improve the predictive performance of the model.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the identical result that biomarkers did not improve predictive performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A historical record of severity scores provides rich and insightful dynamical information required for prediction of therapeutic responses.
alignments.rationale: This conceptual claim about historical records providing rich dynamical information does not appear in the publication and was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Smartphone-based digital biomarker assessments can improve diagnosis and monitoring of Parkinson’s disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Smartphone-based digital biomarker assessments can improve diagnosis and monitoring of Parkinson’s disease.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level conclusion about smartphone-based digital biomarkers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The highest balanced accuracy for differentiating PD patients from healthy controls was 73% using random forest when combining all tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The highest balanced accuracy for differentiating PD patients from healthy controls was 73% using random forest with all tasks combined.
alignments.rationale: The numeric result and method are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Not accounting for age, sex, and comorbidities resulted in higher accuracies of up to 77% when combining all tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Not accounting for age, sex, and comorbidities resulted in higher accuracies of up to 77% when combining all tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result and numeric value about unadjusted accuracies.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Only moderate accuracies were achieved for balance, gait, and voice tasks, with the highest being 60% for balance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Only moderate accuracies were achieved for balance, gait, and voice tasks, with the highest being 60%, 56%, and 53% respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides more detailed per-task accuracy numbers, clarifying the preprint's more general statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach created a synthetic dataset that exhibits very similar statistical characteristics with the real dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A synthetic dataset that exhibits very similar statistical characteristics with the real dataset was generated.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result about the synthetic dataset matching statistical characteristics of the real data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pairwise association of variables in the synthetic dataset is very similar to that of the real dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A synthetic dataset that exhibits very similar statistical characteristics with the real dataset was generated.
alignments.rationale: The publication's statement about overall statistical similarity subsumes the specific claim about pairwise associations, clarifying it with a broader summary.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A high degree of Jaccard similarity and a successful K-S test further support the effectiveness of the synthetic dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) based approach is fit for purpose in generating realistic synthetic datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the statistical-test evidence into an overall assertion that the GAN approach is fit for purpose, which clarifies and summarizes the supporting results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proof of concept of generating realistic synthetic datasets was successful.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proof of concept of generating realistic synthetic datasets was successful.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same proof-of-concept conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individuals reporting adverse childhood experiences (ACEs) have fewer total followers in online social networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individuals reporting adverse childhood experiences (ACEs) have fewer total followers in web-based social networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with only minor wording change ('online' vs 'web-based'), preserving meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individuals with ACEs exhibit higher reciprocity in following behavior compared to those without ACEs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individuals with ACEs exhibit higher reciprocity in following behavior compared to those without ACEs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individuals with ACEs are more likely to follow and be followed by other individuals with ACEs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a higher tendency for individuals with ACEs to follow and be followed by other individuals with ACEs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same network homophily result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Supportive interpersonal connections online for individuals with ACEs may enhance social connectedness and resilience. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Supportive interpersonal connections on the web for individuals with ACEs may enhance social connectedness and resilience. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same concluding implication about supportive online connections and resilience with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EnsembleUNets demonstrated better performance in brain tumor segmentation with a Dice Similarity Coefficient score of 0.93 and a Hausdorff Distance of 18. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EnsembleUNets excelled, achieving a Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) of 0.93 and a Hausdorff Distance (HD) of 18, outperforming the other methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same performance metrics and the same comparative conclusion, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Comparative analysis using radiomic features revealed that EnsembleUNets is the most precise segmentation method compared to CaPTk, 2DVNet, and ResNet50. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further comparative analysis of radiomic features confirmed EnsembleUNets as the most precise segmentation method, surpassing other methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same comparative radiomic finding with consistent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EnsembleUNets achieved a Concordance Correlation Coefficient of 0.79, a Total Deviation Index of 1.14, and a Root Mean Square Error of 0.53, outperforming its counterparts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EnsembleUNets recorded a Concordance Correlation Coefficient (CCC) of 0.79, a Total Deviation Index (TDI) of 1.14, and a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.53, underscoring its superior performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same quantitative agreement metrics and interprets them similarly, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides valuable insight into the comparative efficacy of EnsembleUNets for accurate brain tumor segmentation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further comparative analysis of radiomic features confirmed EnsembleUNets as the most precise segmentation method, surpassing other methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's general concluding insight maps to the publication's explicit comparative confirmation; the published wording is more assertive about comparative efficacy, clarifying the earlier conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Ensemble Long Short-term Memory neural network (ELSTM) achieved a significant increase in predictive performance using longitudinal information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Ensemble Longitudinal Network (ELN) achieved a significant increase in predictive performance using longitudinal information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core result is the same, but the model name/description changed in the publication (ELSTM -> ELN), so the claim is clarified/renamed rather than substantively different.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ELSTM showed AUROCs of 0.83 for AdmDemo and 0.87 for AdmDemoDx in predicting one-year mortality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ELN showed AUROCs of 0.83 for AdmDemo and 0.87 for AdmDemoDx in predicting one-year mortality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the identical predictive performance metrics for the corresponding models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The precision of decision-making increased from 0.25 for the standard process to 0.28 for AdmDemo and 0.36 for AdmDemoDx. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The precision of decision-making increased from 0.25 for the standard process to 0.28 for AdmDemo and 0.36 for AdmDemoDx. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same decision-making precision improvements with identical values and interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Feature importance analysis confirmed that the utility of longitudinal information increases with the number of patient hospitalizations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating patients’ longitudinal data provides better insights into the severity of illness and the overall patient condition. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's specific feature-importance observation into a broader conclusion about integrating longitudinal data for patient insights, broadening the scope of the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Following chemotherapy, there was a significant decrease in the PROMIS score. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Following chemotherapy, there was a significant decrease in the PROMIS score. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this behavioral result identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Durations of EEG microstates were significantly longer and less evenly distributed following chemotherapy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Overall, microstate durations were significantly longer and less evenly distributed following chemotherapy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same overall microstate-duration changes with equivalent emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean duration of microstate D significantly increased following chemotherapy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean duration of microstate D, which is involved in attention/executive functions, significantly increased following chemotherapy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds functional interpretation (attention/executive functions) to the same significant increase in microstate D duration, clarifying but not changing the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No significant correlations between microstate features and the PROMIS score were observed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: No significant correlations were observed between behavioral and microstate measures either before or after chemotherapy. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication broadens the claim to state no significant correlations between behavioral and microstate measures at both timepoints (before/after), extending the preprint's PROMIS-focused statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results introduce a potential novel biomarker to evaluate the changes in large scale brain dynamics related to the cognitive effects of chemotherapy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This exploratory study provides new evidence showing that changes occur in large scale brain dynamics related to the cognitive effects of chemotherapy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the exploratory conclusion that chemotherapy affects large-scale brain dynamics and frames the findings as new evidence, consistent with the preprint's implication about a potential biomarker.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Grey matter injury predominated in dyskinetic cerebral palsy (DCP) cases, accounting for 51% of injuries. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Subcortical grey matter lesions are the most common MRI finding in dyskinetic cerebral palsy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey the same core result that grey matter/subcortical grey lesions are the predominant MRI finding in DCP; the publication omits the exact percentage but retains the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lesion network mapping (LNM) revealed functional connectivity to a wide network including the brainstem, cerebellum, basal ganglia, cingulate, and sensorimotor cortices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The lesion-derived map reveals functional connectivity to a wide network including the brainstem, cerebellum, basal ganglia, cingulate, and sensorimotor cortices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same lesion-network connectivity findings with equivalent detail and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The motor thalamus was identified as the strongest connectivity node in the neural network of DCP. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The motor thalamus is identified as a common network node in dyskinetic cerebral palsy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication weakens the claim from 'strongest connectivity node' to a more moderate 'common network node' and lowers certainty, representing a tempered/revised claim with narrower strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network of DCP includes areas previously implicated in hyperkinetic disorders. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The lesion-derived map reveals functional connectivity to a wide network including the brainstem, cerebellum, basal ganglia, cingulate, and sensorimotor cortices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's network description corresponds to the preprint's observation that the DCP network includes regions implicated in hyperkinetic disorders; the mapping is consistent in content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future trials should explore the effects of targeting motor thalamic networks with neuromodulation in DCP. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results of this study should be validated and their therapeutic implications explored in prospective trials. (certainty=low, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Both suggest prospective trials to explore therapeutic implications, but the publication frames this as a validation/limitation with lower certainty, tempering the original recommendation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ConvNet trained using manual annotations obtained the best results with an AUC-ROC of 0.97 and F-score of 0.69. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ConvNet trained using manual annotations obtained the best results with an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.98 and an F score of 0.70. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same result with slightly updated numeric values (0.98 and 0.70), representing a small refinement rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ConvNet’s results were robust to reduction of the number of manually annotated documents. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ConvNet's results were also robust to reduction of the number of manually annotated documents. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same robustness claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the absence of manual annotations, topic models performed better than methods trained using ICD-10 codes, which were unsatisfactory for identifying LBP acuity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the absence of manual annotations, topic models performed better than methods trained using ICD-10 codes, which were unsatisfactory for identifying LBP acuity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result identically, with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study uses clinical notes to delineate a potential path toward systematic learning of therapeutic strategies, billing guidelines, and management options for acute LBP at the point of care. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study uses clinical notes to delineate a potential path toward systematic learning of therapeutic strategies, billing guidelines, and management options for acute LBP at the point of care. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding statement appears unchanged between preprint and publication, with equivalent scope and low certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed fully automatic approaches can classify chest X-Ray images acquired by portable equipment into three clinical categories: normal, pathological, and COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed fully automatic approaches classify chest X-ray images acquired by portable equipment into three clinical categories: normal, pathological, and COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same classification capability; wording is essentially identical (minor change in label to 'contribution').

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The joint response of the two complementary deep learning approaches enhances the differentiation between COVID-19 infected patients and those with other diseases that exhibit similar characteristics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The joint response of the three deep learning approaches enhances the differentiation between COVID-19 infected patients, patients with other diseases, and normal cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the claim from 'two complementary approaches' to 'three' and explicitly includes normal cases in the differentiation, changing the approach count and broadening the comparison.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approaches provided satisfactory results despite the poor quality of chest X-Ray images from portable equipment. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approaches achieved global accuracy values of 79.62%, 90.27%, and 79.86% despite the poor quality of the chest X-ray images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication supplies specific accuracy numbers and reports the results with higher certainty, amplifying and clarifying the preprint's qualitative 'satisfactory' claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of portable equipment for chest X-Ray imaging is beneficial for infection control and minimizes the risk of cross-contamination. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of portable equipment for chest X-ray imaging minimizes the risk of cross-contamination and is beneficial for infection control. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about infection control and reduced cross-contamination is reiterated verbatim with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average precision (AP 75) of the SSD on one class of targets was 97.7%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AP75 for all subjects was 99.7% ± 0.6%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports AP75 values for all subjects with a slightly different numeric value; this appears to be a refinement/clarification of the preprint's AP75 result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The horizontal and vertical target locations significantly and positively correlated with the horizontal and vertical positions of both eyes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The horizontal and vertical target locations were significantly and positively correlated with each eye position in the horizontal and vertical directions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The correlation finding is restated with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VOG with SSD is suitable for the evaluation of eye version movements in standard clinical assessments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The addition of SSD-driven recording of hand-held target positions with VOG allows for quantitative assessment of SPEMs following a target during an SPEM test. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes a more specific claim about quantitative assessment of SPEMs (smooth pursuit eye movements) with the combined method, narrowing the original broader suitability claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of VOG and SSD can be used to evaluate the smooth pursuit eye movements (SPEM) in clinical settings. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combined methods of VOG and SSD can be used to detect SPEMs with greater accuracy, which can improve the outcome of clinical evaluations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication similarly asserts usability for SPEM evaluation but adds an explicit statement about improved accuracy and clinical evaluation outcomes, clarifying the benefit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mask-RCNN performs the best among all the models for semantic segmentation with a DICE score of 0.43 ± 0.01. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Benchmark results are provided on defined protocols using popular deep learning models for classification and semantic segmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports benchmark results but does not reproduce the specific Mask-RCNN DICE score or the explicit claim of 'performs the best,' indicating the model-specific performance claim was revised/tempered into general benchmarking.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MobileNetv2 yields the best results for 2-class and 3-class classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Benchmark results are provided on defined protocols using popular deep learning models for classification and semantic segmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides benchmark summaries without restating the preprint's specific claim that MobileNetv2 yielded the best classification results, indicating a revision to more general benchmarking.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep models report a lower performance for classifying other classes apart from the COVID class. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Benchmark results are provided on defined protocols using popular deep learning models for classification and semantic segmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's benchmark reporting encompasses model performance across classes and likely reflects this observation, though the preprint's specific comparative emphasis is presented in more general benchmark form.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The annotated data and protocols provided will advance the usage of CXRs as a viable solution for efficient COVID-19 diagnostics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Annotated databases of X-rays can significantly enhance the design and development of algorithms for disease prediction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same general claim about the value of annotated X-ray databases for algorithm development; wording is slightly less strong (medium vs high certainty).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The large-scale data will be useful for ML algorithms and can be used for learning radiological patterns observed in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Annotated databases of X-rays can significantly enhance the design and development of algorithms for disease prediction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the utility of annotated large-scale X-ray datasets for algorithm development and pattern learning, but with somewhat lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A 10-year all-cause mortality risk score was developed using only digitally-obtainable variables from the UK Biobank dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts focus to predictive algorithms for depression and anxiety rather than an all-cause mortality score, so this preprint claim does not appear in the published paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The c-index for the Cox proportional hazards model was 0.771 before feature selection and decreased to 0.770 after selecting 34 features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported Cox model c-index values in the preprint relate to all-cause mortality and are not present in the publication, which reports concordance indices for depression and anxiety models instead.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DeepSurv model achieved a c-index of 0.774 before feature selection and decreased to 0.758 after feature selection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The DeepSurv performance metrics for mortality reported in the preprint are not retained in the publication focused on depression and anxiety predictions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed score improves on the previous C-Score by incorporating non-modifiable factors along with modifiable variables. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not discuss the prior C-Score or present an improved all-cause mortality score; the topic and comparisons were not carried over.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The score is comprehensive, easily interpretable, and actionable, making it a powerful tool for preventative healthcare. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This broader preventive-health claim about the mortality score is absent from the publication, which instead presents predictive models for mental-health outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The microscopic architecture of prostate glands is the basis for prognostic grading by pathologists.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim asserts or restates the general claim that microscopic gland architecture underlies prognostic grading; the pub focuses on 3D pathology and clinical utility rather than this epistemic basis, so the claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 3D glandular features are superior to corresponding 2D features for risk stratification of low-to intermediate-risk prostate cancer patients based on their clinical biochemical recurrence outcomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 3D glandular features in cancer biopsies were superior to corresponding 2D features for risk stratification of patients with low- to intermediate-risk prostate cancer.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same result on superiority of 3D over 2D glandular features for low- to intermediate-risk prostate cancer with equivalent specificity and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed workflow for non-destructive 3D pathology and computational analysis can improve risk assessment and treatment decisions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed workflow for nondestructive 3D pathology and computational analysis improves risk assessment and treatment decisions in prostate cancer.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution but expresses it more directly (removing 'can') and specifies prostate cancer, making the claim clearer without changing its substantive scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ITAS3D enables tissue microstructures to be volumetrically segmented in an annotation-free and objective manner without requiring real immunolabeling.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ITAS3D is a generalizable deep learning-based strategy that enables tissue microstructures to be volumetrically segmented in an annotation-free and objective manner.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds that ITAS3D is a generalizable deep-learning strategy but otherwise preserves the original claim about annotation-free volumetric segmentation, thus clarifying/generalizing implementation details.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep learning-based algorithm achieves a cluster-wise true positive rate (TPR) over 90% with an average of less than 2 false positives per subject.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method achieved a cluster-wise true positive rate (TPR) of over 90% with an average of less than 2 false positives per subject.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact performance result, matching metrics and thresholds reported in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method demonstrates flexibility in terms of input modality and provides comparable cluster-wise TPR and better cluster-wise precision compared to existing state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method provides comparable cluster-wise TPR and better cluster-wise precision compared to existing state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the modality-flexible performance claim and the comparative precision/TPR statement without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When evaluated across different datasets, the method shows good generalisability with a cluster-wise TPR greater than 80% with different modalities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: When evaluating across different datasets, our method showed good generalizability with a cluster-wise TPR greater than 80% with different modalities.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same cross-dataset generalizability result (TPR >80%), matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates robust binary classifier models to differentiate blood serum from osteoarthritis (OA) and rheumatoid arthritis (RA).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall model performance for classifying OA and RA patients achieved an AUC-ROC value of 0.72 in the test dataset and 0.87 in the validation dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents specific AUC performance (including validation) that operationalizes the preprint's broader claim of robust classifiers for OA vs RA, adding detail and validation results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall model performance was better in classifying OA and RA patients compared to RA and controls, and OA and controls.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning models demonstrated better performance in classifying between OA and RA patients compared to RA and controls, and OA and controls.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative performance ordering across the three pairwise classification tasks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AUC-ROC values for the test dataset were 0.84 for OA vs. controls, 0.76 for RA vs. controls, and 0.72 for OA vs. RA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall model performance for classifying OA and RA patients achieved an AUC-ROC value of 0.72 in the test dataset and 0.87 in the validation dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication revises the reported numeric performance: the OA vs RA test AUC remains 0.72 but other test-set AUCs differ and a validation AUC (0.87) is added, indicating updated/ corrected metrics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study indicates the potential of ATR-FTIR as an effective aid in rheumatic disease classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ATR-FTIR shows potential as an effective aid in the classification of arthritic diseases based on blood serum samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same medium-certainty conclusion about ATR-FTIR's potential as an aid in arthritic disease classification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Four biologically distinct subphenotypes of COVID-19 were derived using clustering analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four biologically distinct subphenotypes of COVID-19 were derived using clustering analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same clustering result and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified subphenotypes were highly predictive of clinical outcomes, particularly 60-day mortality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identified subphenotypes were highly predictive of clinical outcomes, particularly 60-day mortality.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same predictive relationship between subphenotypes and outcomes including 60-day mortality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Social determinants of health specifically influenced mortality outcomes in Subphenotype IV, which is associated with older age, worse clinical manifestation, and high comorbidity burden.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Social determinants of health specifically influenced mortality outcomes in Subphenotype IV, which is associated with older age, worse clinical manifestation, and high comorbidity burden.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same result linking SDOH to mortality specifically in Subphenotype IV with the same characteristics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings may lead to a better understanding of how COVID-19 causes disease in different populations and potentially benefit clinical trial development.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings may lead to a better understanding of how COVID-19 causes disease in different populations and potentially benefit clinical trial development.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same prospective implication regarding understanding disease mechanisms and aiding trial development.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The temporal patterns and implications of social determinants of health may add new insights to health policy to reduce social disparity in the pandemic.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include or emphasize the specific policy-oriented claim about temporal SDOH patterns and health policy implications, so this preprint claim was not retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning methods demonstrate high detection accuracy of 92.5% in predicting mortality in COVID-19 patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dimensionality reduction provided strong benefits for performance, increasing the baseline accuracy of a random forest classifier from 89.2% to 92.5% when training on 7 chi-square-extracted features.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same 92.5% accuracy figure and attributes it to feature selection/dimensionality reduction; the core numeric accuracy claim is retained though context is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stratifying Random Forest and Logistic Regression methods achieve modestly strong performance with 77.4%-79.3% sensitivity and 71.4%-72.6% accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the same sensitivity/accuracy ranges for stratified RF/LR models; those specific reported metrics do not appear in the published claims and so were dropped.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mutual information as a dimensionality reduction technique greatly improves performance and allows for timely and accurate predictions using a small number of easily retrievable attributes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Strong dimensionality reduction was shown to further improve baseline performance on selected classifiers and only marginally reduce it in others.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the central claim that dimensionality reduction boosts performance but frames it more cautiously and generally across classifiers, clarifying and slightly tempering the original emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset has limitations that should be explored and detailed, as it can produce valid conclusions only for a limited set of applications.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims focus on performance results and benefits of feature selection and do not emphasize the specific caveats about dataset limitations expressed in the preprint, so this limitation statement was not carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future use of this dataset should be cautious and accompanied by disclaimers on issues of real-life reproducibility.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include an explicit cautionary recommendation or disclaimer language about future dataset use, so this preprint limitation/recommendation was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictive models successfully identified 6 fingerprints that were associated with COVID-19 positive status. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive models successfully identified 6 fingerprints that were associated with COVID-19 positive status. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication sentence is identical in content and certainty to the preprint claim, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lower integration across the brain modules and increased segregation were associated with higher infection rates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lower integration across the brain modules and increased segregation were associated with higher infection rates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The association statement is reproduced verbatim in the publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: COVID-19 positive status was associated with reduced connectivity between the central executive and ventral salience networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: COVID-19 positive status was associated with reduced connectivity between the central executive and ventral salience networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same network-level connectivity finding with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individuals are at increased risk of COVID-19 infections if their brain connectome shows reduced connectivity in top-down attention and executive networks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individuals are at increased risk of COVID-19 infections if their brain connectome shows reduced connectivity in top-down attention and executive networks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion linking reduced top-down connectivity to increased infection risk is carried forward unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified risk networks could be investigated as targets for treatment of illnesses with impulse control deficits. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identified risk networks could be investigated as targets for treatment of illnesses with impulse control deficits. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The translational suggestion about investigating the networks as treatment targets is preserved with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine learning-based model was created to automatically identify PubMed articles likely to report clinical outcome results from registered trials in ClinicalTrials.gov. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning-based model was created to automatically identify PubMed articles likely to report clinical outcome results from registered trials in ClinicalTrials.gov. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the development of the ML model with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evaluation of the model showed that NCT-linked articles tend to be top ranked, with a median best rank of 1.0 and 91% ranked in the top ten. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evaluation of the model showed that known linked articles tend to be top ranked, with a median best rank of 1.0 and 91% ranked in the top 10. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The evaluation metrics and conclusions are the same; minor wording change (NCT-linked vs known linked) does not alter meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A free, public web-based tool was developed that presents a ranked list of PubMed articles based on their estimated probability of reporting clinical outcome data from a given registered trial. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A free, public web-based tool was developed that presents a ranked list of PubMed articles based on their estimated probability of reporting clinical outcome data from a given registered trial. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution describing the web tool with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Subsets of accuracy-cost optimised assessment items can perform better in terms of diagnostic accuracy and/or total assessment time than most other standard assessments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Subsets of accuracy-cost optimised assessment items can perform better in terms of diagnostic accuracy and/or total assessment time than most other standard assessments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about optimized item subsets is reproduced verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis and accompanying sandbox tool can facilitate clinical users and other stakeholders to apply their own domain knowledge to analyse and decide which dementia diagnostic assessment items are useful. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis and accompanying sandbox tool can facilitate clinical users and other stakeholders to apply their own domain knowledge to analyse and decide which dementia diagnostic assessment items are useful. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about the utility of the analysis and sandbox tool for stakeholders.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: By optimising diagnostic accuracy and assessment time, we redesign predictive and efficient dementia diagnostic assessments. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: By optimising diagnostic accuracy and assessment time, we redesign predictive and efficient dementia diagnostic assessments. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim about redesigning assessments is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A combination of elevated levels of interleukin-6 and C-reactive protein, coupled with reduced monocyte HLA-DR expression and reduced neutrophil CD10 expression, are strongly predictive of severe COVID-19 disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Severe COVID-19 is most strongly associated with elevated CRP and IL-6, loss of DLA-DR expression on monocytes, and CD10 expression on neutrophils. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same four-marker association using slightly different phrasing/nomenclature but preserves the same result and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These immunological changes can be identified from tests carried out prior to peak illness severity in a diagnostic laboratory. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These four markers could be used to identify patients developing severe COVID-19 and allow timely delivery of therapeutics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the laboratory-detectable changes as actionable markers for identifying patients at risk and enabling timely therapy — a clarified, more application-focused statement of the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictive model for severe COVID-19 was derived from a cohort of patients with a wide range of ages, frailty, and COVID-19 severity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unbiased machine learning demonstrated that the four identified features were strongly associated with severe disease, achieving an average prediction score of 0.925. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides additional methodological detail and a numeric performance metric for the predictive model derived from the diverse cohort, thereby clarifying and strengthening the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The median average treatment effect on the treated (ATT) of 13 common treatments in children and young adults with CP is medium at the anatomy and physiology level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current treatments have medium effects on body structures in children and young adults with cerebral palsy. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about medium effects on anatomy/physiology (body structures) but with slightly different certainty labeling and broader wording; the claim is clarified and supported by reported results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The efficacy of treatments becomes smaller as we move along the causal chain from anatomy and physiology to gait parameter, overall gait pattern, and function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effects of treatments on gait and functional mobility are modest to minimal, with the median ATT decreasing along the causal chain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same pattern of decreasing effect size along the causal chain, matching the preprint claim in both meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current treatments have modest to minimal efficacy on gait and function in children and young adults with CP. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effects of treatments on gait and functional mobility are modest to minimal, with the median ATT decreasing along the causal chain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about modest-to-minimal effects on gait and function is directly reflected in the publication's statement about gait and functional mobility outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further work is needed to understand the source of heterogeneous treatment effects in this patient population. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further work is needed to understand the source of heterogeneous treatment effects in this patient population. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation for further investigation is preserved with the same scope and level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Replication or refutation of these findings by other centers will be valuable to establish the generalizability of these results. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
