alignments.pub_claim_text: Replication or refutation of these findings by other centers will be valuable for establishing the generalizability of these results. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same call for external replication to assess generalizability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A data-driven diagnostic model was developed to predict the likelihood of HFpEF among patients with unexplained dyspnea and preserved left ventricular EF. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A data-driven diagnostic model was developed to predict the likelihood of HFpEF among patients with unexplained dyspnea and preserved left ventricular EF. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The diagnostic power of detecting HFpEF in a validation cohort had an AUROC of 90% and an average precision of 74%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The diagnostic power of detecting HFpEF in a validation cohort had an AUROC of 90% and an average precision of 74%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance metrics are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two HFpEF diagnostic signatures were derived, comprising symptoms, diagnoses, and investigation results. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two HFpEF diagnostic signatures were derived, comprising symptoms, diagnoses, and investigation results. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result about the two diagnostic signatures with matching content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed diagnostic signature can assist in the diagnostic evaluation in patients with unexplained dyspnea. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This diagnostic signature can assist in the evaluation of patients with unexplained dyspnea. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same conclusion with only minor wording differences; scope and certainty are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Participants with a diagnosis of corpus callosum dysgenesis demonstrated significantly higher persuadability, credulity, and insensitivity to social trickery than neurotypical participants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants with a diagnosis of corpus callosum dysgenesis demonstrated significantly higher persuadability, credulity, and insensitivity to social trickery than neurotypical participants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is restated verbatim in the publication with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning analysis indicated that participants with corpus callosum dysgenesis were best classed along the same axis as persuadability, credulity, and insensitivity to social trickery. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning algorithm was able to classify participants with corpus callosum dysgenesis with a mean accuracy of 71.7% based on dimensions of persuadability, credulity, and insensitivity to social trickery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a quantitative accuracy and stronger certainty, clarifying and strengthening the original ML claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings have wide-reaching implications for the characterisation of corpus callosum dysgenesis and the role of the corpus callosum in social inference. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These results have implications for the characterisation of corpus callosum dysgenesis and the role of the corpus callosum in social inference. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down "wide-reaching" to simply "have implications," reducing rhetorical breadth while preserving the core conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study was able to distinguish children with suicide thoughts or behaviors (STB) from healthy controls and clinical controls using a machine learning approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study successfully distinguished children with suicide thoughts or behaviours from healthy controls with an area under the receiver operating characteristics curve of 0.80 for child-report and 0.81 for parent-report. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies report types and exact AUROC values, clarifying the preprint's general result without changing its scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The area under the receiver operating characteristics curve (AUROC) for distinguishing the STB group from healthy controls ranged from 0.79 to 0.81. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study successfully distinguished children with suicide thoughts or behaviours from healthy controls with an area under the receiver operating characteristics curve of 0.80 for child-report and 0.81 for parent-report. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides more precise breakdowns (child vs parent report) for the AUROC range reported in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Factors that differentiated the STB group from the clinical control group included family conflict, prodromal psychosis symptoms, impulsivity, depression severity, and a history of mental health treatment. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study found that family conflict, prodromal psychosis symptoms, impulsivity, depression severity, and history of mental health treatment were factors that differentiated the suicide thoughts or behaviours group from the clinical control group. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same set of differentiating factors but reports higher certainty for this result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study could not distinguish children with suicidal ideation from those who attempted suicide, with an AUROC range of 0.49 to 0.59. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study could not distinguish children with suicidal ideation from those who attempted suicide, with an area under the receiver operating characteristics curve of 0.55-0.58 for child-report and 0.49-0.53 for parent-report. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives report-specific AUROC breakdowns that refine the preprint's overall range without changing the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future research is needed to determine if the identified variables prospectively predict subsequent suicidal behavior. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The specific call for future prospective validation is not present among the publication claims provided; thus the explicit statement is not represented.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Trialstreamer has indexed 669,895 publications of randomized controlled trials (RCTs) as of May 2020. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Trialstreamer has indexed 673,191 publications of randomized controlled trials (RCTs) as of early June 2020. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication updates the indexed count and date; the core claim (index size) is the same but numerically revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The median trial sample size in the RCTs was 66. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The median trial sample size among the indexed RCTs was 66. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The median sample size is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An automated system for finding and categorizing RCTs has been developed and evaluated. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system automatically categorizes RCTs and extracts structured information from their abstracts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives more detail about what the automated system does (categorization and extraction), clarifying the preprint contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The database of structured information extracted from RCTs is made freely available for download and via a search portal. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: None of the provided publication claims explicitly restates the availability of the downloadable/searchable database, so this specific claim is not represented in the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with exemplary phenotypes are more genetically separable than those with less exemplary phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients with exemplary phenotypes are more genetically separable than those with less exemplary phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same core finding; wording shifts the label to a conclusion but content and certainty are the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best LiR exemplars had later illness onset, completely episodic clinical course, absence of rapid cycling and psychosis, and few psychiatric comorbidities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific list of clinical exemplar characteristics does not appear among the provided publication claims, so this detailed descriptive claim is not represented.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best exemplars of LiR and LiNR were genetically separable with an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.88. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best clinical exemplars of lithium response and non-response were genetically separable with an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.88. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numerical separability result for clinical exemplars with identical AUC and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Variants in the Alzheimer's amyloid secretase pathway, along with G-protein coupled receptor, muscarinic acetylcholine, and histamine H1R signaling pathways were particularly informative predictors. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Variants in the Alzheimer’s amyloid–secretase pathway, along with G-protein-coupled receptor, muscarinic acetylcholine, and histamine H1R signaling pathways were informative predictors of lithium response. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same pathway-level genetic predictors with equivalent specificity and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed radiogenomics ML model quantifies uncertainty using transductive Gaussian Processes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed radiogenomics ML model quantifies uncertainty using transductive Gaussian Processes and a dataset of 95 image-localized biopsies from 25 untreated Glioblastoma patients. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological claim but adds dataset specifics, which clarifies rather than changes the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predictive uncertainty informed the likelihood of achieving an accurate sample prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stratifying predictions based on uncertainty resulted in higher performance, with 75% accuracy in the group cohort and 83% accuracy among sample predictions with the lowest uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication operationalizes the preprint's general claim by showing that stratification by uncertainty improved performance and provides the performance numbers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieved 75% accuracy in the group cohort and 83% accuracy in sample predictions with the lowest uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stratifying predictions based on uncertainty resulted in higher performance, with 75% accuracy in the group cohort and 83% accuracy among sample predictions with the lowest uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance figures and context verbatim, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach enhances model performance and clinical interpretability in radiogenomics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This novel approach to quantify radiogenomics uncertainty enhances model performance and clinical interpretability, aiding in more reliable medical decision-making. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the original conclusion but expands its practical implications (aiding medical decision-making), which broadens scope and modestly amplifies the practical confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All 20 participants could perform monthly home-monitoring, resulting in an adherence rate of 98%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: All 20 participants could perform monthly home monitoring, achieving an adherence rate of 98% of tests. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same adherence finding with equivalent wording and numbers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There was good concordance between visual fields measured at home and in the clinic, with a correlation coefficient of r = 0.94. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There was good concordance between visual fields measured at home and in the clinic, with a correlation coefficient of r = 0.94. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adding home-monitoring data to two Standard Automated Perimetry tests reduced measurement error in 97% of eyes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adding home-monitoring data to standard automated perimetry tests reduced measurement error in 97% of eyes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same result with equivalent scope and numbers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Substantial variations in ambient illumination had no observable effect on visual field measurements. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Substantial variations in ambient illumination had no observable effect on visual field measurements. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BRCA1 gene variants with unknown clinical significance were identified from clinical sequencing data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BRCA1 gene variants with unknown clinical significance were identified from clinical sequencing data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the identification of VUS in BRCA1 from sequencing data unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An optimized random forest model scored 0.85 in area under the receiver-operating characteristic curve, outperforming other models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimized random forest model scored 0.85 in area under the receiver operating characteristic curve, outperforming other models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported model performance and relative superiority are preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimized random forest model predicted 4724 benign and 1591 pathogenic variants from the analysis of BRCA1 variants and ClinVar database. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best random forest model was applied to predict the pathogenicity of 6321 BRCA1 variants from both sequencing data and the ClinVar database. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a single aggregate count (6321) rather than the preprint's split benign/pathogenic counts and the numeric totals differ slightly, indicating a revision/aggregation of the original numbers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed machine learning models assist in the interpretation of variants of unknown significance and improve clinical diagnosis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictive pathogenic risks of BRCA1 variants of unknown significance were obtained. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports that predictive risks were obtained but stops short of claiming improved clinical diagnosis/interpretation, so the conclusion is toned down compared with the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The genetic risk score based on chromosomal-scale length variation could stratify women such that the highest 20% had a 160x risk compared to the lowest 20%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The genetic risk score based on chromosomal-scale length variation could stratify women such that the highest 20% had a 160x risk compared to the lowest 20%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this stratification result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The genetic risk score we developed had an area under the curve of the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.88. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The genetic risk score developed had an area under the curve of the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.88. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The AUC value and claim are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A genetic risk score based on chromosomal-scale length variation of germ line DNA provides an effective means of predicting whether or not a woman will develop ovarian cancer. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A genetic risk score based on chromosomal-scale length variation of germ line DNA provides an effective means of predicting whether or not a woman will develop ovarian cancer. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same overarching conclusion about predictive utility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AAS users had higher brain age gap (BAG) compared to weightlifting controls, with the strongest effects observed in frontal and insular regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AAS users had higher brain age gap (BAG) compared with weightlifting control subjects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the increased BAG finding; regional detail is slightly abbreviated but the core result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher BAG was associated with current AAS use, dependency, and a longer history of AAS use. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The higher BAG in AAS users was associated with dependency and a longer history of AAS use. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication omits the association with current use reported in the preprint, narrowing the set of associated usage characteristics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Group differences in BAG could not be explained by substance use, general cognitive abilities, or depression. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Group differences in BAG could not be explained by other substance use, general cognitive abilities, or depression. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same control analyses and conclusion about potential confounders.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Longitudinal data revealed no group difference in the rate of BAG change. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Accelerated brain aging was seen with longer AAS exposure. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's longitudinal interpretation differs from the preprint's null finding, instead emphasizing acceleration with exposure, representing a substantive revision and broader/stronger longitudinal claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that long-term high dose AAS use may have adverse effects on brain aging. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Long-term high-dose AAS use may have adverse effects on brain aging. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same cautionary conclusion about long-term high-dose AAS effects on brain aging.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presented machine learning method achieved an MAE of 0.106 ± 0.098 on the testing set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The presented method achieved a mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.106 ± 0.098 on the testing set using preoperative biometry and patient sex as predictors.
alignments.rationale: The numeric result is identical but the publication adds detail on the predictors used, so the claim is clarified rather than changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MAE of the proposed method was significantly lower than that of five existing methods (p < 0.01).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean absolute error of the proposed method was significantly lower than that of five existing methods.
alignments.rationale: The core comparative result is retained, but the p-value reported in the preprint is omitted in the publication, which slightly tempers the expressed statistical certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presented method achieved accuracy surpassing that of previously reported methods in the prediction of postoperative ACD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The presented machine learning method achieved greater accuracy than previously reported methods for the prediction of postoperative ACD.
alignments.rationale: Both statements make the same comparative accuracy claim with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increasing accuracy of postoperative ACD prediction with the presented algorithm has the potential to improve refractive outcomes in cataract surgery.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increasing accuracy of postoperative ACD prediction with the presented algorithm has the potential to improve refractive outcomes in cataract surgery.
alignments.rationale: The contribution/potential impact statement is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: When IOL power was used as an additional predictor, the method achieved an MAE of 0.105 ± 0.091.
alignments.rationale: This result about including IOL power as an additional predictor does not appear in the preprint and is newly reported in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modified SIR model demonstrates superiority over the Logistic model based on analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modified SIR model demonstrates superiority over the logistic model in predicting COVID-19 cases.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert model superiority for prediction; wording is consistent and certainty remains high.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models provide accurate predictions for up to 14 days and can assess the effectiveness of decisions made by authorities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both the modified SIR and logistic models provide accurate predictions for a period up to 14 days.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the 14-day accuracy claim but drops the explicit claim about assessing authorities' decisions and adds a limitation elsewhere, which clarifies scope and tempers confidence in policy-assessment implications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The government action of Unlock 1.0 was not a wise decision considering the nature of the pandemic.
alignments.rationale: The publication omits the evaluative policy judgment about Unlock 1.0; related discussion in the paper focuses on prediction limitations rather than making that judgment.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Municipal corporations should utilize this study to make decisions and evaluate their effectiveness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models have proven effective in predicting COVID-19 cases at both National and Municipal Corporation levels.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the prescriptive recommendation into an efficacy statement that covers both municipal and national levels, broadening the scope while removing the direct prescriptive language.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The strategy of combining impairment levels for two-class classification resulted in significantly higher sensitivities and NPVs compared to the three-class approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The strategy of combining impairment levels for two-class classification resulted in significantly higher sensitivities and negative predictive values compared to the three-class approach.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated verbatim (NPV spelled out) with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Just four neuropsychological features enabled classification sensitivity of 94.53% with the addition of four more features increasing sensitivity to 95.18%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four features enabled classification sensitivity of 94.53% with an 88.43% positive predictive value.
alignments.rationale: The publication separates the two-stage results and supplements them with PPV values; the core sensitivity figure (94.53%) matches, so the claim is clarified by additional metrics and splitting of results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The high detection rate paired with the minimal assessment time of the four identified features may act as an effective starting point when screening for cognitive impairment defined at CDR 0.5 and above.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The high detection rate paired with the minimal assessment time of the four identified features may act as an effective starting point for developing screening protocols targeting cognitive impairment defined at CDR 0.5 and above.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the intended use from a general 'starting point' to explicitly 'developing screening protocols,' clarifying the application without changing the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model outperformed benchmark models for daily prediction of the AD severity scores.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model successfully made daily predictions of the AD severity scores.
alignments.rationale: The preprint claimed explicit outperformance of benchmarks, while the publication presents successful prediction without the explicit benchmark superiority claim, representing a tempering/revision of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictive performance of AD severity scores was not improved by the addition of measured environmental factors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive performance of the model was not improved by the addition of measured environmental factors.
alignments.rationale: Both versions make the same negative finding about added environmental predictors and retain the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Any potential short-term influence of environmental exposures on AD severity scores was outweighed by the underlying persistence of preceding scores.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The potential short-term influence of environmental exposures on daily AD severity scores was outweighed by the underlying persistence of preceding scores.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this conclusion with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our data does not offer enough evidence to support a claim that AD symptoms are associated with weather or air pollutants on a short-term basis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The data does not offer enough evidence to support a claim that weather or air pollutants can make short-term predictions of AD signs.
alignments.rationale: Both statements express the same null/equivocal conclusion about short-term associations between environmental factors and AD symptoms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inferences about the magnitude of the effect of environmental factors require consideration of the time-dependence of the AD severity scores.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inferences about the magnitude of the effect of environmental factors on AD severity scores require consideration of their time-dependent dynamic nature.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same methodological caveat, restated with slightly different wording but equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Electroencephalography (EEG) in infants can predict later autism spectrum disorder (ASD) diagnoses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These results demonstrate that speech processing EEG measures can facilitate earlier identification of ASD.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's general claim that infant EEG can predict later ASD aligns with the publication's conclusion that speech-processing EEG measures can facilitate earlier identification; the publication frames this as facilitation but conveys the same core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 100% predictive diagnostic accuracy was observed at both 6-months and 12-months in infants with high familial risk for ASD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a combination of Pearson correlation feature selection and support vector machine classifier, 100% predictive diagnostic accuracy was observed at both 6 and 12 months.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the exact performance claim and adds methodological detail (feature selection and classifier), so the result is unchanged in substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predictive features selected at 6-months and 12-months differ and originate from different scalp locations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictive features differed between the models trained on 6- versus 12-month data.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the finding that features differ between ages but omits the explicit mention of differing scalp locations, so the claim is slightly clarified/streamlined.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Performance of machine learning algorithms for predicting ASD declines with increased sample size and behavioral heterogeneity at 12-months.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Diagnosis prediction accuracy substantially decreased in the larger, more behaviorally heterogeneous 12-month sample.
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the same observed decline in accuracy in the larger, more heterogeneous 12-month sample.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Speech processing EEG measures can facilitate earlier identification of ASD but require age-specific predictive models with large sample sizes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a need for age-specific predictive models with large sample sizes to develop clinically relevant classification algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the need for age-specific models and large samples (the requirement part of the preprint claim) and separately states facilitation by speech EEG, splitting the combined preprint claim into complementary publication statements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated processing of thermal images of the back can be used to detect COVID-19 with up to 92% sensitivity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel image processing algorithms achieved an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.85 in COVID-19 detection with up to 92% sensitivity.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same sensitivity and adds an AUC metric, which clarifies but does not revise the original detection claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extracted texture features of the thermal image are associated with COVID-19 disease progression and lung injury.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Thermal imaging scores were inversely correlated with clinical variables associated with COVID-19 disease progression.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the association as inverse correlations with clinical variables, clarifying the relationship described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A portable thermal camera that connects directly to smartphones can be used to detect COVID-19.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A hand-held thermal imaging device can be used to detect COVID-19.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the device as a hand-held tool suitable for detection; this rephrases the preprint's portable/smartphone-connected device claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Non-invasive thermal imaging could be used to screen for COVID-19 in out-of-hospital settings and regions with limited imaging resources.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Non-invasive thermal imaging could be used to screen for COVID-19 in out-of-hospital settings, especially in low-income regions with limited imaging resources.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the screening claim and adds emphasis on low-income regions, which clarifies rather than broadens or narrows the original recommendation.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A new point-of-care, non-contact thermal imaging tool was developed to detect COVID-19.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states development of a new point-of-care tool, a contribution not separately asserted in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differential DNA methylation is observed in undifferentiated arthritis (UA) patients compared to healthy controls.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Differential DNA methylation was found in undifferentiated arthritis (UA) patients compared to healthy controls.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A DNA methylation signature characteristic of UA cases that differentiate to rheumatoid arthritis (RA) has been identified.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A DNA methylation signature characteristic of UA cases that differentiated to rheumatoid arthritis (RA) was identified.
alignments.rationale: The identified methylation signature claim is carried over verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methylome of peripheral mononuclear cells can be used to anticipate the evolution of UA to RA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methylome of peripheral mononuclear cells can be used to anticipate the evolution of UA to RA.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this predictive result without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DNA methylation profiling provides a good predictor of UA-to-RA progression to anticipate targeted treatments and improve clinical management.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DNA methylation profiling provides a good predictor of UA-to-RA progression to anticipate targeted treatments and improve clinical management.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is the same but the publication reports a lower certainty label (medium), indicating a tempered confidence relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The actigraphy classifier achieved 95.2% sensitivity and 90.9% precision for detecting iRBD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The actigraphy classifier achieved 95.2% sensitivity and 90.9% precision for detecting iRBD.
alignments.rationale: Identical performance metrics are reported in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The questionnaire classifier achieved 90.6% accuracy and 92.7% precision, exceeding the performance of RBD-I and prodromal questionnaire alone.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The questionnaire classifier achieved 90.6% accuracy and 92.7% precision, exceeding the performance of the Innsbruck RBD Inventory and prodromal questionnaire alone.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the classifier performance, specifying the named instruments consistent with the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Concordant predictions between actigraphy and questionnaire reached 100% specificity and precision with 88.1% sensitivity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Concordant predictions between actigraphy and questionnaire reached a specificity and precision of 100% with 88.1% sensitivity.
alignments.rationale: The concordant prediction performance is reported unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Actigraphy detected iRBD with high accuracy in a mixed clinical and community cohort.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Actigraphy detected iRBD with high accuracy in a mixed clinical and community cohort.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about actigraphy performance is carried forward without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This cost-effective fully remote procedure has potential for large scale screening of iRBD in the general population.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This cost-effective fully remote procedure can be used to diagnose iRBD in specialty outpatient settings and has potential for large-scale screening of iRBD in the general population.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a use-case (diagnosis in specialty outpatient settings) while retaining the large-scale screening potential, broadening the original screening-only claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel predictive panel with four proteins was developed to predict spontaneous preterm birth.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel predictive panel with four proteins was developed to predict spontaneous preterm birth.
alignments.rationale: The contribution of the four-protein predictive panel is presented unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimal model of logistic regression achieved an AUC of 0.934 for predicting spontaneous preterm birth.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimal model of logistic regression achieved an AUC of 0.934 for predicting spontaneous preterm birth.
alignments.rationale: The reported model performance (AUC) is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher-risk subjects identified by the prediction model generally gave birth earlier than lower-risk subjects.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or restate the specific result about higher-risk subjects delivering earlier; this claim appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: First trimester modeling based on maternal serum LC-MS/MS can identify pregnant women at risk of spontaneous preterm birth.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive model can identify pregnant women at risk of spontaneous preterm birth in the first trimester.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the first-trimester identification claim in equivalent terms.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model provides additional prediction of spontaneous preterm birth in the second and third trimester with an AUC of 0.868.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces an additional claim about model performance in second/third trimesters that is not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Optimal performance for AKI prediction is achieved by using GBDT with the time-based stacking technique, resulting in an AUPRC of 65.7%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Optimal performance for AKI prediction is achieved by using GBDT with the time-based stacking technique, resulting in an AUPRC of 65.7%.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim and technique are reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Following the official KDIGO definition substantially increases the number of annotated AKI events.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Following the official KDIGO definition substantially increases the number of annotated AKI events.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is the same but the publication labels this as a medium-certainty conclusion versus high in the preprint, indicating tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GBDTs outperform LSTM models for AKI prediction in the study.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GBDT models outperform LSTM models for AKI prediction in a multi-disciplinary ICU setting.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same comparative result, specifying the study setting but not changing the outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both GBDT and LSTM models are robust across different subcohorts and exhibit no issues in gender transfer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both GBDT and LSTM models show robust performance across different subcohorts and exhibit no issues in gender transfer.
alignments.rationale: The robustness claim is maintained in the publication with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study assessed the ability of several candidate processing pipelines to identify the presence or absence of tremor in kinematic data from movement disorders patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance across the six processing algorithms was generally comparable.
alignments.rationale: Both claims describe the study's evaluation of pipeline performance; the publication frames that evaluation in terms of comparability across algorithms, making the original assessment more specific.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average F1 score across the algorithms was 0.84 ± 0.02, indicating generally comparable performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average F1 score for the processing pipelines was 0.84±0.02, indicating strong performance in identifying tremor.
alignments.rationale: The numeric result is identical but the publication emphasizes stronger positive interpretation (“strong performance”) rather than ``generally comparable'', increasing the strength of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The highest performing algorithm achieved a cross-validated F1 score of 0.87, utilizing engineered features and a modern Support Vector Machine classifier.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The second highest performing algorithm achieved a cross-validated F1 score of 0.87.
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance (0.87) is retained but the rank changed from "highest" to "second highest," which revises the original claim and narrows its scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results suggest the potential to update legacy clinical decision support systems to incorporate modern machine learning classifiers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results suggest the potential to update legacy clinical decision support systems with modern machine learning classifiers.
alignments.rationale: The substantive conclusion is the same, but the publication reports lower certainty for this conclusion (publication lists lower certainty), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified neural biomarker classified high and low depression symptom severity states with a cross-validated accuracy of 0.97.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A personalized depression neural biomarker was identified with a cross-validated accuracy of 0.97.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric performance and characterization of the biomarker with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural biomarker simultaneously tracked both weekly (slow) and daily (fast) depression symptom variation dynamics, achieving test data explained variance of 0.74 and 0.63, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identified neural biomarker simultaneously tracked both weekly and daily depression symptom variation dynamics.
alignments.rationale: The core claim that the biomarker tracked weekly and daily dynamics is preserved; the publication omits the numeric explained-variance details but does not change the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural biomarker responded to DBS frequency alterations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural biomarker responded to DBS frequency alterations and can be neurobiologically interpreted as indicating LHb excitatory and inhibitory balance changes during DBS treatment.
alignments.rationale: The publication incorporates this response claim together with an interpretation; the measured response to DBS frequency is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural biomarker can be neurobiologically interpreted as indicating changes in LHb excitatory and inhibitory balance during DBS treatment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural biomarker responded to DBS frequency alterations and can be neurobiologically interpreted as indicating LHb excitatory and inhibitory balance changes during DBS treatment.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same neurobiological interpretation, combining it with the DBS-frequency response claim but not altering its content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results hold promise to identify clinically-viable neural biomarkers to facilitate future aDBS for treating TRD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates the potential of aDBS guided by a neural biomarker to improve treatment outcomes for TRD.
alignments.rationale: Both claims express potential clinical translation to aDBS for TRD; the publication frames it as potential to improve outcomes, a slightly different emphasis but substantively aligned.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study confirmed that data could be best explained by 3 clusters of pain types.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Data could be best explained by 3 clusters of pain types.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same clustering result with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified clusters were characterized by a priori specified features and agreed with the designation of an experienced clinician with 82% accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The clusters identified aligned with the designation of an experienced clinician with 82% accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The claim about alignment with clinician designation and the 82% accuracy is repeated verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Supervised analysis highlighted features that contributed most and least to the classification of pain type.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Supervised analysis highlighted features that contributed most and least to the classification of pain type.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Probabilistic analysis reinforced the presence of mixed pain types.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Probabilistic analysis reinforced the presence of mixed pain types.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings support the foundation for further refinement of a clinical tool to discriminate between pain types.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings support the foundation for further refinement of a clinical tool to discriminate between pain types.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is carried over without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PWAS platform quantifies the impact of genetic variants on protein-coding genes’ biochemical functions using machine learning modeling and probabilistic considerations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PWAS approach quantifies the impact of genetic variants on protein-coding genes' biochemical functions using machine learning modeling and probabilistic considerations.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same methodological claim about PWAS with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PWAS Hub lists statistically significant genes associated with 99 common diseases and conditions, each with over 10,000 diagnosed individuals per phenotype.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PWAS Hub provides a user-friendly interface for exploring gene-disease associations from the UK Biobank, covering 99 common diseases and conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the Hub's user interface and dataset coverage rather than explicitly listing statistically significant genes with per-phenotype sample-size detail, narrowing the original emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analyses conducted in the PWAS Hub account for sex-based genetic effects and inheritance modes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analyses conducted in PWAS account for sex-based genetic effects, inheritance modes, and the pleiotropic nature of associated genes.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the original accounting for sex and inheritance and explicitly adds pleiotropy, broadening the analytical scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PWAS Hub indicates whether the analyzed damaged gene is associated with an increased or decreased risk for each phenotype.
alignments.rationale: This specific claim about indicating directionality of risk per gene/phenotype does not appear in the publication's listed claims, so it appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three susceptibility loci possibly associated with HBsAg seroclearance were identified: SNP rs2399971, gene LINC00578, and locus 11p15.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three susceptibility loci possibly associated with HBsAg seroclearance were identified: SNP rs2399971, gene LINC00578, and locus 11p15.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same identification of three loci unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SNP rs2399971 is a biomarker reported in the literature to be significantly associated with HBsAg seroclearance in patients who had received antiviral treatment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SNP rs2399971 is a biomarker reported in the literature to be significantly associated with HBsAg seroclearance in patients who had received antiviral treatment.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim carried into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed integrated framework demonstrates the potential to identify disease-associated susceptibility loci.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed integrated framework demonstrates the potential to identify disease-associated susceptibility loci.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion retained without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The latter two loci, gene LINC00578 and locus 11p15, are linked with diseases influenced by the presence of hepatitis B virus infection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The latter two loci, LINC00578 and locus 11p15, are linked with diseases influenced by the presence of hepatitis B virus infection.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this association claim with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Altered gene expression of factors relevant for local estrogen metabolism, preadipocyte commitment, and immune cell infiltration was found in lipedema on tissue level as well as in distinct cellular subpopulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Altered gene expression of factors relevant for local estrogen metabolism, preadipocyte commitment, and immune cell infiltration was found in lipedema on the tissue level and in distinct cellular subpopulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording and content are effectively identical between preprint and publication, preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning analysis revealed a morphological difference in the cellular junctions of endothelial cell cultures derived from healthy and lipedema individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning analysis revealed a morphological difference in the cellular junctions of endothelial cell cultures derived from healthy and lipedema individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim text and implication are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The secretome of lipedema-derived stromal vascular fraction cells significantly increases the leakiness of healthy human primary endothelial cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The secretome of lipedema-derived stromal vascular fraction cells significantly increases the leakiness of healthy human primary endothelial cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical experimental result reported in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The secretome of SVF cells creates an environment that triggers endothelial barrier dysfunction in early-stage lipedema. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The secretome of SVF cells creates an environment that triggers endothelial barrier dysfunction in early-stage lipedema. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion language is preserved verbatim between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Alterations in gene expression were detected on the cellular and/or tissue level, indicating that the choice of sample material is of high importance in elucidating lipedema. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Alterations in gene expression were detected on the cellular and/or tissue level, indicating that the choice of sample material is of high importance in elucidating lipedema. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The interpretive claim about sample material and gene-expression alterations is unchanged in content and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Chromophobe-Oncocytoma Gene Signature (COGS) can effectively differentiate between malignant chromophobe renal cancer and benign oncocytoma. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate an explicit claim about a COGS signature; this specific assertion about COGS is absent from the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The unsupervised learning methods used in the study achieved high accuracy, with UMAP showing an accuracy of 0.97 and hierarchical clustering achieving an accuracy of 1.0. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unsupervised learning with UMAP achieved an accuracy of 0.97 in identifying clusters congruent with histology. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The UMAP accuracy statement is preserved; the publication emphasizes congruence with histology and does not restate hierarchical clustering accuracy explicitly, so wording is slightly clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The supervised UMAP model classified all cases correctly, demonstrating a sensitivity and specificity of 1. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Supervised UMAP demonstrated perfect classification of all cases with sensitivity, specificity, and accuracy all equal to 1. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The supervised UMAP perfect-classification claim is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The random forest model also performed well, with a sensitivity of 0.84, specificity of 1, and accuracy of 1. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No published claim reports the random forest performance metrics; the specific model results appear omitted in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This diagnostic pipeline can be utilized by pathologists in clinical settings to differentiate between chRCC and RO. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed classification pipeline can be utilized as a clinical tool by pathologists to differentiate between malignant chromophobe renal cancer and benign oncocytoma. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The core claim that the pipeline is usable clinically is preserved, but publication presents it with lower certainty and frames it more cautiously.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Pathologists were able to correctly classify malignant chromophobe renal cancer and benign oncocytoma with an accuracy of 91.8% using histology and immunohistochemistry-based methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This clinical-pathologist result is present in the publication but was not stated in the preprint, so it is an added published claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper assesses the correlation between various news and tweets collected during the COVID-19 pandemic on community functionality and resilience. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper assesses the correlation between various news and tweets collected during the COVID-19 pandemic on community functionality and resilience. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The high-level assessment claim is identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Community resilience is quantified based on two key factors: community wellbeing and resource distribution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Community resilience is quantified based on two key factors: community wellbeing and resource distribution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Quantification approach and factors are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study uses fact-checking organizations to classify news as real, mixed, or fake, and machine learning algorithms to classify tweets as real or fake. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study uses fact-checking organizations to classify news as real, mixed, or fake, and machine learning algorithms to classify tweets as real or fake. (certainty=high, type=method)
alignments.rationale: The methodological description is the same; only the label of the claim changed from "contribution" to "method."

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extent to which community resilience measured from news articles can reflect the actual state of community resilience measured from tweets is analyzed. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extent to which community resilience measured from news articles can reflect the actual state of community resilience measured from tweets is analyzed. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Content of the analytic aim is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Risk factors that predict mortality in the overall cohort of COVID-19 ARDS patients do not necessarily predict mortality in phenoclusters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The risk factors predicting mortality in the overall cohort of COVID-19 ARDS may not necessarily apply to specific phenoclusters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The statement about non-equivalence of overall vs phenocluster risk factors is maintained with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some risk factors increase the risk of hospital mortality in some phenoclusters, but decrease mortality in others. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some risk factors increase the risk of hospital mortality in some phenoclusters but decrease mortality in others. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact correspondence in content and framing between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Phenocluster-specific risk factors would not have been observed with a single predictive model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication does not explicitly state this assertion about single-model inability to reveal phenocluster-specific factors, so the specific methodological claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Heterogeneity in phenoclusters of COVID-19 ARDS as well as drivers of mortality may partially explain challenges in finding effective treatments when applied to all patients with ARDS. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Heterogeneity in phenoclusters of COVID-19 ARDS may partially explain challenges in finding effective treatments for all patients with ARDS. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The explanatory conclusion about heterogeneity and treatment challenges is preserved with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The literature on remote measuring technologies for depression is characterized by small sample sizes, short follow-up duration, and great variability in quality of reporting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The literature is characterised by small sample sizes, short follow-up duration and great variability in the quality of reporting, limiting the interpretability of pooled results. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Content about sample size, follow-up, and reporting variability is preserved; the publication frames it as a limitation limiting pooled interpretability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bivariate analyses show some consistency in statistically significant associations between depression and digital features from sleep, physical activity, location, and phone use data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bivariate analyses show consistency in statistically significant associations between depression and digital features from sleep, physical activity, location, and phone use data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The same associations are reported; the publication frames the consistency more strongly (highercertainty), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Regression and classification machine learning models found predictive value of aggregated features related to depression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models found the predictive value of aggregated features. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The finding is present in both, but the publication expresses it with lower stated certainty, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are threats to reproducibility and transparency due to failure to provide comprehensive descriptions of recruitment strategies, sample information, feature construction, and handling of missing data. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Improvements in reporting standards are recommended to enhance generalisability and reproducibility in future studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's specific reporting shortcomings are reframed in the publication as recommendations to improve reporting; the issue is clarified into actionable guidance and expressed with lower certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified leading digital signals for depression. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This explicit contribution (identifying leading digital signals) appears in the publication but was not stated as a distinct claim in the preprint, so it is an added published claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a significant metabolic perturbation in the serum of ROP cases compared to non-ROP controls, characterized by increased levels of lipid, nucleotide, and carbohydrate metabolites, and decreased levels of peptides. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a significant metabolic perturbation in the plasma of ROP cases compared with non-ROP controls, characterized by increased levels of lipids, nucleotides, and carbohydrate metabolites, and lower levels of peptides. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content and certainty are effectively the same aside from 'serum' versus 'plasma' wording; the metabolic perturbation and directional changes match directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning identified a cluster of metabolic pathways, including glycometabolism, redox homeostasis, lipid metabolism, and the arginine pathway, that are strongly related to the development of ROP. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning revealed that specific metabolic pathways, including glycometabolism, redox homeostasis, lipid metabolism, and the arginine pathway, are strongly correlated with the development of ROP. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same machine-learning-identified pathways with equivalent certainty and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The severity of ROP is related to the levels of creatinine and ribitol. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The severity of ROP is associated with the levels of creatinine and ribitol, and overactivity of aerobic glycolysis and lipid metabolism is noted in the metabolic profile of ROP. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the creatinine/ribitol association but adds additional metabolic features (aerobic glycolysis and lipid metabolism), expanding the reported result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study suggests a strong link between metabolic profiling and retinal neovascularization during ROP pathogenesis. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results suggest a strong correlation between metabolic profiling and retinal neovascularization in ROP pathogenesis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is the same but the publication frames it with somewhat stronger certainty ('medium' vs 'low'), effectively amplifying confidence while keeping the claim content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings provide insight into identifying novel metabolic biomarkers for ROP diagnosis and prevention. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings provide insight into the identification of novel metabolic biomarkers for the diagnosis and prevention of ROP, but the clinical significance requires further validation. (certainty=low, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the biomarker insight claim but adds a caveat calling for further validation and lowers the stated certainty, tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We quantified 7,961 proteins using the HSPC biopsies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the preprint's explicit claim about quantifying 7,961 proteins; that specific enumeration is absent in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A total of 306 proteins were differentially expressed between patients with a long- or short-term progression to CRPC. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A total of 251 proteins showed differential expression between patients with a long- or short-term progression to CRPC. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core claim (differentially expressed proteins between progression groups) remains but the reported number changed from 306 to 251, a substantive revision narrowing the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a random forest model, we identified ten proteins that significantly discriminated long-from short-term cases, which were used to classify PCa patients with an 86% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Seven proteins were identified that significantly discriminated long- from short-term progression patients, achieving an area under the curve of 0.873. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a smaller panel (seven vs ten) and uses AUC rather than percent accuracy, representing a narrowed and revised model description though the overall discriminative claim persists.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A nomogram model using clinical parameters and identified proteins was generated for stratifying patients into groups with significant progression disparities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A nomogram model using the Gleason sum and two proteins (BGN and MAPK11) was generated for stratifying patients into groups with significant progression differences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both describe a nomogram for stratification, but the publication specifies fewer inputs (Gleason + two proteins), narrowing and concretizing the preprint's broader description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These tools may aid clinicians in predicting the progression of patients, guiding individualized clinical management and decisions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning and nomogram models may aid clinicians in predicting the progression of patients and guiding individualized clinical management. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about potential clinical utility is preserved with similar framing and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The identified proteins are associated with fast progression to CRPC and an unfavorable prognosis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This publication-level statement about prognosis is not present in the preprint claims and appears as an addition highlighting clinical associations of the identified proteins.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 110 bivariate ex-miRNA biomarkers passed both discovery and verification criteria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extracellular miRNA biomarkers can be identified for early diagnosis and prognosis of preeclampsia. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's specific numeric result (110 bivariate biomarkers) is not echoed; the publication presents a broader concluding claim that such biomarkers can be identified for diagnosis/prognosis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three bivariate miRNA biomarkers differentiated cases from controls with a sensitivity of 93% and a positive predictive value of 55%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three bivariate miRNA biomarkers were identified that distinguished between preeclampsia cases of different severity and differentiated cases from controls with a sensitivity of 93% and specificity of 79%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The three-biomarker result is preserved (sensitivity 93%) but the publication reports specificity (79%) rather than PPV (55%) and adds severity differentiation; metrics were revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In an independent validation cohort, these three biomarkers had a sensitivity of 91% and a positive predictive value of 85%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In a small independent validation cohort, the identified ex-miRNA biomarkers had a sensitivity of 91% and specificity of 57%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The validation sensitivity is the same but the performance metric reported differs (PPV in preprint vs specificity in publication) and specificity values differ, so the claim was revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bivariate ex-miRNA biomarkers can distinguish between different subtypes of hypertensive disorders in pregnancy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three bivariate miRNA biomarkers were identified that distinguished between preeclampsia cases of different severity and differentiated cases from controls with a sensitivity of 93% and specificity of 79%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's suggestion that biomarkers can separate subtypes is made concrete in the publication as distinguishing severity of preeclampsia, with higher stated certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining the ex-miRNA biomarkers with the established sFlt1:PlGF protein biomarker ratio performed better than either set of biomarkers alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The combination with the sFlt1:PlGF ratio is a result presented only in the publication and does not have a counterpart in the preprint claims, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Septic cardiomyopathy (SCM) and sepsis-related cardiogenic shock (SeRCS) affect a minority of patients with septic shock. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Septic cardiomyopathy (SCM) and sepsis-related cardiogenic shock (SeRCS) affect a minority of patients with septic shock. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A prior history of cardiac dysfunction is the strongest predictor for developing SCM and SeRCS in patients with septic shock. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A prior history of cardiac dysfunction is the strongest predictor for developing SCM and SeRCS in patients with septic shock. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The predictive factor and wording are preserved exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mortality did not significantly differ among patients with septic shock, SCM, and SeRCS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mortality did not significantly differ among patients with septic shock, SCM, and SeRCS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No change in claim content or certainty between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SCM and SeRCS disproportionately affect individuals with a history of cardiac disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SCM and SeRCS disproportionately affect individuals with a history of cardiac disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion and certainty remain the same in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Additional work is needed to define further subtypes and treatment options for patients with SCM and SeRCS. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Additional work is needed to define further subtypes and treatment options for patients with SCM and SeRCS. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation for further work is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our machine learning pipeline identified differentially methylated regions in HCC that benchmarked positively against independent methylation signatures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning pipeline identified differentially methylated regions in HCC that benchmarked positively against independent methylation signatures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This is an essentially identical restatement of the pipeline result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining our signature of 38 DNA methylation regions, we derived a HCC detection score which confirmed the utility of our approach by identifying in an independent dataset 96% of HCC tissue samples with a precision of 98%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The HCC detection score derived from a signature of 38 DNA methylation regions identified 96% of HCC tissue samples with a precision of 98%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The detection score performance and signature size are reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our risk score could identify cell-free DNA samples from patients with other tumours, including colorectal cancer. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The risk score could identify cell-free DNA samples from patients with other tumours, including colorectal cancer. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claim about cross-tumour cfDNA identification with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a comprehensive HCC DNA methylation fingerprint and an associated risk score for the early diagnosis and early relapse detection of HCC from liquid biopsies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A comprehensive HCC DNA methylation fingerprint and an associated risk score for detection of HCC from tissue and liquid biopsies is proposed. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both propose a comprehensive fingerprint and risk score, but the publication frames it as detection from tissue and liquid biopsies without the explicit 'early relapse detection' emphasis, narrowing scope slightly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Early immune signatures, including plasma levels of RIG-I and CCR2 ligands, are associated with control of oropharyngeal viral load, symptom severity, and immune memory in COVID-19 patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Early immune signatures, including plasma RIG-I levels and early IFN signaling, are associated with subsequent disease progression and control of viral shedding in COVID-19 patients.
alignments.rationale: Both claims link early immune signatures (including RIG-I) to viral control/disease course, but the publication emphasizes IFN signaling rather than CCR2 ligands and frames outcomes as disease progression/viral shedding rather than explicitly listing symptom severity and immune memory, thus clarifying and narrowing the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individuals receiving the BNT162b2 vaccine exhibit early immune trajectories similar to those observed in a natural infection cohort.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several biomarkers for immunological outcomes are shared between individuals receiving the BNT162b2 vaccine and COVID-19 patients.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports shared biomarkers rather than asserting broadly similar early immune trajectories; this is a more specific, somewhat tempered characterization of the vaccine–infection similarity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models using 8-10 plasma protein markers measured early in the infection can accurately predict symptom severity, T cell memory, and antibody response post-infection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine-learning models using 2-7 plasma protein markers measured early in the course of infection can accurately predict disease progression and immune responses in COVID-19 patients.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports model specifications and predicted outcomes that differ (fewer markers, slightly different outcome wording — 'disease progression' and 'immune responses'), indicating a substantive revision of the original model description.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Early immune signatures following infection can accurately predict clinical and immunological outcomes in outpatients with COVID-19.
alignments.rationale: This publication-level concluding statement generalizes predictive capacity of early immune signatures beyond the specific markers/outcomes listed in the preprint and appears as an added, broader concluding claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified 164 out of 272 variants showing identical effect direction to previously reported genome-significant findings in Europeans.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified 164 out of 272 variants showing identical effect direction to previously reported genome-significant findings in Europeans.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is verbatim and unchanged from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance metric of the PRS models was AUC=0.68, which is comparable to previously published PRS models obtained with larger datasets including clinical biomarkers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance metric of the PRS models was AUC = 0.68, which is comparable to previously published PRS models obtained with larger datasets including clinical biomarkers.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim with no substantive change.
