alignments.preprint_claim_text: DTC platforms have the potential of inverting research models of genome sequencing and phenotypic data acquisition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DTC platforms have the potential of inverting research models of genome sequencing and phenotypic data acquisition.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed predictive tools for both phenotypes trained with heterogeneous genotypic and phenotypic data generated outside of the clinical environment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed predictive tools for both phenotypes trained with heterogeneous genotypic and phenotypic data generated outside of the clinical environment.
alignments.rationale: Claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is possible to leverage DTC genetic repositories to identify individuals at risk of debilitating diseases based on their unique genetic landscape.
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is possible to leverage DTC genetic repositories to identify individuals at risk of debilitating diseases based on their unique genetic landscape.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this conclusion unchanged from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The genes ADAP2, KLRC1, MIR21, PDGFD, and CD14 were identified as the most important signatures for the classification model in myocardial infarction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The genes ADAP2, KLRC1, MIR21, PDGFD, and CD14 were identified as the most important signatures for the classification model in myocardial infarction.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the gene-signature result exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classifier output quality was equal to 0.911 by Receiver Operating Characteristic metric on test data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classifier output quality was equal to 0.911 by Receiver Operating Characteristic metric on test data.
alignments.rationale: Identical performance claim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Identified myocardial infarction-associated signatures can be further assisted in myocardial infarction diagnosis and/or prognosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identified MI-associated signatures can be further assisted in MI diagnosis and/or prognosis.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about potential diagnostic/prognostic utility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study presents a pipeline for collapsing the list of differentially expressed genes to define disease-associated diagnostic signatures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study presents a pipeline for collapsing the list of differentially expressed genes to define disease-associated diagnostic signatures.
alignments.rationale: Claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Circulating molecular lipid levels are related to adverse clinical outcomes in individuals at clinical high risk for psychosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Blood lipidomic measures may be useful in predicting which CHR individuals are most likely to develop psychosis.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the relationship as potential predictive utility for psychosis specifically, which narrows and tempers the broader statement that lipid levels relate to adverse clinical outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: At baseline, compared to healthy controls, clinical high risk subjects had higher levels of triacylglycerols with a low acyl carbon number and a double bond count.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CHR subjects had higher levels of triacylglycerols with a low acyl carbon number and a double bond count compared to control subjects.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the baseline triacylglycerol finding without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clinical high risk subjects who subsequently developed psychosis were distinguished from those that did not based on their lipid profile at baseline, achieving an AUC of 0.81.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A lipid profile at baseline can distinguish CHR subjects who subsequently developed psychosis from those who did not, with an area under the receiver operating curve of 0.81.
alignments.rationale: The classification performance claim (AUC=0.81) is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clinical high risk subjects who became psychotic had lower levels of ether phospholipids than those who did not.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CHR subjects who became psychotic had lower levels of ether phospholipids than CHR individuals who did not.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result on ether phospholipid levels.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lipidomic abnormalities predate the onset of psychosis and may be useful in predicting which clinical high risk individuals are most likely to develop psychosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lipidomic abnormalities predate the onset of psychosis in CHR individuals.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the 'predate onset' assertion but omits the explicit predictive-utility framing present in the preprint, effectively narrowing that combined conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gut microbiome sequencing has shown promise as a predictive biomarker for liver disease classification and severity grading.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gut microbiome has potential as a predictive biomarker for liver disease risk prediction.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the gut microbiome's predictive potential for liver disease; the publication language is slightly more cautious, lowering the expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Microbiome augmentation of conventional risk factor models using gradient boosted classifiers significantly improved performance for predicting incident liver disease and alcoholic liver disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Microbiome augmentation of conventional risk factors using machine learning significantly improved predictive performance for incident liver disease.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the improvement for incident liver disease generally and omits the explicit mention of alcoholic liver disease and the specific algorithm phrasing, narrowing and slightly rephrasing the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Disease-free survival analysis demonstrated significantly improved stratification using microbiome-augmented risk models compared to conventional risk factors alone.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Disease-free survival analysis showed significantly improved stratification using microbiome-augmented models.
alignments.rationale: The survival analysis result is preserved in the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A wide range of bacterial taxa associated with hepatic function and disease were identified as predictive microbial signatures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study revealed previously unknown microbial taxa associated with liver disease and those linked to hepatic function.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes discovery of previously unknown taxa, a clarified phrasing of the preprint's identification of a wide range of predictive taxa.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study supports the potential clinical validity of gut metagenomic sequencing to complement conventional risk factors for liver disease risk prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study supports the clinical validity of gut metagenomic sequencing to complement conventional risk factors for liver disease prediction.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the conclusion supporting clinical validity with only minor wording adjustments.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The unadjusted 30-day postoperative mortality rate associated with acute kidney injury requiring renal replacement therapy was 37.5%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The unadjusted 30-day postoperative mortality rate associated with AKI-dialysis was 37.5%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The two statements are numerically and conceptually identical, only differing in terminology (AKI-dialysis vs renal replacement therapy).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The renal risk prediction model demonstrated high AUC for postoperative acute kidney injury requiring dialysis, with values of 0.89 in the training cohort and 0.90 in the test cohort. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AKI risk prediction model had high area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC; training cohort: 0.89, test cohort: 0.90) for postoperative AKI-dialysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same AUC values and conclusion about model performance using equivalent phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This model provides a clinically useful bedside predictive tool for postoperative acute kidney injury requiring dialysis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This model provides a clinically useful bedside predictive tool for postoperative AKI requiring dialysis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same conclusion about clinical utility with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: InpherNet correctly ranks the causative gene among its top 5 candidates in 68% of the cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: InpherNet correctly ranks the causative gene top 1 or top 1-5 in roughly twice as many cases as seven comparable tools. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both describe high top-5 ranking performance; the publication frames it comparatively (twice as many cases) rather than giving the exact 68% figure, clarifying relative performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: InpherNet outperforms comparable tools, achieving a correct ranking rate of 68% compared to 9-44% for Phevor, Phive, and hiPhive. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: InpherNet improves the state of the art in considering candidate gene neighbors to accelerate monogenic diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint gives specific comparative percentages; the publication places the result in a broader, more general claim about improving the state of the art, which is a reframing and less numerically specific (tempering numeric confidence).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: InpherNet can prioritize potentially novel disease genes and help reveal known disease genes with missing or partial annotations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: InpherNet can propose novel pathogenic genes and reveal known pathogenic genes whose diagnosed patient-based annotation is missing or partial. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same capability with slightly stronger wording and certainty (novel/pathogenic and higher certainty), clarifying and amplifying the preprint conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Geriatric mild TBI can lead to AD-like alteration of resting-state activity in the default mode network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Geriatric mild TBI can lead to AD-like alteration of resting-state activity in the default mode network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same finding verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extent of AD-like, post-traumatic degradation in the default mode network can be accurately prognosticated based on the acute cognitive deficits of geriatric mTBI patients with cerebral microbleeds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extent of AD-like, posttraumatic degradation in the default mode network can be accurately prognosticated based on the acute cognitive deficits of geriatric mTBI patients with cerebral microbleeds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the prognostic association identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a predictive physiological association between geriatric mTBI, cognitive impairment, and AD-like functional degradation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a predictive physiological association between geriatric mTBI, cognitive impairment, and AD-like functional degradation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is reproduced without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The association of geriatric mTBI with AD-like changes in functional brain connectivity can be observed as early as ∼6 months post-injury. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The association of geriatric mTBI with AD-like changes in functional brain connectivity as early as ~6 months post-injury has substantial implications for public health. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the timing claim but embeds it within a statement emphasizing public health implications, clarifying the consequence of the early observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings have substantial implications for public health due to the high prevalence of TBI in the elderly. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The association of geriatric mTBI with AD-like changes in functional brain connectivity as early as ~6 months post-injury has substantial implications for public health. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both emphasize public health implications; the publication ties that implication specifically to the early post-injury association, clarifying the basis for the public-health claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified 38 probes at genome-wide significance related to blood DNA methylation in sporadic Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease (sCJD). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified 38 probes at genome-wide significance related to sporadic Creutzfeldt–Jakob disease (sCJD). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric finding and context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nine of the identified probes were successfully replicated in an independent case-control cohort. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nine of the identified probes were successfully replicated in an independent case–control cohort. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Replication result is restated identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The blood-based DNA methylation signal was found to be tissue- and disease-specific. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The blood-based DNA methylation signal was tissue- and disease-specific, remaining unchanged in other neurodegenerative conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds specificity that the signal remains unchanged in other neurodegenerative conditions, clarifying and slightly narrowing the original claim about specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning algorithms using blood DNA methylation profiles accurately distinguished sCJD patients from controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning algorithms using blood DNA methylation profiles accurately distinguished sCJD patients from controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the machine-learning classification result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified methylation sites associated with prolonged survival in sCJD patients. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified DNA methylation sites associated with prolonged survival in sCJD patients. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same finding about methylation sites linked to survival.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Vocal markers including speech prevalence, vocal jitter, fundamental frequency, and vocal intensity demonstrated specificity as markers of negative symptom severity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Vocal markers including speech prevalence, vocal jitter, fundamental frequency, and vocal intensity demonstrated specificity as markers of negative symptom severity in schizophrenia. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same vocal marker findings, adding the clinical population explicitly (schizophrenia) which is consistent with the study context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Measurement of facial expressivity demonstrated itself as a robust marker of overall schizophrenia severity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Measurement of facial expressivity demonstrated itself as a robust marker of overall schizophrenia symptom severity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the facial expressivity finding with equivalent meaning and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Established facial and vocal measurements, collected remotely in schizophrenia patients via smartphones in response to automated task prompts, demonstrated accuracy as markers of schizophrenia severity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Established facial and vocal measurements, collected remotely in schizophrenia patients via smartphones, demonstrated accuracy as markers of schizophrenia symptom severity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion about remote smartphone-collected facial and vocal measures as accurate markers of symptom severity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An XGboost classifier yielded the best performance on risk stratification with an F1 score of 0.81. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim reports model type or F1 performance for XGBoost; the model-performance claim from the preprint does not appear in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A logistic regression model yielded the best performance on mortality prediction with an F1 score of 0.71. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific logistic regression model or F1 score for mortality prediction, so this specific model-performance claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Significant biomarkers for predicting risk and mortality were identified. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Blood parameters, diabetes, co-morbidity, and SpO2 levels are important risk stratification features for COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives specific important blood and clinical features that operationalize the preprint's general claim about significant biomarkers for risk/mortality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The comparison indicated a higher survival rate in the Delhi cohort compared to the Wuhan cohort, despite similar parameters. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Indian patients with extreme blood parameters indicated a higher survival rate compared to Wuhan patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the higher-survival-in-India conclusion but specifies it pertains to patients with extreme blood parameters, narrowing and clarifying the original comparison.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study helps in identifying the high-risk patient population and suggests treatment protocols that may be useful in countries with high mortality rates. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not present the broad recommendation about treatment protocols or international applicability as stated in the preprint; instead it reports more specific observational findings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis of 13,190 adult patients revealed that 2,875 died from COVID-19 infection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's highlighted claims focus on the prediction model and performance rather than reporting the specific cohort death count in the provided claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: XGBoost identified O2 saturation, blood pressure, pulse rate, respiratory rate, age, and BUN as significant predictors of inpatient mortality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A clinical prediction model using eight readily available variables can predict in-hospital mortality for COVID-19 patients with an AUC of 94%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same idea as an eight-variable clinical prediction model; it does not contradict the listed predictors but presents them as part of a compact, validated model.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A score of 0 indicates a 0.8% risk of dying, while a score of 12 or more indicates a 98% risk of dying. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model classified 5,677 patients as low risk with a 0.8% risk of dying and 674 patients as high risk with a 97.6% risk of dying. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports equivalent risk estimates and operationalizes the score into patient counts and a slightly refined high-risk percentage (97.6% vs ~98%), clarifying and quantifying the preprint's claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed score can be used to triage patients to hospitalization and intensive care based on their risk of mortality. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed risk calculator can assist in prioritizing admissions and intensive care for hospitalized COVID-19 patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements express the same intended use of the score/risk calculator for prioritizing admissions and ICU resources.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed machine-learning model for COVID-19 detection achieved an area under the curve (AUC) of 81.6% when tested on all individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed machine-learning model for COVID-19 detection achieved an area under the curve (AUC) of 81.6% when evaluated on all individuals in the dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same overall AUC result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model obtained an AUC of 72.8% when tested on individuals who did not report any symptoms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model obtained an AUC of 72.8% when evaluated only on individuals who did not report any symptoms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same asymptomatic-subgroup AUC result with equivalent detail and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model could reach an AUC of 79.5% using only information collected before the testing episode. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: When considering only information collected before the testing episode, the model reached a high AUC of 79.5%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the identical pre-test information AUC result with matching wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ability to predict early outcomes of COVID-19 tests is pivotal for breaking transmission chains. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ability to predict early outcomes of COVID-19 tests is pivotal for breaking transmission chains. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conceptual conclusion about early prediction and breaking chains is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings can be utilized for a more efficient testing policy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can be used for a more efficient testing policy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication state the practical application to testing policy equivalently.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The smartphone Neurological Function Tests Suite (NeuFun-TS) has been developed and its psychometric properties are being systematically evaluated. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The smartphone Neurological Function Tests Suite (NeuFun-TS) has been developed and its psychometric properties are being systematically evaluated. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same development and evaluation objective with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 'Spiral tracing' test, which is the most complex in the NeuFun-TS, shows susceptibility to intra-individual variations, decreasing its reproducibility and clinical utility. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 'Spiral tracing' test within the NeuFun-TS is complex and shows susceptibility to intra-individual variations, which decreases its reproducibility and clinical utility. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this specific result about the Spiral tracing test with equivalent emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simple tests that reproducibly measure single functions are preferable in app design to increase sensitivity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simple tests that measure single functions and can be aggregated to increase sensitivity are preferable in app design. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the clarification that single-function tests can be aggregated to increase sensitivity, refining the preprint's recommendation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The digital intervention resulted in a large clinically meaningful reduction in anxiety symptoms, with a change on GAD-7 of –7.4 for the per-protocol group and –5.4 for the intention-to-treat group. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The digital program achieved a large, clinically meaningful reduction in anxiety symptoms, with a mean GAD-7 change of -7.4 in the per-protocol group. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the per-protocol -7.4 result but omits the ITT -5.4 value reported in the preprint, narrowing the reported summary of outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of the digital intervention was statistically superior to the waiting control group and non-inferior to human-delivered care. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The digital program was statistically superior to the waiting control group and noninferior to both face-to-face and typed cognitive behavioral therapy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves superiority and noninferiority claims but broadens the comparator detail (explicitly naming face-to-face and typed CBT), clarifying and extending the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The digital intervention achieved clinical outcomes comparable to human-delivered care while significantly reducing the required clinician time by up to 8 times. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The digital program significantly reduced the required clinician time by up to 8 times compared with global care estimates. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the same magnitude of clinician time reduction; the publication frames the reduction relative to global care estimates but retains the core time-savings claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that technology can scale effective evidence-based mental healthcare and address unmet needs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's highlighted claims focus on outcomes, noninferiority, time savings, and engagement; the broader scaling/healthcare-systems claim from the preprint is not explicitly stated among the publication claims provided.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The COVID-19 pandemic has been termed as the most consequential global crisis after the World Wars. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The COVID-19 pandemic has been termed as the most consequential global crisis since the World Wars. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Wording changed minimally ('after' -> 'since') but the claim content and certainty remain the same, so it is a clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Non-pharmaceutical measures like social distancing and personal hygiene are the first line of defense against the spread of COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Non-pharmaceutical measures like social distancing and personal hygiene are the first line of defense against the spread of COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Open source data is necessary to promote the extension, validation, and collaboration of scientific efforts in the fight against COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Open source data is necessary to promote the extension, validation, and collaboration of scientific efforts in the fight against COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same claim with no substantive change in meaning or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The survey categorizes open source efforts related to COVID-19 into diagnosis, case reporting, emotional analysis, and knowledge-based discovery. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Efforts to combat COVID-19 include computer-aided digital technologies for diagnosis, prevention, and estimation. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the categories toward computer-aided digital technologies (diagnosis, prevention, estimation) rather than the preprint's four-category taxonomy, representing a substantive revision and narrower focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future research directions for data-driven COVID-19 research are debated in the article. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Future research directions for data-driven COVID-19 research are debated in the article. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim about discussing future research directions is preserved verbatim in the publication with equivalent framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrated that the three CAD systems had broadly similar diagnostic accuracy with regard to TB screening. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrated that the three CAD systems had broadly similar diagnostic accuracy with regard to TB screening and comparable accuracy to an expert radiologist against MRS. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds comparison to an expert radiologist (against MRS), expanding the original claim to include an additional benchmark.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lunit performed better than both qXR and CAD4TB against microbiological reference standards. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lunit performed better than both qXR and CAD4TB against MRS, and CAD4TB and Lunit better than qXR against RadRS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core finding about Lunit but expands it with additional subgroup comparisons (RadRS), so the result is clarified and broadened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: At a set specificity of 70%, Lunit had the highest sensitivity of 54.5%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific numeric threshold and sensitivity value reported in the preprint do not appear among the publication's summarized claims, indicating the numeric detail was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CAD systems performed comparable to the sensitivity and, except CAD4TB, to the specificity of expert radiologists. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrated that the three CAD systems had broadly similar diagnostic accuracy with regard to TB screening and comparable accuracy to an expert radiologist against MRS. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes comparability to an expert radiologist (against MRS) and omits the preprint's specific exception about CAD4TB's specificity, so the nuance was revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings suggest that CAD systems could be a useful tool for TB screening programs in remote, high TB prevalent places. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings suggest that CAD systems could be a useful tool for TB screening programs in remote, high TB prevalent places where access to expert radiologists may be limited. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the specific context 'where access to expert radiologists may be limited,' narrowing the original recommendation while retaining the conclusion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the CADs can be impacted by characteristics of subgroup of population. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This limitation about subgroup effects is present in the publication but has no corresponding preprint claim, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The main limitation was that the study relied on retrospective data and MRS was not routinely done in individuals with a low suspicion of TB and a normal CXR. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This specific limitation appears in the publication but was not stated as a claim in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Aggregate segmentations generated from multiple non-experts can meet or exceed recognized expert agreement. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multiple nonexpert-generated consensus ROIs met or exceeded expert-derived acceptability thresholds. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about consensus segmentations meeting or exceeding expert thresholds.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Dice Similarity Coefficient for STAPLE non-expert versus STAPLE expert was higher than the comparator expert interobserver Dice Similarity Coefficient for most regions of interest. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Dice similarity coefficient (DSC) values for STAPLE nonexpert versus STAPLE expert were higher than comparator expert interobserver DSC for most regions of interest (ROIs). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative result about DSC comparisons across ROIs without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The minimum number of non-expert segmentations needed to achieve expert acceptability criteria ranged from 2 to 5 depending on the region of interest. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The minimum number of nonexpert segmentations needed for a consensus ROI to achieve expert acceptability criteria ranged between 2 and 4 for breast, 3 and 5 for sarcoma, 3 and 5 for head and neck, 3 and 5 for gynecologic, and 3 for gastrointestinal cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides more granular, ROI-specific ranges (and raises certainty), clarifying and refining the preprint's broader 2–5 range.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Consensus segmentations generated by 5 non-experts can approximate expert performance for most regions of interest. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Five nonexperts could potentially generate consensus segmentations for most ROIs with performance approximating experts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the original conclusion about five nonexperts approximating expert performance for most ROIs with the same framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most of ChatGPT’s responses were rated as satisfactory or better. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most of ChatGPT's responses to questions on tinnitus were rated as satisfactory or better. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the central result about response ratings being satisfactory or better, only specifying the domain (tinnitus) already implicit in the study.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: After 3 months, the responses from ChatGPT generally improved. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Over the first 3 months, the ratings of ChatGPT's responses generally improved. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same temporal improvement over the first three months with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No solid references to sources of information were ever supplied by ChatGPT. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's summarized claims do not include the specific observation about lack of solid references, so this preprint claim appears omitted in the published summary.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some mistakes in ChatGPT’s responses could be seen as somewhat misleading. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some mistakes in ChatGPT's responses could be seen as somewhat misleading. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the limitation about misleading mistakes with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT shows great potential if further developed by experts in specific areas. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT shows great potential if further developed by experts in specific areas. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same concluding statement about potential and need for domain experts.

alignments.pub_claim_text: There was no more significant improvement in response ratings at 6 months. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The observation about ratings plateauing at six months appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint, so it is an added published claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified 26 AI-based products that met our inclusion criteria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 24 out of 26 AI-based products for digital pathology received regulatory approval via the self-certification route as General in vitro diagnostic medical devices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication builds on the preprint's identified set of 26 products by adding regulatory approval details for 24 of them, clarifying and expanding the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The majority (73%) of the identified products were focused on breast pathology or uropathology. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's summarized claims do not include the distribution statistic about focus on breast or uropathology, so this specific claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Only 10 of the products (38%) were associated with peer-reviewed scientific publications describing their development and internal validation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Only 38% of the AI-based products had peer-reviewed internal validation studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same statistic about 38% having peer-reviewed internal validation studies, matching the preprint's numeric claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The availability of public information on new products for digital pathology is struggling to keep up with the rapid pace of development. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An online register was developed to support transparency and provide an accessible resource on novel devices. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts from diagnosing a transparency problem to describing an action taken (developing a register) to improve transparency; this revises the original claim's tone and emphasis.

alignments.pub_claim_text: 42% of the AI-based products had peer-reviewed external validation studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the percentage with external validation studies, which was not present among the preprint claims, making this an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new stochastic SHARUCD model framework was able to describe the disease incidence data provided by the Basque Health Services. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Short and longer-term predictions from the model were tested with good results adjusted to the available epidemiological data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's statement about describing incidence data into tested model predictions adjusted to available data, which clarifies and narrows the original claim and reduces certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model predicted the course of the epidemic and provided important projections on the national health system necessities during increased population demand on hospital admissions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new stochastic SHARUCD model framework was able to predict the course of the epidemic and provide important projections on the national health system’s necessities during increased population demand on hospital admissions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this prediction-and-projection claim verbatim with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The partial lockdown measures were effective and sufficient to slow down disease transmission in the Basque Country. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The partial lockdown measures were effective and sufficient to slow down disease transmission in the Basque Country. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same conclusion about the effectiveness of partial lockdown measures with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reproduction ratio r is estimated to be below the threshold behavior of r = 1, indicating that new cases are decelerating. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding reproduction-number statement appears in the publication claims, so this specific result is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework is being used to monitor disease transmission as the country lock-down is gradually lifted. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis of growth rates from the data led to improved model versions that describe the response to control measures after the exponential phase. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes development of improved model versions describing responses to control measures rather than stating active monitoring during lockdown easing, which clarifies and narrows the original usage claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Acupuncture at acupoints enhances anaerobic glycolysis and modifies mitochondrial function in migraine patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Acupuncture at acupoints enhances anaerobic glycolysis and modifies mitochondrial function in migraine patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result directly and with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Acupuncture significantly adjusts the levels of plasma pyruvic acid, lactic acid, and citrate in migraine patients compared to pre-treatment levels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Acupuncture significantly adjusts the levels of plasma pyruvic acid, lactic acid, and citrate in migraine patients compared to pre-treatment levels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same metabolite-level changes with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sham acupuncture may partially supply energy to migraine patients through lipid metabolism. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sham acupuncture partially supplies energy to migraine patients through lipid metabolism by changing the levels of plasma lipid, glycerine, and pyruvic acid. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The different ways of supplying energy contribute to the different effects of acupuncture and sham acupuncture. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The different ways of supplying energy contribute to the different effects of acupuncture and sham acupuncture. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding interpretation is preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings provide novel metabolic evidence for the specific effect of acupuncture in relation to sham acupuncture. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings provide novel metabolic evidence for the specific effect of acupuncture in relation to sham acupuncture. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same contribution statement about novel metabolic evidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The S4D-CfC model achieves a strong average F1 score of 0.88 and an average AUROC value of 98% for cardiac abnormality detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The S4D-CfC model demonstrates strong average F1 score and average area under the receiver operating characteristic curve values of 0.88% and 98%, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The S4D-CfC model is significantly smaller in size (242 KB) compared to traditional models while maintaining reasonable performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The S4D-CfC model can be significantly reduced to 25 KB while maintaining reasonable performance on 2.5 s data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On-device fine-tuning of the S4D-CfC model enhances personalized solutions by allowing the system to learn each patient’s data features. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: On-device fine-tuning of the S4D-CfC model can enhance personalized solutions by allowing the system to learn each patient’s data features. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same conclusion about on-device fine-tuning enabling personalization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The S4D-CfC model demonstrates superior performance compared to the structured 2D Convolutional LSTM (ConvLSTM2D) CfC model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The S4D-CfC model outperforms a structured 2D convolutional long short-term memory CfC model. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The S4D-CfC model represents a groundbreaking advancement in cardiac abnormality detection, suitable for resource-constrained hardware. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The S4D-CfC model offers robustness, generalization, and practicality for efficient deployment on limited-resource platforms. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Menstruation blood shows excellent correlation with venous blood for a clinical panel of analytes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a strong correlation between biomarker levels in menstruation and venous blood for many clinical analytes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Semi-quantitative detection of C-reactive protein (CRP), carcinoembryonic antigen (CEA), and cancer antigen 125 (CA-125) is demonstrated using the MenstruAI platform. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The wearable, microfluidic monitoring platform integrated into hygiene pads allows for electronic-free, naked-eye detection of disease biomarkers in menstruation blood. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MenstruAI platform has the potential to revolutionize women’s health by providing a non-invasive and accessible approach to health monitoring. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MenstruAI can revolutionize women's health by offering a non-invasive, affordable, and accessible health monitoring method. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a consistent upward trend in published works in the domain of osteoporosis and nutrition. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Seoul National University was identified as the most prolific institution in osteoporosis and nutrition research. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most prolific author in this field was Geng, Bin, while Kanis J.A. had the highest citation count. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Research hotspots identified in the analysis included bone density, postmenopausal women, and vitamin D. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: State-of-the-art nonlinear predictive models, such as boosted decision trees, can be more efficient for phenotype prediction than conventional linear models if model features include a sufficiently rich set of covariates. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The highest prediction accuracy can ultimately be achieved by ensembling individual linear and nonlinear models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim into the publication with unchanged wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For some of the phenotypes considered, such as asthma and hypothyroidism, the results represent a new state-of-the-art. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: For some of the phenotypes considered, such as asthma and hypothyroidism, the results represent a new state-of-the-art. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spatially dependent, immune cell-specific expression patterns in the tumor immune microenvironment can predict nodal and distant metastasis. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The comprehensive characterization of tumor infiltrating lymphocytes and other immune infiltrates is achievable using highly multiplexed spatial omics technologies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The comprehensive characterization of tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes and other immune infiltrates is achievable using highly multiplexed spatial omics technologies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further investigation of identified biomarkers may reveal independent risk factors for colorectal cancer metastasis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An interpretable machine learning model was developed to predict COVID-19 severity using common and rare exonic variants from whole exome sequencing data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An interpretable machine-learning model was developed to predict COVID-19 severity using common and rare exonic variants. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Integrated PolyGenic Score provides a synthetic and interpretable index for describing the contribution of host genetics in COVID-19 severity. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Around one quarter of the selected genes associated with COVID-19 severity are sex-specific. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Around one quarter of the selected genes associated with COVID-19 severity are sex-specific. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Pathway analysis of the selected genes reflects the multi-organ nature of COVID-19. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model performance tested on an independent dataset resulted in an accuracy of 67.18%, sensitivity of 52.17%, and specificity of 75.60%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach has the potential to be a prognostic method for COVID-19 despite high inter-sample variation in saliva. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results demonstrate that the approach has the potential to be a prognostic method for COVID-19 despite the high inter-sample variation in saliva. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Tempus Tumor Origin (Tempus TO) assay is a machine learning classifier that can discriminate between 68 clinically relevant cancer subtypes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Tempus Tumor Origin (Tempus TO) assay is capable of discriminating between 68 clinically relevant cancer subtypes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is equivalent in both versions with only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Tempus TO model demonstrates 91% accuracy when assessed on a cohort of 9,210 samples with known diagnoses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Tempus TO model demonstrates 91% accuracy when assessed on a cohort of 9210 total samples with known diagnoses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The accuracy and cohort size are the same; wording differences do not change the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Tempus TO model recapitulated established associations between genomic alterations and cancer subtype when evaluated on a cohort of cancers of unknown primary (CUPs). (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model recapitulated established associations between genomic alterations and cancer subtype when evaluated on a cohort of cancers of unknown primary (CUPs). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication expresses this result with higher certainty, so the claim is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining diagnostic prediction tests like Tempus TO with sequencing-based variant reporting may expand therapeutic options for patients with cancers of unknown primary or uncertain histology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining diagnostic prediction tests with sequencing-based variant reporting may expand therapeutic options for patients with cancers of unknown primary or uncertain histology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is preserved verbatim aside from minor wording; certainty remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cardiorespiratory signatures detect late-onset sepsis in very low birth weight infants at three NICUs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cardiorespiratory signatures detect late-onset sepsis in very low birth weight infants at three NICUs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The primary result is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The heart rate and oxygenation model performed better than models using either heart rate or oxygenation alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The heart rate-oxygenation model performed better than models using either heart rate or oxygenation alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparative performance claim is present verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Demographics improve the discrimination of all physiologic data models but dampen dynamic performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Demographics improve the discrimination of all physiologic data models but dampen dynamic performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about demographics is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predictive modeling with both heart rate and oxygenation features provides the best dynamic risk prediction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictive models using both heart rate and respiratory data may improve early sepsis detection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the benefit more cautiously ('may improve early sepsis detection') and narrows the claim compared with the preprint's stronger assertion of providing the best dynamic risk prediction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 41.2% and 17.3% of the sample were classified as having at least one cognitive domain impaired using -1 and -1.5 standard deviations as cutoff points. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 41.2% and 17.3% of the sample were classified as having at least one cognitive domain impaired using -1 and -1.5 standard deviations as cutoff points. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric results and wording are identical between preprint and publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Attention/processing speed was the most frequently impaired domain among patients with cognitive dysfunction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Attention/processing speed was the most frequently impaired domain among patients with post-COVID syndrome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements identify the same domain as most frequently impaired; the publication specifies the clinical context but does not change the finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cognitive impairment was associated with younger age and lower education levels, but not hospitalization. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cognitive impairment was associated with younger age and lower education levels, but not hospitalization. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The association pattern and qualifiers are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a harmonization of the criteria to define and classify cognitive impairment in the post-COVID syndrome. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a harmonization of the criteria to define and classify cognitive impairment in the post-COVID syndrome. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The proposal to harmonize criteria appears verbatim and unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These criteria may be extrapolated to other neuropsychological batteries and settings, contributing to the diagnosis of cognitive deficits after COVID-19. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed criteria may be extrapolated to other neuropsychological batteries and settings, contributing to the diagnosis of cognitive deficits after COVID-19. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same extrapolation claim with only minor wording adjustments, preserving meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IBD patients do not have higher risks of infection and worse outcomes in COVID-19 than the general population. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: IBD patients do not have higher risks of infection and worse outcomes in COVID-19 than the general population. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about comparable COVID-19 risk/outcomes for IBD patients is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The activity of soluble angiotensin-converting enzyme 2, CD209L alternate receptor, and phosphorylated α subunit of eukaryotic translation initiation factor 2 may exert a protective impact in IBD in case of coronavirus infection. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The activity of soluble angiotensin-converting enzyme 2, CD209L, and other receptors may exert a protective impact in IBD in case of coronavirus infection. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication drops the specific phosphorylated eIF2α mention and generalizes to 'other receptors,' changing the specific mechanistic detail and broadening the scope of the receptor claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IBD medication may have beneficial effects on COVID-19 pathogeny, including cytokine storm prevention and treatment, immunomodulation, interferon signaling blocking, and viral endocytosis inhibition. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: IBD medication may have beneficial effects on COVID-19 pathogeny, including cytokine storm prevention and treatment. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication shortens the list of proposed mechanisms to only cytokine storm-related effects, reducing the breadth of mechanistic claims and tempering the original scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further exploration is needed to determine the applicability of the findings to the pathogenesis, disease susceptibility, and treatment management of SARS-CoV-2 infection in IBD. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further exploration is needed to determine the applicability of findings to the pathogenesis, disease susceptibility, and treatment management of SARS-CoV-2 infection in IBD. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The cautionary call for further exploration remains, but the publication softens the certainty level from high to medium.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model achieved a strong correlation of 0.83 between retinal age and chronological age. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model achieved a strong correlation of 0.81 between retinal age and chronological age. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published correlation differs slightly (0.83 -> 0.81), a small numerical adjustment that clarifies the final reported performance without changing the substantive conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Each one-year increase in the retinal age gap was associated with a 2% increase in mortality risk. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Each 1 year increase in the retinal age gap is associated with a 2% increase in risk of all-cause mortality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The effect size and direction are the same; the publication specifies 'all-cause mortality' but does not alter the estimated association.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a non-linear relationship between retinal age gap and mortality risk. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or emphasize a non-linear relationship; this preprint claim about non-linearity is absent from the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher retinal age gaps were associated with substantially increased risks of mortality, but only if the gap exceeded 3.71 years. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific threshold-based finding (3.71 years) is not present in the publication's claim set and therefore appears removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Retinal age gap is a clinically significant biomarker of ageing. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Retinal age gap might be a potential biomarker of ageing that is closely related to risk of mortality. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint stated a stronger, definitive biomarker status while the publication hedges ("might be a potential") and links it to mortality risk more cautiously, reducing confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: No significant association was identified between retinal age gap and cardiovascular- or cancer-related mortality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result about cause-specific (cardiovascular/cancer) mortality does not appear in the preprint claim list and is newly reported in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher tortuosity was significantly associated with higher incidence of angina, myocardial infarction, stroke, deep vein thrombosis, and hypertension. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Higher retinal tortuosity was significantly associated with higher incidence of angina, myocardial infarction, stroke, deep vein thrombosis, and hypertension. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The association list and significance are consistent between preprint and publication with only minor wording addition ('retinal').

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified 175 significantly associated genetic loci in the UK Biobank, of which 173 were novel. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified 175 significantly associated genetic loci in the UK Biobank, of which 173 were novel. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The locus count and novelty statement are identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mendelian randomization revealed causal effects between tortuosity, BMI and LDL. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrated that retinal tortuosity loci served pleiotropic functions as cardiometabolic disease variants and risk factors. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint frames a causal MR result between traits, while the publication emphasizes pleiotropy of loci; this shifts interpretation away from direct causality to shared genetic effects.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrated a shared genetic architecture between retinal tortuosity and certain diseases related to the vasculature. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrated that retinal tortuosity loci served pleiotropic functions as cardiometabolic disease variants and risk factors. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements express the same conclusion about shared genetic architecture/pleiotropy between tortuosity loci and cardiometabolic diseases.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Measuring tortuosity in terms of the distance factor had higher heritability and more associated loci than other tortuosity measures. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific methodological claim about the distance factor measure does not appear among the publication claims provided and thus appears removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational pipeline identified an optimal set of nine biomarker features for training the learner. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computational pipeline identified an optimal set of nine biomarker features for training the learner. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This methodological result is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned model achieved a performance of 99.5% accuracy on an internal test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Validation of the learned model on an independent test dataset yielded a performance of 99.5% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies that the 99.5% was obtained on an independent test dataset (rather than an internal test), strengthening external validity and thus amplifying confidence in the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Blind validation on an external dataset yielded a balanced accuracy of 95.5%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Blind validation on an out-of-domain external dataset yielded a balanced accuracy of 95.5%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The balanced accuracy and blind external validation claim remain the same; the publication adds 'out-of-domain' but does not change the reported performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This model represents the best-performing freely available tool for the high-confidence diagnosis of breast cancer. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This model represents the best-performing freely available tool for the high-confidence diagnosis of breast cancer. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim that the model is best-performing is retained, but the publication lowers the stated certainty from high to medium, tempering the confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MOGDx demonstrated state-of-the-art performance in classifying heterogeneous diseases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MOGDx demonstrated state-of-the-art performance in classifying heterogeneous diseases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Textual claims are essentially identical between preprint and publication, with no change in scope or expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MOGDx has the ability to identify relevant multi-omic markers in classification tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MOGDx has the ability to identify relevant multi-omic markers in classification tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MOGDx integrates more genomic measures with greater patient coverage compared to other network integrative methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MOGDx integrated more genomic measures with greater patient coverage compared to other network integrative methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same claim about data integration and coverage; only minor tense change, no substantive difference in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MOGDx is a promising tool for integrating multi-omic data and interpreting genomic markers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MOGDx is a promising tool for integrating multi-omic data and aiding interpretation of genomic marker data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication wording slightly clarifies the interpretation role ("aiding interpretation of genomic marker data") but retains the same overall conclusion and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A small ensemble of readily observable clinical features can predict in-hospital mortality in severe COVID-19 patients with an accuracy greater than 0.85. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A small ensemble of readily observable clinical features can predict in-hospital mortality in patients with severe COVID-19 with an accuracy greater than 0.85. (certainty=high, type=result)
