alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with severe COVID-19 often experience neurologic symptoms, and those with pre-existing neurodegenerative disease have an increased risk of severe COVID-19. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients with severe COVID-19 often exhibit neurologic symptoms, and those with pre-existing neurodegenerative disease have an increased risk of severe COVID-19. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Substantively identical epidemiologic/clinical conclusion with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis of electronic health records of 471 patients with severe COVID-19 identified clinical characteristics that are predictive of mortality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis of electronic health records of 471 patients with severe COVID-19 identified clinical characteristics that are predictive of mortality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same dataset/result claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings have important implications for prioritizing clinical measures to identify COVID-19 patients at elevated risk of death. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings have important implications for prioritizing clinical measures to identify patients with COVID-19 at elevated risk of death. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is restated with minor wording changes but identical meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating calls from multiple ES-based CNV algorithms using random forest machine learning generated a higher quality dataset than using individual algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating calls from multiple ES-based CNV algorithms using random forest machine learning generated a higher quality data set than using individual algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical methodological/result claim with same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both ES- and aCGH-based approaches had the same sensitivity of 89% and detected the same number of unique pathogenic CNVs not called by the other approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both ES- and array comparative genomic hybridization-based approaches had the same sensitivity of 89% and detected the same number of unique pathogenic CNVs not called by the other approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same numeric sensitivity and comparative result; only acronym expansion in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: De novo CNVs were strongly enriched in known DD-associated genes and exhibited no bias in parental age or sex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: De novo CNVs were strongly enriched in known DD-associated genes and exhibited no bias in parental age or sex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match of result claim and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ES-based CNV calling has higher sensitivity than low-resolution CMAs currently in clinical use, and comparable sensitivity to exon-resolution CMA. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ES-based CNV calling has higher sensitivity than low-resolution CMAs currently in clinical use and comparable sensitivity to exon-resolution CMA. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same comparative conclusion about sensitivity retained in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With sufficient investment in bioinformatic analysis, exome-based CNV detection could replace low-resolution CMA for detecting pathogenic CNVs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: With sufficient investment in bioinformatic analysis, exome-based CNV detection could replace low-resolution CMA for detecting pathogenic CNVs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The policy/conclusion about potential replacement remains the same with similar expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Converting between the EPIC Urinary Irritative/Obstructive subscale and the IPSS using linear regressions resulted in mean absolute errors (MAEs) of 3.88 and 6.12 below the respective previously published minimal important differences (MIDs) of 5.2 and 10 points. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Converting between the EPIC Urinary Irritative/Obstructive subscale and the IPSS using linear regressions resulted in mean absolute errors (MAEs) of 3.88 and 6.12, which is below the respective previously published minimal important differences (MIDs) of 5.2 and 10 points. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric results and interpretation relative to MIDs are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Converting between the EPIC Urinary Summary and the IPSS was less accurate with MAEs of 5.13 and 10.45, similar to the MIDs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Converting between the EPIC Urinary Summary and the IPSS was less accurate with MAEs of 5.13 and 10.45, similar to the MIDs. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content of the accuracy claim is unchanged but the publication reports a lower certainty level (medium vs. high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: More complex model architectures did not result in improved performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: More complex model architectures did not result in improved performance in this study. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same empirical finding; publication adds "in this study" but meaning and certainty are maintained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Linear regressions can be used to convert between the IPSS and the EPIC Urinary subscales. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Linear regressions can be used to convert between the IPSS and the EPIC Urinary subscales. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The equations obtained in this study can be used to compare results across clinical trials, but should not be used to inform clinical decision-making in individual patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The equations obtained in this study can be used to compare results across clinical trials, but should not be used to inform clinical decision-making in individual patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Policy recommendation and cautionary note are identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The behavioral clusters express symptoms co-occurring in accordance with the literature on behavioral deficits covariance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The emergent clusters correspond to well-documented syndromes, validating the purely data-driven symptom groupings alongside established neurological knowledge. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication frames the same cluster finding as correspondence to documented syndromes, which clarifies and strengthens the link to established literature while preserving the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The significant voxels found are aligned with the literature and well separated even when lesion density maps overlap. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clusters exhibit critical voxels in group-specific anatomical locations, suggesting that our method disentangles functionally distinct substrates within shared vascular territories. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements refer to anatomically localized voxels associated with clusters; the publication interprets this as disentangling substrates and expresses slightly lower certainty, so the claim is clarified and confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presented workflow offers concrete, clinically useful correlations between patients profiles, individual patients-to-group associations, in-group co-occurrences of symptoms, and brain lesion correlates. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our workflow represents a significant methodological advancement, providing robust, clinically relevant insights into symptom phenotyping and lesion patterns. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication reframes the workflow claim as a methodological advancement but retains the assertion of clinically relevant, robust insights, representing a clarified and slightly more formal presentation of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The underlying mathematical assumptions and the unsupervised machine learning techniques offer statistically robust results. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study introduces a novel unsupervised framework to stratify patients into clinically coherent subgroups based on behavioral symptom profiles. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the novel framework contribution (shifting focus from technical assumptions to the introduced method) and presents it with higher certainty; this reframes and amplifies the original methodological/assurance-oriented claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spatial neglect is a strong predictor for poor performance on everyday tasks and resistance to rehabilitation after right hemispheric stroke. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Spatial neglect is a strong predictor for poor performance on everyday tasks and resistance to rehabilitation after a right hemispheric stroke. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication sentence is essentially identical in content and strength to the preprint claim, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best variable combination for predicting long-term prognosis in stroke patients includes individual neglect severity in the acute phase, along with lesion location and size. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best variable combination for predicting long-term prognosis in stroke patients includes individual neglect severity in the acute phase, along with lesion location and size. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording and implications are the same between preprint and publication, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Demographic information was found to be less beneficial in predicting outcomes for stroke patients. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Demographic information was found to be less beneficial in predicting outcomes for stroke patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same; only the label changed from 'result' to 'conclusion' in the publication, but the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified variables explained 66% of the total variance of neglect patients, indicating their potential in predicting individual outcome prognosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model achieved a high level of accuracy by explaining 66% of the total variance of neglect patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same quantitative result (66% variance explained) but the publication frames it more directly as model accuracy; this is a rephrasing/clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The synthetic images increase the accuracy of a diagnostic classifier from a baseline accuracy of around 61% to 79%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The synthetic images increase the accuracy of a diagnostic classifier from a baseline accuracy of around 61% to 79%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric result and claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best architecture, a GAN with spectral normalization regulation and an additional encoder (α-SN-GAN), is capable of generating distinct image sets for schizophrenia patients and healthy controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best architecture, a GAN with spectral normalization regulation and an additional encoder (α-SN-GAN), is capable of generating distinct image sets for schizophrenia patients and healthy controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same methodological/result claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This novel end-to-end pipeline for schizophrenia diagnosis demonstrates a data and memory efficient approach to support clinical decision-making. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This novel end-to-end pipeline for schizophrenia diagnosis demonstrates a data and memory efficient approach to support clinical decision-making. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generation of synthetic images from real data is a promising approach to overcome the limitations of small medical data sets. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generation of synthetic images from real data is a promising approach to overcome the limitations of small medical data sets. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is the same in both versions, unchanged in scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CR has been proven to reduce mortality and morbidity in patients with CVD. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cardiac rehabilitation (CR) has been proven to reduce mortality and morbidity in patients with cardiovascular disease. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the established conclusion about CR with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best-performing ML models reported AUC between 0.82 and 0.91, sensitivity from 0.77 to 0.95, indicating good prediction capabilities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best-performing ML models reported area under the receiver operating characteristics curve between 0.82 and 0.91, with sensitivity from 0.77 to 0.95, indicating good prediction capabilities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric performance ranges are identical and presented the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: None of the ML-based clinical models underwent calibration or external validation. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: None of the ML models underwent calibration or external validation, raising concerns about bias. (certainty=high, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most studies raised concerns for bias. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: None of the ML models underwent calibration or external validation, raising concerns about bias. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same concern about bias; it ties the bias concern to the lack of calibration/validation but the substantive concern remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: External validation of existing models and development of new models with robust methodology based on larger populations and targeting diverse clinical outcomes in CR are needed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review identifies key research gaps in ML application in CR and provides constructive suggestions for improving the quality of ML models specifically in CR. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the preprint recommendation in a more synthesized form (gaps and suggestions), which clarifies and reframes the recommended next steps without changing intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cognitive impairments are a core feature of psychosis that impact clinical and real-world functional outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This background/result statement from the preprint is not restated in the publication's listed claims, so it appears omitted from the final claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PRECOGNITION project aims to translate normative modelling approaches from brain imaging to psychosis data to create cognitive growth charts for individual tracking and prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PRECOGNITION project will produce cognitive growth charts for longitudinal tracking and individual prediction by translating normative modeling approaches from brain imaging to psychosis data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same core project aim (cognitive growth charts via normative modelling) with equivalent framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The project will develop machine learning models to harmonise and stratify cohorts based on cognitive data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The project will develop machine learning models for harmonizing and stratifying cohorts based on cognitive data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This methodological objective is carried forward verbatim into the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PRECOGNITION project will provide comprehensive reference models that include broad sociodemographic coverage for individuals with psychosis. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study will provide integrated next-generation norms that include broad sociodemographic coverage and distinct norms for individuals with psychosis and unaffected individuals. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands and clarifies the claim by specifying integrated next-generation norms and extending coverage to unaffected individuals; certainty is also stronger in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differentially modified 5hmC genes were identified across T2D patients with diabetic nephropathy, non-DN vascular complications, and controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Genome-wide analyses of 5hmC in cfDNA detected 427 and 336 differential 5hmC modifications associated with DN, compared with non-DN individuals and controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives specific counts and frames the same finding more concretely, so the result is clarified rather than altered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A ten-gene panel was shown to provide differential 5hmC patterns between controls and diabetic nephropathy patients, as well as between controls and non-DN patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An exploratory model comprised of ten 5hmC genes shows the possibility to distinguish DN from non-DN individuals or controls. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down the strength of the claim (from 'was shown' to an exploratory model that 'shows the possibility'), reflecting a revision and reduced confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 5hmC profiles in cfDNA reflected novel DN-associated epigenetic modifications relevant to the disease pathogenesis of diabetic nephropathy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis suggested relevant pathways such as NOD-like receptor signaling pathway and tyrosine metabolism. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides specific pathway-level results that support the preprint's more general statement linking 5hmC changes to disease-relevant biology, thus clarifying the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings in cfDNA have the potential to be exploited as a clinically useful tool for predicting diabetic nephropathy in high risk T2D patients. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Genome-wide analysis suggests the possibility of exploiting novel 5hmC in patient-derived cfDNA as a non-invasive tool for monitoring DN in high risk T2D patients in the future. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the translational potential more cautiously (possibility, monitoring rather than prediction) and lowers certainty, representing a narrowing and tempering of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interoceptive awareness is associated with fatigue in individuals with multiple sclerosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interoceptive awareness is associated with fatigue in individuals with multiple sclerosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication text are effectively identical in meaning and certainty, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Autonomic function is associated with exteroceptive metacognition, but not with fatigue. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Exteroceptive metacognition is associated with autonomic function but not with fatigue. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same association and non-association with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individual fatigue scores can be predicted from measurements of interoceptive awareness and sleep quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individual fatigue scores can be predicted from measurements of interoceptive awareness and sleep quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The prediction claim is verbatim between preprint and publication, with unchanged certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study supports theoretical concepts of interoception as an important factor for fatigue. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study supports theoretical concepts of interoception as an important factor for fatigue. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions present the same interpretive conclusion with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Amalgamation of computed tomogram (CT) data with clinical parameters can prognosticate the need for invasive mechanical ventilation in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Amalgamation of computed tomogram (CT) data with clinical parameters can prognosticate the need for invasive mechanical ventilation in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same in both documents, with identical scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models developed from clinical parameters alone, clinical parameters with radiologist derived CT severity score, and clinical parameters with automated lesion-to-lung ratio achieved AUCs of 0.87, 0.89, and 0.91, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models developed from clinical parameters alone, clinical parameters with CT severity score, and clinical parameters with automated lesion-to-lung ratio achieved AUCs of 0.87, 0.89, and 0.91, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance metrics and model comparisons are presented identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An operating point on the ROC can be chosen to aid clinicians in risk characterization according to resource availability and ethical considerations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An operating point on the ROC can be chosen to aid clinicians in risk characterization according to resource availability and ethical considerations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach of using CT data and clinical parameters can be deployed in more general settings to effectively predict outcomes of severe COVID-19 patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach of using CT data and clinical parameters can be deployed in more general settings to effectively predict outcomes of severe COVID-19 patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The deployment claim is preserved unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models predicting with causes of the outcome are more transportable than those predicting with consequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models predicting with causes of the outcome are more transportable than those predicting with consequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The central empirical result about causal predictors being more transportable is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AUC differences indicated inconsistent trends of transportability between different external settings. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AUC differences indicated inconsistent trends of transportability between the different external settings. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The description of inconsistent AUC trends across settings is essentially identical, with only minor wording variation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predicting with causes of the outcome results in better transportability compared to anti-causal predictions measured by calibration differences. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predicting with causes of the outcome results in better transportability compared to anti-causal predictions when considering calibration differences. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is the same; the publication slightly rephrases but preserves the focus on calibration differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Calibration differences are more suitable than AUC differences to assess model transportability to external settings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Calibration performance is crucial when assessing model transportability to external settings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The clinical decision support system (CDSS) demonstrates AUC-ROC scores of 0.83 for rejection and 0.95 for graft failure during internal validation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The clinical decision support system (CDSS) demonstrates AUC-ROC scores of 0.83 for rejection and 0.95 for graft failure in internal validation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reader study indicates that predictions by physicians converge towards the suggestions made by the CDSS, but performance in terms of AUC-ROC does not improve. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reader study shows that predictions by physicians converge toward the CDSS. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CDSS detects partially different patients at risk compared to physicians without the CDSS. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CDSS detects partially different patients at risk compared to physicians. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of medical professionals and a CDSS might help detect more patients at risk for graft failure. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of medical professionals and a CDSS might help detect more patients at risk for graft failure. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Heritability estimates for treatment-resistant depression (TRD) ranged from 2% to 4.2%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Significant genetic overlap was observed between TRD and traits related to cognition, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder, schizophrenia, alcohol and smoking traits, and body mass index. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Two genome-wide significant loci were identified that are previously implicated in metabolic traits, suggesting shared biology and potential pharmacological implications. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Classification accuracies for diagnosing Parkinson's disease range from 67% to 85%, depending on the language. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper introduces the concept of language robustness as a desirable quality in digital speech biomarkers, ensuring consistent behavior across languages. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A generative embedding procedure based on combining regression DCM with a support vector machine enabled the best predictions for future episodes of depression. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate the use of our tool for evaluating several imputation methods, showing modest accuracy of imputation overall, with iterative imputation having the best performance. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: D-cycloserine was independently associated with breathlessness improvement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: D-cycloserine was independently associated with breathlessness improvement. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SCOAT-Net is inspired by the biological vision mechanism for the segmentation of COVID-19 lung opacification from CT images. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pan-cancer analysis associates common genomic signatures in poor survival subtypes with extracellular matrix modeling, immune deregulation, and mitosis processes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pan-cancer analysis associates common genomic signatures in poor survival subtypes with extracellular matrix modeling, immune deregulation, and mitosis processes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The incidence of adverse drug reactions (ADRs) has not changed over the last 30 years, contributing to 5-10% of acute care hospital admissions worldwide. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated ADR reporting using a deep learning natural language processing algorithm can increase reporting rates compared to traditional spontaneous reporting methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed model achieved a ROC-AUC of 0.955 for identifying discharge summaries containing ADR mentions, significantly outperforming two comparator models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The final model demonstrated good performance with a ROC-AUC of 0.955 for the task of identifying discharge summaries containing ADR mentions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric ROC-AUC performance is reproduced exactly in the publication; the performance claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed model achieved a ROC-AUC of 0.955 for identifying discharge summaries containing ADR mentions, significantly outperforming two comparator models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed deep learning natural language processing algorithm significantly outperformed two comparator models in identifying ADRs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication separates the comparator-performance statement into its own claim but it conveys the same result as in the preprint (significant outperformance).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model was fine-tuned on a corpus of 861 annotated discharge summaries to ensure accurate detection of ADRs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Details about the fine-tuning on a specific 861-summary corpus are not present in the publication claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning model predicts mortality in patients with severe mental illness using clinical data and relevant factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning model predicts mortality in patients with severe mental illness using clinical data and relevant factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the predictive-modeling result directly from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Class-contrastive reasoning can be used to generate complex explanations for machine model predictions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our methodology demonstrates how class-contrastive reasoning can generate complex explanations for machine model predictions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the methodological contribution and expresses the same level of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology can generate new hypotheses that can be tested in follow-up studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state that the methodology generates new testable hypotheses; instead it reports specific model-associated findings, so the general hypothesis-generation claim is not present in the pub claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The technique can create intricate explanations from healthcare data and potentially other areas requiring explainability. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This technique can be employed to create intricate explanations from healthcare data and possibly other areas where explainability is important. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the contribution but states it with higher certainty, amplifying confidence in general applicability.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Diuretics seemed to be associated with a lower probability of mortality in a group of patients with cardiovascular disease. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific clinical association is present in the publication but was not claimed in the preprint claim set.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of delirium and dementia in Alzheimer's disease may predispose some patients towards a higher probability of predicted mortality. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep neural networks can identify human balance patterns to predict fall-risk. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep neural networks can effectively identify human balance patterns to predict fall-risk. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed One-One-One Deep Neural Networks algorithm provides a considerable increase in performance compared to other algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed One-One-One Deep Neural Networks algorithm significantly outperforms other algorithms in predicting fall-risk. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The One-One-One Deep Neural Network model achieved an accuracy of 99.9%, precision of 100%, and sensitivity of 100% at the 12th epoch. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The One-One-One Deep Neural Network model achieved an accuracy of 99.9%, precision of 100%, and sensitivity of 100% at the 12th epoch. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This methodology represents a novel approach for accurately predicting human risk of fall. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study presents a novel methodology for accurately predicting human risk of fall using force-plate time series signals. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the data modality (force-plate time series), which clarifies and narrows the original more general claim about a novel methodology.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Artificial intelligence, particularly deep learning, has great potential for detecting hidden tobacco advertising. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Artificial intelligence, particularly deep learning, has great potential for detecting hidden tobacco advertising. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level conclusion about AI potential.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model can achieve 74% accuracy for images and 98% for text in detecting smoking cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves 74% accuracy for images and 98% for text. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance figures are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed system integrates the possibility of expert intervention through human reinforcement. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system allows for expert intervention through human reinforcement. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using pre-trained multimodal models allows for the detection of smoking in different media even with few training data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sample sizes required for randomized trials evaluating levetiracetam and propofol in treating epileptiform activity are infeasible. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sample sizes required for RCTs evaluating levetiracetam and propofol in patients with acute brain injuries are infeasible. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the infeasibility conclusion but specifies the population (acute brain injuries), which clarifies and narrows the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulations indicated that 500 participants are needed for levetiracetam 7 mg/kg vs placebo to achieve 80% power. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulations indicate that 500 participants are needed for levetiracetam 7 mg/kg versus placebo to achieve 80% power. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulations comparing propofol at varying doses did not reach 80% power even at sample sizes greater than 1200. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulations comparing propofol at varying doses did not reach 80% power even at sample sizes greater than 1200. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The propofol simulation finding is carried forward unchanged into the published paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights the utility of simulations with observational data to inform null hypotheses and assess the feasibility of future trials for treating epileptiform activity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The simulation-based RCT paradigm can be used to assess the feasibility of future trials of anti-seizure treatment in acute brain injury. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Slower RMDA is associated with less preserved EZ and IZ area, and decreased IZ thickness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Delayed RMDA (longer RIT) is most strongly associated with less preserved IZ area. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published result focuses specifically on IZ area as the strongest association and omits explicit claims about EZ area and IZ thickness, thereby narrowing and tempering the original multi-feature association.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The association with IZ area and RMDA in large-scale data is biologically plausible. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This correlation is consistent with a previous analysis of only foveal B-scans. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the plausibility claim as consistency with prior analyses, which supports the original plausibility statement though with different wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integrity of IZ may provide insight into the health of photoreceptors and RPE in early AMD. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RMDA is correlated with the status of outer retinal bands in early and intermediate AMD eyes, particularly the status of the IZ. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IZ integrity is a potential new imaging biomarker for early AMD. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RMDA is correlated with the status of outer retinal bands in early and intermediate AMD eyes, particularly the status of the IZ. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents correlation evidence for IZ status but stops short of asserting it as a definitive biomarker, effectively tempering the original high-certainty biomarker claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The correlation between RIT and IZ integrity was not apparent when considering normal eyes alone within 1.5 mm of the fovea. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific result about the subset analysis (normal eyes within 1.5 mm) is new in the publication and was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The FT-Transformer model achieves 1.44% Mean Absolute Error (MAE) in the regression task of the gamma pass rate prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The FT-Transformer model achieves 1.44% Mean Absolute Error (MAE) in the regression task of the gamma pass rate prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the binary classification task of PSQA failure prediction, FT-Transformer achieves 0.85 ROC AUC. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the binary classification task of PSQA failure prediction, FT-Transformer achieves 0.85 ROC AUC. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FT-Transformer can reduce the need for patient rescheduling due to PSQA failures by 80%. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FT-Transformer achieves comparable performance with the leading tree ensemble methods while providing an end-to-end differentiable map from MLC leaf positions to the probability of PSQA failure. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FT-Transformer offers an unprecedented benefit of providing an end-to-end differentiable map from MLC leaf positions to the probability of PSQA failure. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI model produced a limited total extreme misclassification rate of 3.4% on held-aside test sets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work represents one of the first efforts at designing a robust, repeatable, accurate, and clinically translatable deep-learning model for cervical screening. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work represents one of the first efforts at designing a robust, repeatable, accurate, and clinically translatable deep-learning model for cervical screening. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PAVE study aims to validate a screen-triage-treat protocol using novel biomarkers for cervical cancer prevention in resource-limited settings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The efficacy phase of the PAVE study will compare the sensitivity of the PAVE strategy for detection of precancer to current standard of care at a given level of specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness phase of the PAVE study will examine the feasibility and acceptability of the PAVE strategy for clinicians and patients.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of HPV genotype and AVE classification will generate a risk score that corresponds to the risk of precancer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PAVE study aims to assess a novel screening-triage-treatment strategy integrating self-sampled HPV testing, deep-learning-based automated visual evaluation, and targeted therapies.
alignments.rationale: The publication mentions integrating HPV testing and AVE but does not assert the specific claim that their combination generates a validated risk score; the specific predictive-risk assertion is therefore softened/revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inherently interpretable deep learning model obtained an accuracy of .906 and an AUC of .904 on the internal test set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inherently interpretable deep learning model achieved an accuracy of .906 and an AUC of .904 on the internal test set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High evidence regions directly extracted from the model contained clinically relevant lesions such as microaneurysms or hemorrhages with a high precision of .960.
alignments.pub_claim_text: High evidence regions extracted from the model contained clinically relevant lesions with a precision of .960, surpassing post-hoc techniques applied to a standard DNN.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the precision claim but adds a comparative statement (surpassing post-hoc techniques), which clarifies and contextualizes the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Decision support by the model highlighting high-evidence regions in the image improved screening accuracy for difficult decisions and improved screening speed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Decision support by the model highlighting high-evidence regions improved screening accuracy and speed for difficult decisions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inherently interpretable deep learning models can reach state-of-the-art performance and support screening for early DR by improving human-AI collaboration.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates that inherently interpretable deep learning models can provide clinical decision support while achieving state-of-the-art performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating a machine learning method for predicting postoperative anterior chamber depth improves the refraction prediction performance of existing intraocular lens calculation formulas.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating a machine learning method for predicting postoperative anterior chamber depth improves the refraction prediction performance of existing intraocular lens calculation formulas.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean absolute errors in refraction prediction for the ML-modified effective lens position were significantly lower than those of the original formulas.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean absolute errors in refraction prediction for the modified formulas were significantly lower than those of the original formulas.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean absolute errors for the ML-modified formulas were 0.356 for Haigis, 0.352 for Hoffer Q, 0.371 for Holladay, and 0.361 for SRK/T.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean absolute errors for the modified Haigis, Hoffer Q, Holladay, and SRK/T formulas were 0.356, 0.352, 0.371, and 0.361 Diopters, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The reported numeric MAEs match exactly between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The original mean absolute errors for the formulas were 0.373 for Haigis, 0.408 for Hoffer Q, 0.384 for Holladay, and 0.394 for SRK/T.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The original mean absolute errors for the Haigis, Hoffer Q, Holladay, and SRK/T formulas were 0.373, 0.408, 0.384, and 0.394 Diopters, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The original formulas' MAEs are reported identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Modern machine learning methods such as tree boosting and multilayer perceptrons perform comparably to traditional logistic regression methods in predicting stroke outcomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Modern machine learning methods such as tree boosting and multilayer perceptrons perform comparably to traditional logistic regression methods in predicting stroke outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The interpretability analysis demonstrated that age and stroke severity are consistently rated as the most important predictive features across all models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The interpretability analysis demonstrated that age and stroke severity are consistently rated as the most important predictive features across different models.
alignments.rationale: The key interpretability finding is preserved with only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Modern machine learning methods can provide explainability that aligns with domain knowledge interpretation and traditional method rankings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Modern machine learning methods can provide explainability that aligns with domain knowledge interpretation and traditional method rankings.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about alignment of explainability with domain knowledge.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future work should focus on replicating these findings in other datasets and further testing different explainability methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Future work should focus on replicating these findings in other datasets and further testing of different explainability methods.
alignments.rationale: The recommendation for future work is preserved, though the published version frames it with lower certainty (limitation), tempering the confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CoA duration and severity were predictive of aortic remodeling and active dysfunction in rabbits, and HTN in CoA patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CoA duration and severity were predictive of aortic remodeling and active dysfunction in rabbits, and hypertension in patients with CoA.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The interaction between patient age and BPGpp at surgery contributed significantly to HTN, suggesting preclinical findings translate to patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The interaction between patient age and BPGpp at surgery contributed significantly to hypertension, suggesting preclinical findings translate to patients.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pre-operative BPGpp and surgical age predict risk of HTN along with residual post-operative BPGpp.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Preoperative BPGpp and surgical age predict the risk of hypertension along with residual postoperative BPGpp.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The current BPGpp threshold determined decades ago is likely too high to prevent adverse coarctation-induced aortic remodeling.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The current BPGpp threshold determined decades ago is likely too high to prevent adverse coarctation-induced aortic remodeling.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results and decision tree analysis provide a foundation for revising CoA treatment guidelines considering the interaction between CoA severity and duration to limit the risk of HTN.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results and decision tree analysis provide a foundation for revising CoA treatment guidelines considering the interaction between CoA severity and duration to limit the risk of hypertension.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT's GPT-4 model scored 82.7% for essential questions and 77.2% for basic and clinical questions on Japan's National Medical Licensing Examination (NMLE). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best model scored 82.7% for essential questions and 77.2% for basic and clinical questions, both of which sufficed the minimum passing scoring rates. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of ChatGPT on the NMLE surpassed the minimum passing rates of 80.0% for essential questions and 74.6% for basic and clinical questions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best model scored 82.7% for essential questions and 77.2% for basic and clinical questions, both of which sufficed the minimum passing scoring rates. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified three major factors contributing to incorrect answers: insufficient medical knowledge, information on Japan-specific medical system and guidelines, and mathematical errors. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three major factors contributing to incorrect answers were identified: insufficient medical knowledge, information on Japan-specific medical system and guidelines, and mathematical errors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Despite achieving a minimum passing rate, there are strong concerns regarding the use of ChatGPT for medical purposes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT may have the potential to serve as a valuable tool for solving medical and healthcare problems. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning techniques, including deep learning, are essential for categorizing electronic health record data into predefined groups. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning techniques, including deep learning, are essential for categorizing electronic health record data into predefined groups. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Label noise in electronic health records poses a significant risk during the training of deep learning models. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Label noise in electronic health records poses a significant risk during the training of deep learning models. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
