alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication describe the same set of models and modalities used, with effectively identical scope and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For venous thromboembolism, the combined model achieved a ROC-AUC of 66.1% for linear models and 65.4% for deep learning models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: For venous thromboembolism, the combined model achieved a ROC-AUC of 66.6% for linear models and 62.6% for deep learning models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports different (updated) ROC-AUC values for both linear and deep learning models, altering the numeric results while keeping the same comparison and context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inclusion of genetics in the predictive models seemed to improve accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inclusion of genetics in the predictive models seemed to improve accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about genetics improving accuracy is stated identically in both versions with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Overall, predictability was similar between linear and deep learning models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Overall, predictability was similar between linear and deep learning models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both documents state that predictability was similar between the two modelling approaches without change in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Automated Visual Evaluation (AVE) tool identified cases of cervical precancerous and cancerous lesions with high performance, achieving an AUC of 0.91. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Automated Visual Evaluation (AVE) tool identified cases of cervical precancerous and cancerous lesions with high performance, achieving an AUC of 0.91. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The AUC and high-performance claim are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AVE demonstrated a sensitivity of 85% and specificity of 86% for identifying cervical precancerous lesions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AVE demonstrated a sensitivity of 85% and specificity of 86% for identifying cervical precancerous lesions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Sensitivity and specificity values are reported unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of AVE represents a considerable improvement over the visual inspection with acetic acid (VIA), which has a sensitivity of 66%. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of AVE represents a considerable improvement over traditional visual inspection with acetic acid (VIA), which has a sensitivity of 66%. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions explicitly compare AVE to VIA with the same sensitivity baseline, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study's findings may be subject to verification bias due to biopsies being obtained only from participants with visible aceto-white cervical lesions. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Limitations of the study include potential inflation of performance estimates due to verification bias and the internal validation of the test data. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the verification-bias concern and explicitly adds internal validation as an additional limitation, broadening the limitations discussion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AVE algorithm's performance may be inflated as the images used for testing were acquired from the same set of patients and devices used during training. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Limitations of the study include potential inflation of performance estimates due to verification bias and the internal validation of the test data. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication consolidates the preprint's concern about inflated performance due to internal validation/overlap between training and test data into a limitations statement, clarifying the point.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI framework achieved a sensitivity of 99.1% and specificity of 98.0% for the test dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI algorithm achieved a sensitivity of 98.0% and specificity of 99.0% in classifying liver tissue images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports different numeric sensitivity/specificity values (and swaps the exact numbers), revising the quantitative performance while keeping the same evaluation context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of the AI framework was 98.5%, which is higher than the accuracy of human experts, which fluctuated between 93.0% and 95.0%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the proposed AI framework in classifying histological images with HCC was comparable to that of human experts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's stronger claim of outperforming human experts is softened in the publication to 'comparable' performance for HCC, indicating a tempered confidence and a revision of the comparative claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed AI framework demonstrates potential universality in histological image classification with limited training. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracies of the AI framework in classifying colorectal carcinoma and breast invasive ductal carcinoma were 96.8% and 96.0%, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides accuracy results across multiple cancer types, giving concrete evidence that clarifies and somewhat broadens the preprint's claim of potential universality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI framework has excellent performance in classifying hepatocellular carcinoma. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the proposed AI framework in classifying histological images with HCC was comparable to that of human experts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down the preprint's characterization of 'excellent' performance to 'comparable' with human experts for HCC, representing a revision and reduced confidence in the strength of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm is able to classify four classes of disease severity (normal, mild, moderate, and severe) with an average area under curve (AUC) of 0.93. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm is able to classify four classes of disease severity (normal, mild, moderate, and severe) with an average Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.93. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The wording and reported metric are effectively identical between preprint and publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The outputs of different layers of the CNN under dominant filters provide valuable insight about the subtle patterns in the CXRs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The outputs of different layers of the CNN under dominant filters provide valuable insight about the subtle patterns in the CXRs, which can improve the accuracy in the reading of CXRs by a radiologist. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit application (improving radiologist reading accuracy), clarifying and broadening the original observation without changing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach can be used for studying the disease progression in single patients and its influencing factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach can be used for studying the disease progression in a single patient and its influencing factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text and meaning are effectively identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our technique can form the foundation of a more concrete clinical model to predict the evolution of COVID-19 severity and the efficacy of different treatments for each patient. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our technique can form the foundation of a more concrete clinical model to predict the evolution of COVID-19 severity and the efficacy of different treatments for each patient. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried forward verbatim into the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of AI to assess the severity and possibly predict the future stages of the disease early on will be essential in dealing with the upcoming waves of COVID-19. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This broader policy/utility statement appears only in the publication and does not have a counterpart in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed deep learning model achieved predictive accuracy of over 96% to differentiate between non-severe COVID-19, severe COVID-19, non-COVID viral infection, and healthy classes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: All 3 machine learning models had high overall prediction accuracy (95.4%-97.7%) and high class-specific prediction accuracy (90.6%-99.9%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a more detailed numeric range for accuracy that clarifies the earlier single-value claim without contradicting it.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All three machine learning models demonstrated high accuracy, ranging from 95.4% to 97.7%, in differentiating the overall four classes and 90.6% to 99.9% for each individual class. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: All 3 machine learning models had high overall prediction accuracy (95.4%-97.7%) and high class-specific prediction accuracy (90.6%-99.9%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same accuracy ranges and meaning given in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multimodal features provided substantial performance gain compared to using any single feature modality. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multimodal features provided substantial performance gain from the use of any single feature modality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core claim is the same, but the publication frames it as a higher-certainty/result statement rather than a medium-confidence conclusion, indicating amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study offers a novel and effective breakthrough for clinical applications by utilizing high-dimensional multimodal biomedical features. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study's hybrid deep learning-machine learning framework provided a novel and effective breakthrough for clinical applications compared to existing binary classification benchmarks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the novelty by comparing to existing binary benchmarks, refining but not reversing the original contribution claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings and analytical workflow can be utilized as clinical decision support for current COVID-19 and other clinical applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings from this study will supplement and assist with clinical decision support for current COVID-19 diagnostic methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the stated application slightly to supplement current COVID-19 diagnostics, clarifying the intended use as clinical decision support.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An ensembled deep learning model was developed that achieved a patient-level sensitivity of 93.1% and specificity of 93.9% on the multi-center external validation dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ensembled deep learning model yields a patient-level sensitivity of 93.1% and specificity of 93.9% on the multi-center external validation dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance metrics and claim are carried forward unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of human experts in diagnosing biliary atresia can be improved with the help of the developed model. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of human experts in diagnosing biliary atresia is improved with the help of the ensembled deep learning model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication states the improvement more assertively (present tense/result), increasing confidence compared with the preprint's conditional phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrated expert-level performance in diagnosing biliary atresia using smartphone photos and video sequences of sonographic gallbladder images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Diagnosis based on smartphone photos of sonographic gallbladder images through a smartphone app yields expert-level performances. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core claim is the same, but the publication frames it with slightly lower stated certainty, tempering confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides a deep learning solution to assist radiologists in improving biliary atresia diagnosis in rural and undeveloped regions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ensembled deep learning model provides a solution to help radiologists improve the diagnosis of biliary atresia in various clinical application scenarios. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication broadens the suggested deployment from rural/undeveloped regions to various clinical scenarios and presents the claim with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A growing number of studies demonstrate the potential of AI for automatic pre-surgical skin tumor delineation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A growing number of studies demonstrate the potential of automatic pre-surgical skin tumor delineation, which could have tremendous impact on clinical practice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit statement about potential clinical impact, expanding the implication of the original observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current methods for skin tumor delineation rely on a ground truth image, which is not clinically possible. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel approach using hyperspectral images and artificial neural networks circumvents the need for ground truth images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the issue by presenting a method that obviates the stated limitation; the original limitation is effectively addressed, so the claim is revised in context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our novel approach uses hyperspectral images to generate prediction maps for skin tumor borders without needing a complete ground truth image. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel approach using hyperspectral images and artificial neural networks circumvents the need for ground truth images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this methodological contribution with equivalent meaning and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The individual network models developed for each patient enhance the clinical relevance and adaptability of our approach. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ANN model is trained with data from each individual patient, representing a more clinically relevant approach. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same point about per-patient models increasing clinical relevance.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Digital imaging will serve as an important platform for AI to aid decision making and diagnostics in health care. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This broader, general statement about digital imaging and AI appears only in the publication and was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The OLO hematology analyzer demonstrates high accuracy for all complete blood count parameters when compared to the Sysmex XN-Series System. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: OLO performs with high accuracy for all CBC parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same accuracy claim for CBC parameters as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High repeatability and reproducibility were demonstrated for most of the testing parameters of the OLO analyzer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: High repeatability and reproducibility were demonstrated for most of the testing parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The repeatability/reproducibility claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analytical performance of the OLO hematology analyzer was validated in a multicenter clinical laboratory setting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analytical performance of the OLO hematology analyzer was validated in a multicenter clinical laboratory setting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The multicenter validation claim is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrated the validity of complete blood count analysis of samples collected directly from fingerpricks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrated the validity of CBC analysis of samples collected directly from fingerpricks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The fingerprick CBC validity claim remains the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Heatmaps calculated from Attention Rollout and Integrated Gradients are limited by artifacts and in quantitative performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Attention Rollout and Integrated Gradients are prone to artifacts in generating explanations for Vision Transformers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the artifact critique but omits the explicit statement about quantitative performance limitations, focusing the claim on artifacts (a narrowing/clarification of scope).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Removal-based methods like RISE and ViT-Shapley exhibit better qualitative attribution maps and show better results in interpretability metrics for insertion and deletion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RISE and particularly ViT-Shapley generate more reliable and interpretable heatmaps compared to other techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative conclusion about RISE and ViT-Shapley with similar emphasis on reliability and interpretability, refining wording but keeping the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ViT-Shapley shows faster runtime and produces the most promising, reliable, and practical heatmaps. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ViT-Shapley demonstrated faster runtime and superior performance in insertion and deletion metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides concrete metric-based evidence (insertion/deletion) and runtime data supporting the preprint's performance claim, strengthening (amplifying) it and clarifying how superiority was measured.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating the heatmaps generated from approximate Shapley values in pathology reports could help to integrate xAI in the clinical workflow and increase trust in a scalable manner. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating ViT-Shapley-based heatmaps into pathology reports could enhance trust and scalability in clinical workflows. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same conclusion about integrating ViT-Shapley heatmaps to improve trust and scalability in clinical workflows with similar certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Artificial intelligence (AI) is effective in the rapid and reliable detection of COVID-19 based on chest X-ray imaging. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The outstanding performance of pretrained models can significantly improve the speed and accuracy of COVID-19 diagnosis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the general effectiveness claim more specifically around pretrained models and their impact on speed/accuracy, narrowing and clarifying the original broad assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model vgg16 demonstrated superiority over other models in detecting COVID-19-induced pneumonia from chest X-ray images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: VGG16 outperformed all other models in COVID-19 detection with an accuracy of 98.72%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives the same superiority claim but adds a precise accuracy figure, which strengthens and clarifies the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning with X-ray imaging is useful in collecting critical biological markers associated with COVID-19 infection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning with X-ray imaging is useful in collecting critical biological markers associated with COVID-19 infections. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same utility claim but with higher certainty and framed as a result rather than a medium-certainty conclusion, increasing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The high accuracy of the computer-aided diagnostic tool can significantly improve the speed and accuracy of COVID-19 diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The outstanding performance of pretrained models can significantly improve the speed and accuracy of COVID-19 diagnosis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same anticipated diagnostic benefit but couches it with a medium certainty and attributes it to pretrained models, modestly tempering the original high-confidence claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pre-adolescent growth period is the best time for the skeletal Class-III malocclusion treatment. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pre-adolescent growth period is the best time for the skeletal Class-III malocclusion treatment. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion verbatim with unchanged scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Delayed recognition of Class-III malocclusion will yield significant functional, aesthetic, and psychological concerns. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Delayed recognition of Class-III malocclusion will yield significant functional, aesthetic, and psychological concerns. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in the publication with the same implications and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study presents the first fully automated machine learning method to accurately diagnose Class-III malocclusion applied across mobile images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study presents the first fully automated machine learning method to accurately diagnose Class-III malocclusion applied across mobile images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is reproduced exactly in the publication with no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning method achieved an accuracy of 76% in diagnosing Class-III malocclusion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning method achieved an accuracy of 76% in diagnosing Class-III malocclusion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result value and claim are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep learning model achieved an area under the curve (AUC) score of 0.998 for cardiac abnormality diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed deep-learning model achieved AUC scores of 0.998, 0.964, and 0.839 for the diagnosis of cardiac abnormalities, gender identification, and hypertension screening, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same AUC for cardiac abnormality diagnosis while adding analogous results for other tasks, leaving the cardiac result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrated cardiologist-level accuracy in heart diagnosis with interpretability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrated cardiologist-level accuracy in interpretable cardiac diagnosis and advanced mortality risk stratification using ECGs alone. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the cardiologist-level, interpretable diagnosis claim and extends it by adding mortality risk stratification, broadening the claim's scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model shows potential in facilitating clinical knowledge discovery for gender and hypertension detection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model has the potential to facilitate clinical knowledge discovery for gender and hypertension detection, which are not readily available. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same potential for knowledge discovery in gender and hypertension detection with similar certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Key ECG leads identified for the tasks include AVR and V1 leads with an AUC of 0.990. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The V1 ECG lead is important for hypertension screening and mortality risk stratification, achieving an AUC of 0.816 and a univariate HR of 1.70 for these tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's findings differ: it highlights V1 (not AVR) and reports a substantially lower AUC (0.816 vs 0.990), so the original claim is revised, narrowed to V1, and appears tempered by lower performance metrics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model performed three independent medical tasks: cardiac abnormality diagnosis, gender identification, and hypertension screening. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed deep-learning model achieved AUC scores of 0.998, 0.964, and 0.839 for the diagnosis of cardiac abnormalities, gender identification, and hypertension screening, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly reports the three-task performance with corresponding AUCs, matching the preprint's claim about performing those tasks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model achieved AUC values of 0·785 for overall survival prediction on the testing dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model achieved AUC values of 0.785 and 0.695 for overall survival prediction on testing and external data sets, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the reported testing AUC and adds external validation results, broadening the claim by extending evaluation to an external dataset.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model outperformed the present TNM and ResNet-Graph models in terms of net benefit for survival prediction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our Transformer-Graph model outperformed the present TNM and ResNet-Graph models in terms of net benefit for survival prediction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative net-benefit conclusion with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our Transformer-Graph model was effective at predicting survival in patients with early-stage lung cancer. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our Transformer-Graph model outperformed the present TNM and ResNet-Graph models in terms of net benefit for survival prediction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the model's outperforming net-benefit (an operationalization of effectiveness), which clarifies how effectiveness was determined without changing the overall claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some high-risk patients were distinguishable by using a similarity score function defined by non-imaging characteristics. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some high-risk patients were distinguishable by using a similarity score function defined by non-imaging characteristics such as age, gender, histology type, and tumour location. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the non-imaging features used (listing examples) and frames the finding at similar certainty; the core claim remains but with more detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AiTiALVSD model demonstrates high diagnostic performance with an AUROC of 0.919. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AiTiALVSD demonstrates high diagnostic performance with an AUROC of 0.919. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance metric and claim are identical between preprint and publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a significant correlation between AiTiALVSD scores and left ventricular ejection fraction values. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a significant correlation between AiTiALVSD scores and left ventricular ejection fraction values, confirming the model's predictive accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the same correlation but adds wording that frames it as confirming predictive accuracy, a slight clarification rather than substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TCAV analysis indicates that the model aligns with medical knowledge, establishing its clinical plausibility. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TCAV analysis indicates that the model aligns with medical knowledge, establishing its clinical plausibility. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same; only the label/type differs (conclusion→result) but the claim text and meaning are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Despite its robustness to ECG artifacts, there is a noted decrease in specificity in the presence of ECG noise. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Despite its robustness to ECG artifacts, AiTiALVSD shows a decrease in specificity in the presence of ECG noise. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is restated with the model name inserted; the substance (decreased specificity with ECG noise) is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights the importance of transparency and robustness in AI/ML-based diagnostics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the importance of transparency and robustness in AI-ECG, setting a new benchmark in cardiac care. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication narrows the domain from broad "AI/ML-based diagnostics" to "AI-ECG" and adds the new claim of "setting a new benchmark," while reducing certainty, so the claim is revised and confidence tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Syndromic surveillance can provide a timelier alternative for first-line screening of COVID-19 compared to laboratory confirmed cases. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Syndromic surveillance can provide a timelier alternative for first-line screening compared to laboratory confirmed cases. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level conclusion about timeliness with essentially identical wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning-based approach using autoencoders can distinguish outbreaks of COVID-19 from other influenza-like illnesses that share similar syndromes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning-based approach using autoencoders can distinguish outbreaks of COVID-19 from other influenza-like illnesses that share similar syndromes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is reproduced verbatim in the publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The autoencoder can be trained to avoid false alerts for well-managed influenza-like illnesses such as the common cold and influenza. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The autoencoder can be trained to avoid alerting on known and well-managed influenza-like illnesses such as the common cold and influenza. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording differs slightly but the functional claim—avoiding alerts for well-managed illnesses—is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of the proposed syndromic surveillance system could have provided early warning of a novel influenza-like illness outbreak during the 2019-2020 period. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation of the proposed syndromic surveillance system could have provided early warning of an outbreak of a novel influenza-like illness. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication removes the specific 2019-2020 temporal framing, making the statement more general (broadened) while retaining the same evidentiary claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AORTA Gene model explained more variance in thoracic aortic diameter compared to clinical factors alone in multiple test sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AORTA Gene explained more of the variance in thoracic aortic diameter compared to clinical factors alone. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AORTA Gene had a greater AUROC for identifying diameter ≥4cm in each test set compared to clinical factors alone. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: Publication reframes the benefit of genetic information within a comprehensive model and extends the outcome from diameter estimation to identification of dilation and adverse events, a broader clinical end-point.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Larger and more diverse cohorts will be needed to develop more powerful and equitable scores. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This explicit limitation present in the preprint is not reflected in the publication's listed claims, indicating it was removed from the final claim set.

alignments.pub_claim_text: AORTA Gene was more informative for adverse thoracic aortic events in UK Biobank and All of Us. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of chest CT and plasma cytokines serves as good predictors of both death and maximum severity in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel computational framework predicts the severe disease potential of viral genetic variation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Confounding effects of changing patient demographics, increasing vaccination rates, and improving treatment over time are addressed in the model. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a new explicit statement about addressing confounding effects that was not present in the preprint, representing an added limitation/feature claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review identified 15 studies demonstrating diverse applications of NLP in infectious disease management. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review identified 15 studies demonstrating diverse applications of NLP in infectious disease management. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text and meaning are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPT-4 effectively detects urinary tract infections and BERTweet is used for Lyme Disease surveillance through social media analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GPT-4 effectively detects urinary tract infections and BERTweet is used for Lyme Disease surveillance through social media analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claims match verbatim in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of NLP tools in disease monitoring and public health tracking varies across studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of NLP tools in disease monitoring and public health tracking varies across studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same conclusion appears in both documents with matching wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some NLP tools showed high accuracy in pneumonia detection and high sensitivity in identifying invasive mold diseases from medical reports. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some NLP tools showed high accuracy in pneumonia detection and high sensitivity in identifying invasive mold diseases from medical reports. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result claim is effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review highlights the untapped potential of NLP and LLMs in infectious disease management and calls for further exploration. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review calls for more exploration to fully harness AI's capabilities in infectious disease management. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication rephrases the call to action and lowers stated certainty (high -> medium), clarifying emphasis while reducing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine learning approach was developed to identify clinical indicators from blood tests of COVID-19 patients that predict poor prognosis and morbidity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning approach was developed to identify clinical indicators from blood tests of COVID-19 patients that predict poor prognosis and morbidity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Contribution is identical in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Arterial Blood Gas (ABG) O2 Saturation and C-Reactive Protein (CRP) are identified as the most important clinical biomarkers for determining poor prognosis in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Arterial Blood Gas (ABG) O2 Saturation and C-Reactive Protein (CRP) are identified as the most important clinical biomarkers for determining poor prognosis in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result and certainty are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model can contribute to building quantitative and optimized clinical management systems for COVID-19 and similar diseases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model can contribute to building quantitative and optimized clinical management systems for COVID-19 and similar diseases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion remains the same in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first successful implementation of homomorphically encrypted federated learning (HEFL) in cancer image analysis has been demonstrated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The first successful implementation of somewhat-homomorphically-encrypted federated learning (SHEFL) in cancer image analysis has been demonstrated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication refines the encryption descriptor from 'homomorphically encrypted' to 'somewhat-homomorphically-encrypted (SHEFL)', clarifying the specific scheme while preserving the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HEFL enables the training of AI models that outperform locally trained models and perform on par with centrally trained models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SHEFL enables the training of AI models that outperform locally trained models and perform on par with centrally trained models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance claim is preserved; only the method name was slightly adjusted in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HEFL allows multiple institutions to co-train AI models without transmitting any decryptable data to untrusted servers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SHEFL allows multiple institutions to co-train AI models while maintaining data governance and without transmitting decryptable data to untrusted servers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds emphasis on data governance while keeping the same core privacy claim about not transmitting decryptable data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Federated learning with homomorphic encryption achieves state-of-the-art performance while ensuring privacy guarantees. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SHEFL enables the training of AI models that outperform locally trained models and perform on par with centrally trained models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Preprint combined claims about performance and privacy; the publication splits these aspects and presents performance as Q1 and governance/privacy as Q2, so the original combined claim was revised across publication statements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Aberrant gaze perception in schizophrenia (SZ) and bipolar disorder (BD) is driven by less efficient evidence accumulation and perceptual biases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Aberrant gaze perception in schizophrenia and bipolar disorder is driven by less efficient evidence accumulation and perceptual biases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result is unchanged in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In schizophrenia, greater caution in gaze perception is observed, which may involve sacrificing response speed to preserve accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In schizophrenia, greater caution in gaze perception is observed, which may involve sacrificing response speed to preserve accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion matches between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Poorer social functioning in schizophrenia is related to greater expectation biases. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Poorer social functioning in schizophrenia is related to greater expectation biases. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim content and certainty are consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Perceptual and expectancy biases in schizophrenia are associated with the severity of hallucinations and delusions, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Perceptual and expectancy biases in schizophrenia are associated with the severity of hallucinations and delusions, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Computational modeling can provide a nuanced understanding of the cognitive processes underlying social cognitive difficulties in individuals with schizophrenia and bipolar disorder. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Computational modeling can provide a nuanced understanding of the cognitive processes underlying social cognitive difficulties in schizophrenia and bipolar disorder. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication retains the same contribution but reports a higher certainty (medium -> high), amplifying confidence in the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Children with high myeloperoxidase or Campylobacter infection experienced a greater impact of the interventions on growth. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Children with high myeloperoxidase or Campylobacter infection experienced a greater impact of the interventions on anthropometry. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Core finding is preserved; the publication uses 'anthropometry' rather than 'growth' but meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A treatment rule that assigned the N+WSH and WSH interventions based on EED biomarkers and pathogens increased predicted child growth compared to the randomly allocated intervention. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A treatment rule that assigned the N+WSH and WSH interventions based on EED biomarkers and pathogens increased predicted child growth compared to the randomly allocated intervention. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EED biomarker and pathogen status, particularly Campylobacter and myeloperoxidase, may be related to the impact of N+WSH, WSH, and N interventions on child linear growth. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EED biomarkers and pathogen status may be related to the impact of N+WSH, WSH, and N interventions on child linear growth. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion matches with minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CNN achieved the highest accuracy in detecting synovitis in the middle finger proximal interphalangeal joint at 83%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CNN achieved the highest accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity in detecting synovitis in the MFPIP joint. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same primary result (CNN best for MFPIP synovitis) but adds sensitivity/specificity and omits the explicit 83% figure, so the claim is clarified rather than changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Computer vision was able to detect inflammation in three joints of the hand with reasonable accuracy on standardized photographs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Computer vision was able to detect inflammation in three joints of the hand with reasonable accuracy on standardized photographs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text is the same but the publication frames it as a result with higher certainty, so the content is unchanged while confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Feature engineering was not required for the CNN to work despite a diversity in clinical diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Feature engineering was not required for the CNN to work despite a diversity in clinical diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion verbatim with the same level of certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Larger datasets are likely to improve accuracy and help explain the basis of classification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Larger datasets are likely to improve accuracy and help explain the basis of classification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical recommendation about larger datasets appears in the publication with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These data suggest a potential use of computer vision in screening and follow-up of inflammatory arthritis. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These data suggest a potential use of computer vision in screening and follow-up of inflammatory arthritis. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this tentative translational conclusion unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OCT-MD estimates had a mean absolute error (MAE) of 1.62 dB. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning model developed in this study generated OCT-MD estimates with a mean absolute error (MAE) of 1.62 dB, which is better than any previously published models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The MAE value is preserved but the publication adds a comparative statement about prior work, clarifying and strengthening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The area under the curve (AUC) of MD slopes with partial OCT-MD substitution was significantly worse than the VF-MD slope. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The area under the curve (AUC) of MD slopes calculated using OCT-MD substitution was statistically inferior to that of VF-MD. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same statistical finding using equivalent wording (statistically inferior), so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Supplementing VF-MD with OCT-MD did not improve AUC, regardless of mean absolute error. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Supplementing VF-MD with OCT-MD did not improve the AUC for detecting longitudinal visual field progression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the application (detecting longitudinal VF progression) and drops the "regardless of MAE" clause, narrowing the claim's scope while clarifying its target.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ML models converting OCT data to VF-MD with error levels lower than 1.62 dB were inferior to VF-MD data for detecting trend-based VF progression. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: OCT-MD estimates need to achieve a mean absolute error (MAE) of less than or equal to 1.00 dB for their AUC to be statistically similar to that of VF-MD alone. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication revises the numeric threshold and frames the conclusion differently (MAE ≤1.00 dB for statistical similarity), moderating the original high-certainty claim about inferiority at errors <1.62 dB.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models converting OCT data to VF-MD must achieve better prediction errors (MAE ≤ 1 dB) to be clinically valuable at detecting VF worsening. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Future models converting OCT data to VF-MD must achieve better prediction errors to be clinically valuable at detecting visual field worsening. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the practical recommendation but removes the explicit MAE ≤1 dB target and expresses it with lower certainty, broadening and tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of promotional waterpipe-related tweets showed an overall decreasing trend during the study period. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The number of promotional waterpipe-related tweets showed an overall decreasing trend during the study period. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The posting of promotional waterpipe-related tweets was more active later in the day. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this temporal activity pattern unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates a significant social media activity in promoting waterpipe tobacco smoking. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates a significant social media activity in promoting waterpipe tobacco smoking. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same conclusion present in the publication with matching certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings underscore the urgent need to regulate the promotional content of WTS on social media. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings underscore the urgent need to regulate the promotional content of WTS on social media. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the regulatory recommendation with higher stated urgency/certainty, amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights the importance of promoting public health education messages on social media to counteract the promotion of WTS. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the importance of promoting public health education messages on social media to counteract the promotion of WTS. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Recommendation preserved verbatim with same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A combination of age, fever, and tachypnoea is the most parsimonious predictor of ICU admission for COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A combination of age, fever, and tachypnea is the most parsimonious predictor of ICU admission for patients with COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: COVID-19 patients aged 40 to 79 years are likely to be admitted to the ICU if they have tachypnoea and delay their visit to the ER after being seen in primary care. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients with COVID-19 aged 40 to 79 years are likely to be admitted to the ICU if they have tachypnea and delay their visit to the emergency department after being seen in primary care. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Overall, 6% of hospitalized COVID-19 patients required ICU admission. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Overall, 6.1% of hospitalized patients with COVID-19 required ICU admission. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most common symptoms upon admission for COVID-19 patients were cough, fever, and dyspnoea, but these were present in less than half of cases. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed multitask approach detects suspicious images and segments potential nodules, improving the workflow for thyroid nodule assessment. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An automated multitask approach improves performance in detecting and segmenting thyroid nodules in ultrasound images. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A multi-scale UNet (MSUNet) with anomaly detection achieved the highest F1 score of 0.829 and an image-wide Dice similarity coefficient of 0.782 on the hold-out test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model achieved an F1 score of 0.839 and a Dice similarity coefficient of 0.808 on the hold-out test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports updated (higher) performance metrics for the proposed model compared with the preprint numbers, so the numeric results were revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed architecture demonstrates generalizability and robustness to data variability when evaluated on two external validation datasets. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed architecture demonstrates generalizability and robustness to data variability when evaluated on external validation datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current deep learning architectures for segmentation are typically semi-automated and do not assess the ability to identify suspicious images. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The BERT model of first ADHD visits achieved F1=0.78, precision=0.84, and recall=0.72.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The BioClinicalBERT model trained on the first ADHD visits achieved F1 = 0.76, precision = 0.81, recall = 0.72, and AUC = 0.81.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same evaluation context (first ADHD visits) with very similar metrics and a more specific model name plus an added AUC, representing a clarification rather than a removal or contradiction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Following threshold selection, temporal validation on notes from other visits achieved F1=0.80, recall=0.92 and precision=0.7.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Temporal validation of the model achieved F1 = 0.77, precision = 0.68, and recall = 0.88.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports temporal validation on the same setting but with notably different metric values, indicating a revision of the reported results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deploying a machine learning algorithm on a large and variable set of clinical notes accurately captured pediatrician adherence to guidelines in treatment of children with ADHD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deploying pre-trained language models on a variable set of clinical notes accurately captured pediatrician adherence to guidelines in the treatment of children with ADHD.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion but uses a more specific description ('pre-trained language models' vs 'machine learning algorithm'), so the claim is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach can be used to measure quality-of-care at scale and improve clinical care for various chronic medical conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Validating this approach in other patient populations is needed to achieve equitable measurement of quality of care at scale.
alignments.rationale: The publication tempers the original broad claim by emphasizing the need for validation in other populations to ensure equitable, scalable measurement, narrowing and reducing confidence in the generality of the original claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Fairness analysis revealed low model performance in publicly insured patients (F1 = 0.53).
alignments.rationale: The fairness analysis result appears only in the publication and was not present in the preprint, so it is an added claim reporting subgroup performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieved 99.7% ± 0.1% accuracy on the training dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieves over 99% accuracy on the training dataset, 98.8% on the validation dataset, and 98.6% accuracy on the test dataset for distinguishing cancerous from noncancerous tissue.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same high training accuracy while adding validation and test results, clarifying the overall evaluation though the training number is consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieved 94.9% accuracy on the test dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieves over 99% accuracy on the training dataset, 98.8% on the validation dataset, and 98.6% accuracy on the test dataset for distinguishing cancerous from noncancerous tissue.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a substantially higher test accuracy (98.6%) than the preprint's 94.9%, indicating a revision of the reported test performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach demonstrates significant potential for improving intraoperative decision-making in brain cancer surgeries.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach demonstrates significant potential for improving intraoperative decision-making in brain cancer surgeries.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is stated identically in both versions with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dual-path architecture leverages both the global context capture capabilities of transformers and the local texture analysis strengths of GLCM + CNNs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dual-path architecture combines the global contextual feature extraction capabilities of transformers with the local texture analysis strengths of GLCM + CNNs.

alignments.pub_claim_text: An adversarial discriminator network is incorporated to mitigate patient-specific bias and force the model to learn generalizable cancer-indicative features.
alignments.rationale: The adversarial discriminator is a methodological detail introduced in the publication that was not present in the preprint, so it is an added contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI model developed can accurately diagnose influenza from pharyngeal images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI model developed can accurately diagnose influenza from pharyngeal images.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of the AI model was 0.90, indicating high diagnostic performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve for the AI model was 0.90, indicating high diagnostic performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sensitivity and specificity of the AI model were 76% and 88%, respectively, outperforming three physicians.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sensitivity and specificity of the AI model were 76% and 88%, respectively, outperforming 3 physicians.
alignments.rationale: The performance metrics and comparison to physicians are reported the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI model often focused on follicles on the posterior pharyngeal wall as indicated by importance heatmaps.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI model often focused on follicles on the posterior pharyngeal wall in its diagnostic process.
alignments.rationale: Both versions report the same interpretability finding about focus on posterior pharyngeal wall follicles.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Streamline count generally provided highest prediction accuracies in all prediction tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Streamline count generally provided the highest prediction accuracies in all prediction tasks.
alignments.rationale: The result is stated equivalently in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning did not improve on prediction accuracies from simpler linear models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning did not improve on prediction accuracies from simpler linear models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High correlations between gradient attribution coefficients from deep learning and model coefficients from linear models suggested the models ranked the importance of features in similar ways.
alignments.pub_claim_text: High correlations between gradient attribution coefficients from deep learning and model coefficients from linear models suggested the models ranked the importance of features in similar ways.
alignments.rationale: The interpretative conclusion about feature importance ranking is presented identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model complexity is unlikely to improve detection of associations between structural connectomes and complex phenotypes with the current sample size.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model complexity is unlikely to improve detection of associations between structural connectomes and complex phenotypes with the current sample size.
alignments.rationale: The caution about limited benefit from increased model complexity is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CAD pipeline achieves state-of-the-art performance of 95.2% accuracy for binary cardiomegaly classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The binary cardiomegaly classification model has 95.2% accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The reported cardiomegaly accuracy is the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inter-radiologist agreement on evaluating the report’s semantics and correctness is 0.62 for strict agreement and 0.85 for relaxed agreement.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inter-radiologist agreement on evaluating the generated report's semantics and correctness is 0.62 for strict agreement and 0.85 for relaxed agreement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the CAD system suggests it is on par with human radiologists.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of our CAD system suggests performance on par with human radiologists.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about parity with radiologists is retained with equivalent wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work found and corrected several incorrect or missing metadata annotations for the MIMIC-CXR dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We found and corrected several incorrect or missing metadata annotations for the MIMIC-CXR dataset.
alignments.rationale: The contribution regarding metadata corrections is reported the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The graph neural network based model achieves an 8% improvement in ROC-AUC for endocrinology and a 5% improvement for hematology in personalized procedure recommendations compared to prior medical recommender systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The graph neural network based model achieves an 8% improvement in ROC-AUC for endocrinology and a 5% improvement for hematology in personalized procedure recommendations over prior medical recommender systems. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The recommender algorithm provides more effective medical procedure recommendations for endocrinology referrals than manual clinical checklists. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The recommender algorithm provides more effective medical procedure recommendations for endocrinology referrals compared to manual clinical checklists. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Embedding graph neural network models into clinical care can improve digital specialty consultation systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Embedding graph neural network models into clinical care can improve digital specialty consultation systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study achieved high agreement among annotators with an average kappa of 95% and F1 score of 91.20%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: This study developed a corpus of annotated clinical notes covering a broad spectrum of SDoH classes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Claim is repeated verbatim in the publication with same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The entailment model detected many challenging wordings missed by other models, achieving an average F1 score of 76.04%. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Random Forest ML model achieved 95.52% sensitivity, 82.57% specificity, and 97% AUC, outperforming other ML algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The random forest ML model achieved 95.52% sensitivity, 82.57% specificity, and 97% AUC, outperforming other ML algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DenseNet169 demonstrated superior performance among DL models suitable for mobile integration, achieving 92.63% sensitivity, 92.22% specificity, and 92% AUC. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DenseNet169 demonstrated superior performance among DL models, achieving 92.63% sensitivity, 92.22% specificity, and 92% AUC. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DenseNet169 integration into a mobile POC system demonstrated exceptional accuracy (> 0.84) in testing microfluidics under challenging imaging settings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DenseNet169 integration into a mobile POC system demonstrated exceptional accuracy (>0.84) in testing microfluidics under challenging imaging settings. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study confirms the transformative potential of AI in healthcare, emphasizing its capacity to revolutionize precision medicine through accurate and accessible diagnostics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study confirms the transformative potential of AI in healthcare, emphasizing its capacity to revolutionize precision medicine through accurate and accessible diagnostics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of AI into healthcare systems holds promise for enhancing patient outcomes and streamlining healthcare delivery. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This broader concluding statement about healthcare systems is not explicitly restated in the publication; thus it appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Six percent of the SDY cohort was found to harbor a pathogenic or likely pathogenic genetic variant in an epilepsy, cardiomyopathy or arrhythmia gene. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Six percent of the SDY cohort was found to harbor a pathogenic or likely pathogenic genetic variant in an epilepsy, cardiomyopathy, or arrhythmia gene. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The genomes of SDY cases, but not controls, were enriched for rare, damaging variants in epilepsy, cardiomyopathy and arrhythmia-related genes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The genomes of SDY cases, but not controls, were enriched for rare, potentially damaging variants in epilepsy, cardiomyopathy, and arrhythmia-related genes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A greater number of rare epilepsy genetic variants correlated with younger age at death. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A greater number of rare epilepsy genetic variants correlated with younger age at death. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is an enrichment in epilepsy-related genes in the SDY cohort. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study observed an enrichment in epilepsy-related genes in the SDY cohort. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings emphasize the importance of considering epilepsy genes when evaluating SDY. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings emphasize the importance of considering epilepsy genes when evaluating SDY. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Recommendation and emphasis are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model achieves 96% classification accuracy in distinguishing short-lived from long-lived patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model achieves 96% classification accuracy in distinguishing short-lived from long-lived patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact match in reported accuracy and claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Pearson correlation between predicted and actual months-to-death values is as high as 0.937. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Pearson correlation between predicted and actual months-to-death values is as high as 0.937. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical numeric correlation reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: About 27.4% of patients may survive longer with an alternative medicine chosen by our deep learning model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: About 27.4% of patients may survive longer with an alternative medicine chosen by our deep learning model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The median survival time of all patients can increase by 3.9 months. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our interpretable neural network model reveals the most discriminating pathways in the decision-making process. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our interpretable neural network model reveals the most discriminating pathways in the decision-making process. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Contribution statement is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New knowledge can be captured, tracked and predicted using the evolution of unsupervised word embeddings and machine learning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Thromboembolic complications have emerged as a significant theme in more than 77,000 peer-reviewed publications and over 11,000 WHO vetted preprints on COVID-19.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Thromboembolic complications were detected as an emerging theme as early as August 2020.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning based prediction of emerging links in the networks reveals autoimmune diseases, multisystem inflammatory syndrome and neurological complications as a dominant research theme in COVID-19 publications starting March 2021.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neurological complications gained significance in June 2021.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the neurological-theme finding but revises the timing (June rather than March), so the claim is materially changed rather than unchanged or merely clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automatic segmentation of vestibular schwannomas from MRI could significantly improve clinical workflow and assist patient management.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automatic segmentation of vestibular schwannomas from MRI could significantly improve clinical workflow and assist patient management.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel artificial intelligence framework based on a 2.5D convolutional neural network achieves results equivalent to those of an independent human annotator.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel artificial intelligence framework based on a 2.5D convolutional neural network achieves results equivalent to those of an independent human annotator.
alignments.rationale: Experimental performance claim is reproduced identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first publicly-available annotated imaging dataset of vestibular schwannomas has been released, containing a labelled dataset of 484 MR images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The first publicly-available annotated imaging dataset of vestibular schwannomas has been released, containing a labelled dataset of 484 MR images.
alignments.rationale: The dataset release claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated segmentation algorithm implementation uses MONAI, a freely-available open-source framework for deep learning in healthcare imaging.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated segmentation algorithm implementation uses MONAI, a freely-available open-source framework for deep learning in healthcare imaging.
alignments.rationale: The implementation claim is the same but the publication increases certainty (medium->high), indicating amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The released data will facilitate the development and validation of automated segmentation frameworks for vestibular schwannomas.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The released data will facilitate the development and validation of automated segmentation frameworks for vestibular schwannomas.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the data facilitating future development is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep-learning convolutional neural networks accurately classify COVID-19 lung infections on portable chest x-ray from normal and related lung infections.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep-learning convolutional neural networks accurately classify COVID-19 lung infections on portable chest X-ray from normal and related lung infections.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall sensitivity, specificity, accuracy, and AUC for whole pCXR were 0.79, 0.93, 0.79, and 0.85 respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall sensitivity, specificity, accuracy, and AUC for whole pCXR were 0.79, 0.93, 0.79, and 0.85 respectively.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the CNN was generally better using segmented lungs compared to whole pCXR.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the CNN was generally better using segmented lungs, with sensitivity, specificity, accuracy, and AUC of 0.91, 0.93, 0.88, and 0.89.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds specific performance numbers for segmented lungs, clarifying the preprint's qualitative comparison.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Heatmaps showed that CNN accurately localized areas of hazy appearance, ground glass opacity and/or consolidation on the pCXR.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Heatmaps showed that CNN accurately localized areas of hazy appearance, ground glass opacity and/or consolidation on the pCXR.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach has the potential to help radiologists and frontline physicians by providing more timely and accurate diagnosis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hybrid AI model achieved a diagnostic accuracy of 91.11% in identifying early signs of Parkinson's disease through voice analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hybrid model combining CNN, RNN, MKL, and MLP achieved 91.11% accuracy in detecting early signs of Parkinson's disease using voice analysis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrated a robust ability to distinguish between healthy controls and Parkinson's disease patients using vocal biomarkers.
