alignments.rationale: The publication quantifies diagnostic performance (recall/precision/F1), clarifying the preprint's general statement about robustness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The scoring system developed in this study validated that vocal biomarkers accurately correspond with their assigned scores for Parkinson's disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A probability-based scoring system was developed to help PD patients and clinicians track disease progression.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes a developed scoring tool for tracking progression rather than explicitly stating validation of biomarker-score correspondence, shifting the claim focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) provided insights into the model's decision-making process, identifying influential features for Parkinson's disease diagnosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) identified key features driving the Parkinson's disease diagnosis, enhancing AI interpretability and trustability.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Overall averaged positive, negative, and neutral sentiments toward COVID-19 vaccines were 58%, 22%, and 17% in the United Kingdom, compared to 56%, 24%, and 18% in the United States.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Public optimism over vaccine development, effectiveness and trials, as well as concerns over safety, economic viability and corporation control, were identified in the analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Public optimism over vaccine development, effectiveness, and trials, as well as concerns over their safety, economic viability, and corporation control, were identified.
alignments.rationale: The thematic findings about optimism and concerns are reproduced with equivalent content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI-enabled social-media analysis should be considered for adoption by institutions and governments to assess public attitude towards COVID-19 vaccinations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Artificial intelligence-enabled social media analysis should be considered for adoption by institutions and governments alongside surveys and other conventional methods of assessing public attitude.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed models outperform other models that utilize either unstructured notes or structured data only. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed models outperform other models that utilize either unstructured notes or structured data only. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed fusion models learn better patient representation by combining structured and unstructured data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating heterogeneous data types across EHRs helps improve the performance of prediction models and reduce errors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural network generated intravoxel incoherent motion (IVIM) parameter maps are visually more detailed, less noisy and have a higher test-retest repeatability than conventional least-squares (LSQ) generated images. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tri-exponential model exhibited higher adjusted R 2 values than the bi-exponential model in 33 out of 36 patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In 33 out of 36 patients, the tri-exponential model exhibited higher adjusted R 2 values than the bi-exponential model. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The bi- and tri-exponential neural network approaches outperformed both LSQ models for the majority of IVIM parameters in correlating IVIM data to liver histology. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The bi- and tri-exponential neural networks outperformed both LSQ models for the majority of IVIM parameters in correlating IVIM data to liver histology. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The IVIM 3 -NET can be used to improve image quality compared to a tri-exponential LSQ fit and provides promising correlations with histopathology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The unsupervised physics-informed deep neural network (IVIM<sub>3</sub>-NET) improved parameter map quality for tri-exponential IVIM. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Duration of breastfeeding, household wealth index, and the level of maternal education are the most important predictors of under-five mortality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Duration of breast feeding, number of antenatal visits, household wealth index, postnatal care, and the level of maternal education are significant predictors of under-five mortality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the set of significant predictors beyond the three the preprint highlighted, broadening the result while keeping high certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning techniques are superior to logistic regression for the classification of child survival. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning techniques are superior to logistic regression for the classification of child survival. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This performance comparison is repeated verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings provide an understanding of interventions that need to be prioritized to reduce levels of under-five mortality in LMICs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cross-sectional design of the study means we can only infer association and not causality. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The explicit limitation about cross-sectional design and causality present in the preprint is not reported among the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed model, MedAlbert+LRC, achieved an AUROC of 0.924 and a sensitivity of 86.6% for lung cancer diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed model achieved an AUROC of 0.924 and a sensitivity of 86.6% based on three years of data prior to diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the temporal window (three years) to the same reported performance metrics, clarifying the evaluation context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The comparator regression model achieved a lower AUROC of 0.887 and a PPV of 3.1%, indicating that the transformer-based model outperforms traditional methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model's positive predictive value (PPV) was 3.6%, which is higher than the comparator regression model's PPV of 3.1%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes PPV differences (3.6% vs 3.1%) and omits explicit AUROC comparison in this claim; it refines performance framing but preserves the conclusion that the transformer-based model outperforms the comparator.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates that capturing temporal sequencing in electronic health records significantly enhances the accuracy of predictive models for lung cancer diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Capturing temporal sequencing between cancer and non-cancer pathways to diagnosis enables much more accurate models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the sequencing claim to cancer vs non-cancer pathways and expresses it with slightly lower certainty (medium), tempering the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction model developed could be applied to the UK primary care population for early diagnosis of lung cancer. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include the stated applicability to the UK primary care population, so this contribution appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework designed for modelling patient pathways could be utilized for predicting other cancers or diseases. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not assert broader applicability of the framework to other cancers or diseases in the provided claims, indicating removal of this contribution claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: qXR v4 demonstrated a sensitivity of 0.91 and a specificity of 0.32 for diagnosing pulmonary TB in the triage cohort using culture as the reference standard. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: qXR v4 demonstrated a sensitivity of 0.91 and a specificity of 0.32 for diagnosing pulmonary TB in the triage cohort using culture as the reference standard. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this numeric diagnostic performance result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There was no difference in the area under the receiver-operating-characteristic curve between qXR v3 and qXR v4 with either a culture or Xpert reference standard. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There was no difference in the area under the receiver-operating-characteristic curve between qXR v3 and qXR v4 with either a culture or Xpert reference standard. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This comparative AUC finding is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Screening patients without cough or TB risk factors resulted in a low diagnostic yield. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Screening patients without cough or TB risk factors in this setting resulted in a low diagnostic yield. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion but reports it with lower stated certainty (medium), thus tempering confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A high prevalence of radiographic lung abnormalities was detected by qXR in the triage cohort, including opacities, consolidation, and nodules. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include this specific prevalence-of-abnormalities result, indicating it was not carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings support the need for population and setting-specific thresholds for CAD programs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings support the need for population and setting-specific thresholds for CAD programs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This policy-relevant conclusion is preserved unchanged between preprint and publication.
